message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[19]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_19.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 19: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

240	class SpecProviderTransportError(RuntimeError):
241	    """A10 (iarch006 epic-failure): a TRANSPORT/PARSE fault in the quantified-spec Writer call.
242	
243	    Raised by the ``_q_spec_provider`` closure when the Writer call returns an EMPTY body / no
244	    parseable JSON, OR the JSON fails to decode — i.e. the model never produced a usable spec
245	    because of a transport or parse failure. This is STRICTLY DISTINCT from an explicit model
246	    DECLINE ({"model_id":"none"}), which keeps the legacy ``return None``.
247	
248	    The distinction is the LAW-II receipt: ``run_quantified_section`` collapses BOTH a bare
249	    ``None`` decline and a transport miss into the same ``declined_no_spec`` status, mislabeling a
250	    real failure as a benign "no spec to model". Raising this typed error makes the call land in
251	    the EXISTING ``_call_spec_provider_with_retry`` bounded-retry + ``spec_provider_error`` /
252	    ``QUANTIFIED_STATUS_PARSE_ERROR`` lane (a subclass of ``Exception``), so a real failure is
253	    surfaced LOUD instead of disclosed as a legitimate Writer decline. It touches NO faithfulness
254	    gate or threshold — it is pure fail-loud observability over WHY the quantified section was empty.
255	    """
256	
257	
258	def parse_quantified_spec_response(text: str, *, sourced_count: int = 0) -> "dict | None":
259	    """I-wire-003 B4 (#1317): PURE parse + classify of the quantified-spec Writer's raw text
260	    response, extracted from the (unimportable) ``_q_spec_provider`` closure so the
261	    transport-vs-decline split is directly unit-testable.
262	
263	    Recovers a single JSON object from ``text`` (the model's content, or its reasoning stream
264	    when content was empty — the GLM-5.2 / deepseek reasoning-first misroute) and classifies it:
265	
266	      * no parseable ``{...}`` object .......... RAISE SpecProviderTransportError (empty/no-JSON
267	        body or unparseable — a TRANSPORT/PARSE fault, not a benign decline).
268	      * parses but is not a dict ............... RAISE SpecProviderTransportError.
269	      * dict MISSING the ``model_id`` key ...... RAISE SpecProviderTransportError (a
270	        truncated/partial spec — the closing brace arrived but the required key was never
271	        written; classifying it as a decline would masquerade breakage as honest-empty).
272	      * dict with ``model_id`` in {None,'','none'} -> return None (the EXPLICIT Writer decline:
273	        "the numbers do not support a defensible model" -> declined_no_spec). NOT a fault.
274	      * otherwise .............................. return the spec dict.
275	
276	    Faithfulness-neutral: a ModelSpec is structured DATA validated downstream by
277	    build_quantified_spec (exact datapoint_ref + pure-arithmetic formulas) — never verified
278	    prose. This helper touches no gate/threshold; it is pure observability over WHY the
279	    quantified section did or did not land. ``sourced_count`` only enriches the error message."""
280	    import json as _pj
281	    import re as _pre
282	
283	    match = _pre.search(r"\{.*\}", text or "", _pre.DOTALL)
284	    if not match:
285	        raise SpecProviderTransportError(
286	            "quantified spec_provider returned empty/no-JSON body "
287	            f"(content+reasoning empty or unparseable; {sourced_count} sourced "
288	            "numbers were available)"
289	        )
290	    try:
291	        obj = _pj.loads(match.group(0))
292	    except _pj.JSONDecodeError as jde:
293	        raise SpecProviderTransportError(
294	            f"quantified spec_provider emitted non-JSON: {str(jde)[:160]}"
295	        ) from jde
296	    if not isinstance(obj, dict):
297	        raise SpecProviderTransportError(
298	            "quantified spec_provider emitted JSON that is not a spec object "
299	            f"(got {type(obj).__name__})"
300	        )
301	    if "model_id" not in obj:
302	        raise SpecProviderTransportError(
303	            "quantified spec_provider emitted a JSON object with NO 'model_id' "
304	            "key (truncated/partial spec — not an explicit Writer decline)"
305	        )
306	    if obj.get("model_id") in (None, "", "none"):
307	        # EXPLICIT Writer decline -> benign None return -> declined_no_spec.
308	        return None
309	    return obj
310	
311	
312	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
313	# BUG-B-101 fix: unified manifest.status taxonomy.
314	#
315	# Pre-fix, successful runs wrote manifest.json WITHOUT a "status" key
316	# while abort runs DID include it. Documentation claimed
317	# manifest.status was authoritative — it wasn't. See
318	# outputs/codex_findings/deep_dive_round_1/findings.md for the full
319	# scoping analysis.
320	#
321	# Post-fix, EVERY exit path writes a manifest.json with a "status"
322	# field from this unified taxonomy. `summary["status"]` is preserved
323	# as secondary sweep telemetry for backward compatibility with any
324	# downstream counter that relied on the legacy labels.
325	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
326	
327	UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
328	    # success
329	    "success",
330	    # released-with-disclosure (I-perm-001 #1195 always-release): the report SHIPPED with honest
331	    # disclosed gaps (BLOCK->LABEL) — a RELEASED terminal, not an abort. `released_insufficient_
332	    # safety_evidence` is the honest clinical-safety-floor variant (normal render blocked, honest
333	    # report still ships). The "released_" prefix is a documented exception to the 4-prefix scheme
334	    # (alongside "cancelled") in tests/.../test_manifest_contract_status_prefixes.
335	    "released_with_disclosed_gaps",
336	    "released_insufficient_safety_evidence",
337	    # partial — report produced but degraded signal
338	    "partial_thin_corpus",
339	    "partial_incomplete_corpus",
340	    "partial_rule_check_warnings",
341	    "partial_outline_fallback",      # BUG-M-203: planner failed, fallback used
342	    "partial_evaluator_advisory",    # BUG-M-205: judge flagged critical axes
343	    "partial_qwen_advisory",         # I-modref-004 (#530): legacy alias, historical manifests
344	    "partial_saturation",            # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988): pruned report, some sections under-covered
345	    # abort — pipeline refused to produce a report
346	    "abort_scope_rejected",
347	    "abort_no_sources",
348	    "abort_corpus_inadequate",

452	def _depth_d8_true_drop(
453	    *,
454	    report_path,
455	    final_verdicts: dict,
456	    audit_map: dict,
457	    manifest: dict,
458	    research_question: str,
459	    log,
460	) -> "tuple[dict, dict]":
461	    """I-deepfix-001 wave-3 (conclusion) CHANGE 4 — TRUE drop-not-sink for the grounded DEPTH
462	    cross-source findings.
463	
464	    The depth findings entered the 4-role gate as S3/observe-only ``DS-*`` claims
465	    (native_gate_b_inputs). A NON-VERIFIED depth finding must be DROPPED from report.md (refuse-in-
466	    place to the visible gap) — NOT kept+labeled — REGARDLESS of the always-release annotate path the
467	    SECTION claims take. A rendered depth finding that was never JUDGED (its evidence failed to
468	    resolve, so no DS-* claim was built => absent from ``final_verdicts``) is fail-closed dropped too.
469	    VERIFIED depth findings SURVIVE verbatim (reconcile skips VERIFIED). The two-tier label on
470	    survivors is untouched (this only DROPS, never relabels — cross/single stays honest).
471	
472	    Runs the depth reconcile FIRST (writes report.md), then returns the SECTION-ONLY
473	    ``(final_verdicts, non_verified)`` with EVERY is_synthesized cid removed, so the caller's existing
474	    annotate/reconcile then runs ONLY over the section claims, over the depth-reconciled text.
475	    Faithfulness-STRENGTHENING: depth can only LOSE a finding here, never gain one; §-1.3 is honored
476	    (dropping a synthesized DIGEST sentence removes no basket, no bibliography row, no body citation —
477	    the underlying sources still weight + consolidate in the body); the section path + release
478	    decision are byte-unchanged.
479	    """
480	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.report_redactor import (  # noqa: PLC0415
481	        ReportRedactionError,
482	        reconcile_report_against_verdicts,
483	    )
484	
485	    def _is_synth(cid: str) -> bool:
486	        return bool((audit_map.get(cid) or {}).get("is_synthesized"))
487	
488	    # DROP set: judged non-VERIFIED depth claims + any rendered-but-unjudged is_synthesized claim
489	    # (present in audit_map, absent from final_verdicts) as a fail-closed UNSUPPORTED drop.
490	    depth_drop = {
491	        cid: v for cid, v in final_verdicts.items() if _is_synth(cid) and v != "VERIFIED"
492	    }
493	    for cid, meta in audit_map.items():
494	        if bool((meta or {}).get("is_synthesized")) and cid not in final_verdicts:
495	            depth_drop[cid] = "UNSUPPORTED"
496	
497	    # SECTION-only verdicts (strip EVERY is_synthesized cid) for the caller's existing path.
498	    section_verdicts = {cid: v for cid, v in final_verdicts.items() if not _is_synth(cid)}
499	    section_nonverified = {cid: v for cid, v in section_verdicts.items() if v != "VERIFIED"}
500	    if not depth_drop:
501	        return section_verdicts, section_nonverified
502	
503	    try:
504	        _dd = reconcile_report_against_verdicts(
505	            report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), depth_drop, audit_map
506	        )
507	    except ReportRedactionError as _dd_exc:
508	        # Essentially unreachable (a depth finding renders as a redactable "- ..." body line; the
509	        # report_redactor TIER-4 section-fallback bounds it). Defensive fail-closed: WITHHOLD the
510	        # findings body rather than ship an un-dropped depth leak (the established B16 pattern).
511	        # Preserve the unredacted body for the curator. No depth OR section claim ships. Return EMPTY
512	        # section verdicts so the caller's annotate/reconcile is a harmless no-op over the degraded body.
513	        try:
514	            (report_path.parent / "report_unredacted.md").write_text(
515	                report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), encoding="utf-8"
516	            )
517	        except OSError:
518	            pass
519	        _summary = "released_with_disclosed_gaps"
520	        manifest["status"] = to_unified_status(_summary)
521	        manifest["release_allowed"] = True
522	        manifest["report_redaction_error"] = str(_dd_exc)
523	        manifest.setdefault("disclosed_gaps", []).append(
524	            "depth_synthesis_redaction_unpinnable: a non-VERIFIED cross-source depth finding could "
525	            f"not be bounded for surgical removal ({_dd_exc}); the findings body was withheld and "
526	            "replaced by this disclosure (unredacted body kept as report_unredacted.md). No "
527	            "unverified claim ships."
528	        )
529	        report_path.write_text(
530	            build_finalizer_artifact_body(
531	                research_question=research_question,
532	                status=_summary,
533	                error=(
534	                    "post-gate depth-synthesis redaction could not surgically quarantine a non-"
535	                    f"VERIFIED cross-source finding ({_dd_exc}); the findings body was withheld."
536	                ),
537	            ),
538	            encoding="utf-8",
539	        )
540	        log(f"[depth-drop]   FAIL-CLOSED (unpinnable depth finding): {_dd_exc} -> withheld body")
541	        return {}, {}
542	
543	    report_path.write_text(_dd.report_text, encoding="utf-8")
544	    if _dd.redacted:
545	        # Polish only (never a faithfulness step): tidy a now-empty depth subhead so it does not
546	        # render bare. No-op when nothing to tidy.
547	        try:
548	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.key_findings import (  # noqa: PLC0415
549	                refilter_key_findings_block,
550	            )
551	            _tidied = refilter_key_findings_block(_dd.report_text)
552	            if _tidied != _dd.report_text:
553	                report_path.write_text(_tidied, encoding="utf-8")
554	        except Exception as _tidy_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — polish only; never break the run
555	            log(f"[depth-drop]   subhead tidy skipped (fail-open): {_tidy_exc}")
556	    log(
557	        "[depth-drop]   depth D8 true-drop: dropped="
558	        f"{_dd.redacted_count} already_absent={len(_dd.already_absent)} "
559	        f"depth_claims={len(depth_drop)} (section claims -> annotate/reconcile)"
560	    )
561	    return section_verdicts, section_nonverified
562	
563	
564	def _depth_true_drop_when_all_verified(
565	    *,
566	    report_path,
567	    audit_map_path,
568	    final_verdicts: dict,
569	    manifest: dict,
570	    research_question: str,
571	    log,
572	) -> "tuple[dict, dict, bool]":
573	    """I-deepfix-001 wave-3 (conclusion) CHANGE 4 — P1 CALLER fix.
574	
575	    The section reconcile chain in ``run_one_query`` short-circuits on ``if not
576	    _nonverified_verdicts`` (every JUDGED 4-role verdict is VERIFIED). Both existing
577	    ``_depth_d8_true_drop`` call sites live in the LATER ``elif`` branches, so that short-circuit
578	    would SKIP the depth reconcile entirely — and a rendered synthesized ``DS-*`` bullet whose claim
579	    was NEVER judged (an ``is_synthesized`` row present in the audit_map yet ABSENT from
580	    ``final_verdicts`` — its evidence failed to resolve so no D8 claim was built) would then SHIP
581	    un-adjudicated. Fail-closed: a synthesized cross-source claim that D8 never adjudicated cannot
582	    ship. This runs the depth true-drop EVEN when there are zero non-VERIFIED verdicts, so the depth
583	    reconcile is NOT gated on ``_nonverified_verdicts`` being non-empty (the Codex P1).
584	
585	    Returns ``(final_verdicts, nonverified_verdicts, ran)``. ``ran`` is True iff the depth gate is ON,
586	    both artifacts exist on disk, AND at least one droppable synthesized row is present
587	    (``is_synthesized`` AND either unjudged or judged non-VERIFIED) — in which case
588	    ``_depth_d8_true_drop`` ran and rewrote ``report_path``. When ``ran`` is False this is a
589	    byte-identical no-op and the caller keeps the legacy skip log: gate OFF, a missing artifact, or
590	    no synthesized row to drop (every synthesized claim judged VERIFIED == the legacy all-verified
591	    no-op). STRENGTHENS faithfulness (it can only DROP an un-adjudicated bullet to the visible gap;
592	    it removes no basket, no bibliography row, no body citation, and touches no section claim);
593	    relaxes nothing. The ``>=2`` distinct-origin floor lives upstream and is untouched.
594	    """
595	    section_nonverified = {cid: v for cid, v in final_verdicts.items() if v != "VERIFIED"}
596	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.depth_synthesis import (  # noqa: PLC0415
597	        depth_synthesis_d8_gate_enabled as _depth_d8_gate,
598	    )
599	    if not (_depth_d8_gate() and report_path.is_file() and audit_map_path.is_file()):
600	        return final_verdicts, section_nonverified, False
601	    audit_map = json.loads(audit_map_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
602	    droppable = any(
603	        bool((meta or {}).get("is_synthesized"))
604	        and (cid not in final_verdicts or final_verdicts.get(cid) != "VERIFIED")
605	        for cid, meta in audit_map.items()
606	    )
607	    if not droppable:
608	        return final_verdicts, section_nonverified, False
609	    new_final, new_nonverified = _depth_d8_true_drop(
610	        report_path=report_path,
611	        final_verdicts=final_verdicts,
612	        audit_map=audit_map,
613	        manifest=manifest,
614	        research_question=research_question,
615	        log=log,
616	    )
617	    return new_final, new_nonverified, True

629	    stub instead of vanishing from ``verification_details.json`` (exactly drb_90's
630	    "Comparative Assessment", where a comparative claim spans multiple sources so no single span
631	    survived per-sentence strict_verify). ``dropped`` is present-but-empty so the downstream

647	def reconstruct_release_outcome_from_manifest(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
648	    """A18 (iarch007): rebuild the proof-bearing ReleaseOutcome from a FINAL manifest for the
649	    release-invariant assertion. PURE + unit-callable.
650	
651	    ``adjudicated`` is read HONESTLY from a serialized ``release_disclosure["adjudicated"]`` when
652	    that key is present (a judge-seam records False; the genuine-D8 path records True). When the key
653	    is ABSENT (the always-release block did not run — e.g. a legacy path that produced a
654	    release-asserting status without serializing release_disclosure), it is derived from the REAL
655	    D8 evidence — a non-empty ``four_role_evaluation.final_verdicts`` map, the SAME signal the
656	    artifact-level SWEEP checker uses — never a blind default. So a release whose judge genuinely
657	    ran is NEVER false-HELD, while a truly un-judged release (no disclosure AND empty final_verdicts)
658	    still FAILS CLOSED."""
659	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.release_policy import (  # noqa: PLC0415
660	        ReleaseOutcome as _RO,
661	    )
662	    rd = manifest.get("release_disclosure")
663	    rd = rd if isinstance(rd, dict) else {}
664	    fr = manifest.get("four_role_evaluation")
665	    d8_ran = bool((fr or {}).get("final_verdicts")) if isinstance(fr, dict) else False
666	    return _RO(
667	        released=bool(manifest.get("release_allowed")),
668	        hard_block=bool(rd.get("hard_block", False)),
669	        normal_release_blocked=bool(rd.get("normal_release_blocked", False)),
670	        status=str(manifest.get("status") or ""),
671	        disclosed_gaps=list(rd.get("disclosed_gaps", []) or []),
672	        hard_block_reasons=list(rd.get("hard_block_reasons", []) or []),
673	        release_quality_score=float(rd.get("release_quality_score", 0.0) or 0.0),
674	        safety_floor=str(rd.get("safety_floor", "ok") or "ok"),
675	        adjudicated=(bool(rd["adjudicated"]) if "adjudicated" in rd else d8_ran),
676	        body_withheld=bool(rd.get("body_withheld", False)),
677	        compensating_screen_passed=bool(rd.get("compensating_screen_passed", False)),
678	    )
679

681	# I-arch-004 F20 (#1255): historical 4-role seam WALL floor (I-run11-004). The generator-sized
682	# default can only grow above this, never regress below.
683	_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_FLOOR = 7200.0

686	def _resolve_four_role_seam_timeout() -> float:
687	    """Wall timeout for the WHOLE 4-role D8 seam (brackets MANY sequential per-claim reasoning-ON
688	    verifier calls — MiniMax-M2 Sentinel + xhigh GLM-5.1 Mirror / Qwen Judge).
689	
690	    The historical default was a flat 7200 hardcode with NO relation to how generous a single
691	    reasoning call is allowed to be (F20). Size it off the generator budget instead:
692	      * env PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS set -> that value (LAW VI override, wins outright),
693	      * else -> max(7200 floor, PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_GEN_MULTIPLE x LIVE generator timeout) so the
694	        default can only GROW (honors the Gate-B slate's set_generator_timeout_seconds), never
695	        regress below the historical 7200. The multiple encodes "the seam needs room for many
696	        claims, each up to ~a generator-budget's worth of reasoning."
697	    The per-call budget (PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) and the $ hard cap are separate backstops.
698	    """
699	    explicit = os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS")
700	    if explicit is not None:
701	        return float(explicit)
702	    try:
703	        multiple = float(os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_GEN_MULTIPLE", "4"))
704	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
705	        multiple = 4.0
706	    if not (multiple > 0):
707	        multiple = 4.0
708	    return max(
709	        _FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_FLOOR,
710	        multiple * float(get_generator_timeout_seconds()),
711	    )

714	def _route_seam_worker_exception(exc: BaseException) -> str:
715	    """I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.3 (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1): classify an exception raised by the
716	    4-role D8 seam worker into the seam's HELD-reason string OR re-raise it for a dedicated outer
717	    handler. PURE + behavioral so the routing is unit-testable on real exception objects (NOT a
718	    mirrored predicate) — exactly the `_credibility_abort_status` precedent (#008b P1-1). The seam
719	    block calls this in a single `except BaseException` so the production routing IS the test subject.
720	
721	    Routing (order is load-bearing — most-specific first):
722	
723	      * ``BudgetExceededError`` (PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN breach) -> RE-RAISE: must reach the existing outer
724	        ``abort_budget_exceeded`` handler (the clean budget-abort contract), NEVER be swallowed into a
725	        held ``seam_error``.
726	      * ``RoleTransportExhaustedError`` (force-closed role with PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE OFF) ->
727	        RE-RAISE: the seam already wrote the DISCLOSED ``state/halt_*_role_transport_exhausted.md``
728	        artifact and the typed error carries ``status=abort_role_transport_exhausted``; it MUST
729	        propagate to the dedicated outer handler that maps it to the DISCLOSED hard-halt
730	        manifest.status — NEVER the generic ``released_with_disclosed_gaps`` the held path produces.
731	      * ``concurrent.futures.TimeoutError`` (the seam WALL fired) -> ``"seam_timeout"`` (HELD).
732	      * any OTHER seam failure -> ``"seam_error:<Type>:<msg[:120]>"`` (HELD, fail-closed).
733	
734	    The block-local imports (the seam block imports these at its top) are re-imported HERE so the helper
735	    is self-contained and unit-callable — mirrors the in-file local-import idiom. Faithfulness-neutral:
736	    pure routing, touches NO gate; a re-raise only changes WHICH disclosed abort label the run carries.
737	    """
738	    import concurrent.futures as _seam_futures  # noqa: PLC0415
739	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
740	        BudgetExceededError as _SeamBudgetExceededError,
741	    )
742	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.sweep_integration import (  # noqa: PLC0415
743	        RoleTransportExhaustedError,
744	    )
745	    if isinstance(exc, _SeamBudgetExceededError):
746	        raise exc
747	    if isinstance(exc, RoleTransportExhaustedError):
748	        raise exc
749	    if isinstance(exc, _seam_futures.TimeoutError):
750	        return "seam_timeout"
751	    return f"seam_error:{type(exc).__name__}:{str(exc)[:120]}"
752

754	def _role_transport_abort_manifest(
755	    exc: BaseException,
756	    *,
757	    run_id: str,
758	    q: dict,
759	    retrieval=None,
760	    run_cost: float = 0.0,
761	) -> dict:
762	    """I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.3 (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1): build the DISCLOSED hard-halt
763	    manifest dict for a `RoleTransportExhaustedError` that reached the run-driver's outer handler with
764	    PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE OFF. PURE (no I/O) so the RESULTING manifest.status — the VALUE, not a
765	    structural proxy — is unit-callable: the real outer `except` clause builds the manifest VIA this
766	    helper, so deleting the wiring flips the helper's behavioral test RED (the `_credibility_abort_status`
767	    / `_route_seam_worker_exception` extract-then-execute precedent).
768	
769	    `status` is the typed exception's `.status` routed through `to_unified_status` (so it is the
770	    DISCLOSED `abort_role_transport_exhausted` terminal, registered in UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES /
771	    PipelineStatus / KNOWN_STATUS_VALUES — NOT the generic released_with_disclosed_gaps the seam-held
772	    path produces, NOT the error_unexpected default). The disclosed halt-artifact path (when present) is
773	    surfaced as `role_transport_halt_artifact`. Faithfulness-neutral: pure status/envelope construction.
774	    """
775	    manifest = _base_manifest_envelope(
776	        run_id=run_id, q=q, retrieval=retrieval, run_cost=run_cost,
777	    )
778	    manifest["status"] = to_unified_status(getattr(exc, "status", "") or "")
779	    manifest["error"] = str(exc)[:500]
780	    halt_artifact = getattr(exc, "halt_artifact", None)
781	    if halt_artifact is not None:
782	        manifest["role_transport_halt_artifact"] = str(halt_artifact)
783	    return manifest

786	def _credibility_abort_status(exc: BaseException) -> str | None:
787	    """I-cred-008b (#1162) — Codex #008b P1-1: classify an exception that reached run_one_query's handler.
788	
789	    Returns ``"abort_credibility_coverage_gap"`` for a credibility-disclosure COVERAGE-GAP
790	    ``CredibilityPassError`` (a cited token with no credibility/origin coverage); returns ``None`` for
791	    EVERYTHING ELSE — including a NON-coverage ``CredibilityPassError`` (judge_error / independence gap
792	    raised by the pass itself), which the caller then routes to ``error_unexpected``. Pure + behavioral so
793	    the routing is unit-testable on real exception objects (not a mirrored predicate), which is exactly
794	    what the original sibling-``except`` re-raise missed.
795	    """
796	    # Codex #008b iter-2 P2: classify by message PREFIX (the coverage gap is always raised as
797	    # CredibilityPassError("abort_credibility_coverage_gap: ...")) — a prefix match is less brittle than a
798	    # substring scan for a manifest-status classifier.
799	    if isinstance(exc, _CredPassErrForAbort) and str(exc).startswith("abort_credibility_coverage_gap"):
800	        return "abort_credibility_coverage_gap"
801	    return None

804	def _build_claim_disclosure_doc(multi: Any, quantified_telemetry: Any) -> dict | None:
805	    """I-cred-008b (#1162): serialize the per-claim CREDIBILITY DISCLOSURE for claim_disclosure.json.
806	
807	    Returns ``None`` (=> no artifact, byte-identical) when ``multi.credibility_analysis`` is absent
808	    (master flag off). Otherwise returns ``{"sections": [{"title", "claims": [...]}, ...]}`` where each
809	    claim row carries the six advisory disclosure fields. Sites 1-3 surface via
810	    ``SectionResult.kept_sentences_pre_resolve``; the quantified path (site 4) has no SectionResult, so
811	    its rows arrive in ``quantified_telemetry["claim_disclosure"]``. Pure (no IO) for testability.
812	    """
813	    if getattr(multi, "credibility_analysis", None) is None:
814	        return None
815	    sections: list[dict] = []
816	    for sr in getattr(multi, "sections", None) or []:
817	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
818	            continue
819	        kept = getattr(sr, "kept_sentences_pre_resolve", None) or []
820	        if not kept:
821	            continue
822	        sections.append({
823	            "title": getattr(sr, "title", ""),
824	            "claims": [
825	                {
826	                    "sentence": getattr(sv, "sentence", ""),
827	                    "span_verdict": getattr(sv, "span_verdict", ""),
828	                    "credibility_weight": getattr(sv, "credibility_weight", None),
829	                    "independent_origin_count": getattr(sv, "independent_origin_count", None),
830	                    "certainty_label": getattr(sv, "certainty_label", ""),
831	                    "soft_warnings": list(getattr(sv, "soft_warnings", None) or []),
832	                }
833	                for sv in kept
834	            ],
835	        })
836	    q_rows = (
837	        quantified_telemetry.get("claim_disclosure")
838	        if isinstance(quantified_telemetry, dict) else None
839	    )
840	    if q_rows:
841	        sections.append({"title": "Quantified Trade-off", "claims": list(q_rows)})
842	    return {"sections": sections}

845	def make_feature_telemetry(feature: str, **extra: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
846	    """I-ready-005 (#1076): default per-feature FIRING telemetry surfaced to the manifest so the operator
847	    can prove a forced-ON benchmark feature actually fired. firing_status: not_enabled ->
848	    attempted_empty (block ran) -> fired (produced output) / error. ``extra`` adds feature-specific
849	    count keys (e.g. questions_added, urls_discovered).
850	
851	    I-ready-016 (#1086): the key is ``firing_status`` (NOT ``status``) — it is a per-feature firing
852	    state stamped under manifest['<feature>'], NOT the manifest's own pipeline ``status``. The rename
853	    keeps the manifest-status authoritativeness contract (test_manifest_contract) from mistaking a
854	    feature firing-state for a manifest status."""
855	    base = {"feature": feature, "enabled": False, "fired": False, "firing_status": "not_enabled"}
856	    base.update(extra)
857	    return base

919	def eval_family_disclosure_clause(
920	    gen_family: str, eval_family: str, *, permit_same_family: bool = False
921	) -> str:
922	    """B04 / B4 (#1360 deepfix): the Methods clause that HONESTLY states the generator vs
923	    evaluator training-lineage relationship for THIS run — never the old hardcoded
924	    "(different family)" lie.
925	
926	    Two-family DNA (CLAUDE.md §9.1.1): generator and evaluator MUST be from distinct training
927	    lineages so the evaluator does not share the generator's RLHF blind spots
928	    (Play Favorites arXiv:2508.06709). ``check_family_segregation`` enforces this at construction
929	    UNLESS the operator sets ``PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY=1`` (the operator-approved
930	    all-GLM-5.2 campaign, #1285). When the override is active the invariant is DELIBERATELY voided
931	    for the run; the Methods section must DISCLOSE that LOUD — it must NOT keep claiming a benign
932	    "(glm lineage)" that hides the voided safeguard.
933	
934	    The same-family clause deliberately contains the literal tokens ``not family-segregated``,
935	    ``same family`` and ``self-bias safeguard disabled`` — these are the SHARED-LITERAL CONTRACT the
936	    (foreign) PT03 disclosure check in src/polaris_graph/evaluator/external_evaluator.py matches
937	    against to decide PT03 passes on an override run iff the report HONESTLY discloses the override.
938	    If you change these tokens, update the PT03 matcher in lockstep.
939	
940	    Pure; no I/O, no env read inside (caller passes the resolved ``permit_same_family``) so it is
941	    unit-testable without the pipeline.
942	    """
943	    if gen_family != eval_family:
944	        return (
945	            f"({eval_family} lineage; generator is {gen_family} — distinct training families)"
946	        )
947	    # gen_family == eval_family: the two-family invariant is VOIDED for this run.
948	    if permit_same_family:
949	        return (
950	            f"(NOT family-segregated — operator override "
951	            f"PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY=1; generator and evaluator are the "
952	            f"same family '{eval_family}'; evaluator self-bias safeguard disabled for this "
953	            f"run, disclosed)"
954	        )
955	    # Same family WITHOUT the override should never reach here (check_family_segregation raises
956	    # RuntimeError at construction), but disclose honestly as defense-in-depth if it does.
957	    return (
958	        f"(NOT family-segregated — generator and evaluator are the same family '{eval_family}' "
959	        f"and no PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY override is set; self-bias safeguard "
960	        f"state is INCONSISTENT)"
961	    )

990	    discovery_telemetries: list[dict[str, Any]],
991	    *,
992	    unified_status: str,
993	    gate_on: bool,
994	    storm_min_effective_queries: int = 0,
995	) -> dict[str, Any]:
996	    """FL-05 (#1124): run-health backstop DECISION (pure, no I/O — testable). A FORCE-ENABLED
997	    discovery feature (``enabled`` True) whose ``firing_status`` is in ``_FL05_DEGRADED_FIRING``
998	    means the run silently fell back to the baseline. Returns the observability fields ALWAYS, plus
999	    an ``override_status`` of ``abort_discovery_degraded`` IFF ``gate_on`` AND at least one degraded
1000	    force-enabled feature AND the run is otherwise success-bound. NEVER overrides a partial_/abort_/

content.content:
1000	    force-enabled feature AND the run is otherwise success-bound. NEVER overrides a partial_/abort_/
1001	    error_ status (those are more specific) — only a would-be ``success``. This promotes
1002	    ``feature_firing_warning`` from advisory to gating (CANARY-01 is the pre-spend gate; FL-05 is the
1003	    mid/post-run regression backstop).
1004	
1005	    I-fetch-002 (#1168): STORM UNDER-fire (post-validator collapse). The classic FL-05 path above only
1006	    catches a TOTAL no-fire (firing_status attempted_empty/error). A run where STORM force-on FIRED
1007	    (firing_status=fired) but produced FEWER than ``storm_min_effective_queries`` EFFECTIVE queries
1008	    (``effective_query_count``) is a thin-corpus collapse that would otherwise ship green. Such a
1009	    feature is ADDED to the degraded set (same abort path, same release-withhold). The check requires
1010	    ``effective_query_count`` to be PRESENT and not None (never treats an absent field as 0), so a
1011	    feature that does not publish the count — e.g. agentic, which publishes urls_discovered — never
1012	    trips it. ``storm_min_effective_queries`` defaults to 0 (disabled): with the default, ``count < 0``
1013	    never fires, so every pre-existing caller/test is byte-unchanged. Discovery-health only (no
1014	    faithfulness path touched)."""
1015	    degraded = [
1016	        t for t in discovery_telemetries
1017	        if t.get("enabled") and t.get("firing_status") in _FL05_DEGRADED_FIRING
1018	    ]
1019	    # I-fetch-002 (#1168): UNDER-fire — fired but below the effective-query floor. Mutually exclusive
1020	    # with the TOTAL no-fire set above (firing_status=fired vs attempted_empty/error), so no double-count.
1021	    under_fired = [
1022	        t for t in discovery_telemetries
1023	        if (
1024	            t.get("enabled")
1025	            and t.get("firing_status") == "fired"
1026	            and t.get("effective_query_count") is not None
1027	            and int(t.get("effective_query_count")) < storm_min_effective_queries
1028	        )
1029	    ]
1030	    all_degraded = degraded + under_fired
1031	    override = (
1032	        "abort_discovery_degraded"
1033	        if (gate_on and all_degraded and unified_status == "success")
1034	        else None
1035	    )
1036	    return {
1037	        "discovery_llm_degraded": bool(all_degraded),
1038	        "discovery_rounds_on_fallback": len(all_degraded),
1039	        "degraded_features": [
1040	            {"feature": t.get("feature"), "firing_status": t.get("firing_status")}
1041	            for t in degraded
1042	        ] + [
1043	            {
1044	                "feature": t.get("feature"),
1045	                "firing_status": "under_fired",
1046	                "effective_query_count": int(t.get("effective_query_count")),
1047	            }
1048	            for t in under_fired
1049	        ],
1050	        "override_status": override,
1051	    }

1100	# Lane C (#1179) — presentation hygiene for the three conflict-disclosure blocks.
1101	#
1102	# BB5-P01: the qualitative present-vs-absent detector emits one `- [REVIEW] ...`
1103	# line per flagged pair, uncapped + un-deduped — 246 lines = 82% of drb_75's file
1104	# (Gemini: 0). Qualitative records are NOT passed to the PT08 evaluator gate
1105	# (run_external_evaluation receives numeric + semantic records only — see the
1106	# render site near the report-assembly block), so collapsing review-flags to a
1107	# count + sidecar pointer and gating the clinical-safety detector to the clinical
1108	# domain is PT08-safe.
1109	#
1110	# BB5-P02: the cross-document NLI "Semantic contradiction" block prints each
1111	# record's full scraped row text (bibliographies, figure-markdown, image URLs).
1112	# Semantic records ARE PT08-checked (subject + predicate substring must appear in
1113	# the report), so the helper KEEPS subject + predicate verbatim for every record
1114	# and only trims the QUOTE payload + caps the inline count; full pairs go to a
1115	# sidecar.
1116	#
1117	# BB5-P03: drb_90 ships a verbatim-duplicated Implications/Limitations paragraph
1118	# and two `### Limitations` headers (outline Limitations + appended synthesized
1119	# one). The helper suppresses the appended synthesized Limitations when the
1120	# outline already wrote one, and de-dups identical paragraphs.
1121	#
1122	# All knobs are env-overridable; OFF/empty inputs yield byte-identical output.
1123	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1124	
1125	# BB5-P01 — env knob: render qualitative REVIEW-flag rows inline (default OFF —
1126	# they are advisory, not adjudicated conflicts, and the verbatim dump was 82% of
1127	# drb_75's file). When OFF, only auto-fired hard CONFLICT rows render in prose and
1128	# the review flags collapse to a one-line count + sidecar pointer.
1129	_QUAL_REVIEW_INLINE_ENV = "PG_SWEEP_QUAL_REVIEW_INLINE"
1130	_QUAL_SIDECAR_FILENAME = "contradictions.json"
1131	_TRUE_TOKENS = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
1132	
1133	# BB5-P02 — env knobs: max semantic pairs rendered inline, and the per-quote trim
1134	# length. Excess pairs go to the sidecar; subject + predicate of EVERY record
1135	# still render inline (PT08 contract).
1136	_SEMANTIC_INLINE_CAP_ENV = "PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_INLINE_CAP"
1137	_SEMANTIC_INLINE_CAP_DEFAULT = 10
1138	_SEMANTIC_QUOTE_TRIM_ENV = "PG_SWEEP_SEMANTIC_QUOTE_TRIM"
1139	_SEMANTIC_QUOTE_TRIM_DEFAULT = 200
1140	
1141	# BB5-P02 — a scraped row's "claim" text often carries a numbered bibliography,
1142	# figure/image markdown, and bare URLs. Strip those to a short normalized claim
1143	# summary before trimming to the quote cap.
1144	_BIBLIO_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:\[\d+\]|\d+[.)])\s+.*$", re.MULTILINE)
1145	_IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE = re.compile(r"!\[[^\]]*\]\([^)]*\)")
1146	_LINK_MARKDOWN_RE = re.compile(r"\[([^\]]*)\]\((?:https?://[^)]*)\)")
1147	_BARE_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")
1148	_WHITESPACE_RUN_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")

1150	# I-beatboth-011 b2 (#1289): a TRUNCATED / dangling image-markdown masthead. The COMPLETE
1151	# `_IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE` (`![alt](url)`) cannot match a fragment whose closing `)` was cut off when the
1152	# basket claim_text was capped (e.g. `... ![Image 4: shutterstock_2248569299](`), and these dangling
1153	# image mastheads are the DOMINANT crawl-chrome residue surviving in the rendered "## Bibliography"
1154	# corroboration headers. This strips an unterminated `![...]( ...` from the `![` marker to end-of-line
1155	# (or end-of-string). End-anchored so it only eats a trailing fragment — a complete `![alt](url)`
1156	# mid-string is left to `_IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE`; a bare `!` or `[` in real prose is never matched (the
1157	# `![` two-char marker is required). INPUT-hygiene only: removes confirmed image-masthead chrome,
1158	# never an assertional claim (§-1.3 no-source-dropped).
1159	_DANGLING_IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE = re.compile(r"!\[[^\n]*$")
1160	
1161	# I-beatboth-011 b2 (#1289): the named crawl-chrome tokens this fix screens a rendered TITLE/HEADER
1162	# for. If any survives the cleaner (e.g. an inline image fragment too mangled to strip, or a marker
1163	# the whole-line allowlist missed), the caller falls back to a non-chrome label rather than titling
1164	# the source with crawl chrome. Substring (not whole-line) so it catches the inline-collapsed shape.
1165	_NAMED_TITLE_CHROME_RE = re.compile(
1166	    r"Markdown Content|URL Source|Published Time\s*:|Number of Pages|!\[Image|ISSN\s*:?\s*\d|Cite this paper as",
1167	    re.IGNORECASE,
1168	)

1171	def _title_has_named_chrome(text: str) -> bool:
1172	    """I-beatboth-011 b2 (#1289): True iff a rendered bibliography TITLE/HEADER still carries a named
1173	    crawl-chrome token after cleaning. PURE; used to trigger the non-chrome-label fallback so a source
1174	    is never TITLED with crawl chrome. Never drops the source — only changes the displayed string."""
1175	    return bool(text) and bool(_NAMED_TITLE_CHROME_RE.search(text))

1182	# merge ~8713). Hoist the EXACT predicate + env gate + telemetry of the 6963
1183	# seam into a single module-level helper so all three seams share ONE source of
1184	# truth. A row/source that is NOT junk passes through byte-identical; the env

1203	def _junk_ev_row_direct_quote(_row: Any) -> str:
1204	    """U20 (I-deepfix-001): the row's ACTUAL fetched span (``direct_quote``) with NO
1205	    ``statement`` fallback.
1206	
1207	    ``_junk_ev_row_text`` deliberately falls back to ``statement`` so the error-shell
1208	    BODY screen has text to inspect. But an EMPTY ``direct_quote`` (a source that
1209	    contributed no grounding span) is itself junk that inflates breadth — and the
1210	    statement fallback would MASK it. So the empty-quote screen reads the raw
1211	    ``direct_quote`` field only. '' when absent."""
1212	    if isinstance(_row, dict):
1213	        return str(_row.get("direct_quote") or "")
1214	    return str(getattr(_row, "direct_quote", "") or "")

1217	def _a15_row_furniture_degraded(
1218	    grounding: str, *, screen_on: bool, is_dominant_fn: "Any" = None
1219	) -> bool:
1220	    """I-deepfix-001 B1 (wave-2 RESUME WIRING) — furniture-degraded verdict for ONE banked
1221	    resume row, for the A15 degraded-detector.
1222	
1223	    True iff the B1 furniture-density screen is ON (``PG_FURNITURE_DENSITY_SCREEN``, surfaced by
1224	    the caller as ``screen_on``) AND the row's banked grounding (``direct_quote`` / body) is
1225	    furniture-DOMINANT per the READ-ONLY B1 predicate ``shell_detector.is_furniture_dominant``
1226	    (passed in as ``is_dominant_fn``). A furniture-dominant banked row is a degraded big-PDF
1227	    extraction (masthead / nav / DOI / license welded together) — flagging it degraded lets the
1228	    A15 cascade re-fetch it with the fixed mineru (B2) and recover the real article.
1229	
1230	    Pure + fail-open: a predicate/import error yields ``False`` so a screen failure never INVENTS a
1231	    degraded flag. Gated on ``screen_on`` (default OFF): OFF => always ``False`` => NO extra rows
1232	    flagged => byte-identical. NEVER drops a row — it only marks it for the existing re-fetch."""
1233	    if not screen_on or is_dominant_fn is None:
1234	        return False
1235	    try:
1236	        return bool(is_dominant_fn(grounding))
1237	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a screen error never invents a degraded flag
1238	        return False

1248	def _screen_junk_evidence(
1249	    rows: "list[Any] | None",
1250	    srcs: "list[Any] | None",
1251	    *,
1252	    log: "Any" = None,
1253	    run_dir: "Any" = None,
1254	    label: str = "",
1255	) -> "tuple[list[Any], list[Any], dict[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]":
1256	    """Drop confirmed JUNK rows/sources before they reach generator/bibliography.
1257	
1258	    SINGLE source of truth for the junk-SOURCE screen (Codex P1-1, #1289). Same
1259	    predicate as the original 6963 seam: ``is_junk_source(url, text)`` on each
1260	    evidence row (host junk OR error-shell body) and ``is_junk_source_host(url)``
1261	    on each classified source (host only — sources carry no fetched body). Same
1262	    env gate, same log line, same excluded-json telemetry shape.
1263	
1264	    Args:
1265	      rows: evidence rows to screen (``None`` => no row screen, e.g. the
1266	        required-entity lane which has no separate classified_sources merge).
1267	      srcs: classified sources to screen (``None`` => no source screen).
1268	      log: optional ``_log`` callable; a drop summary is logged when given.
1269	      run_dir: optional ``Path``; excluded telemetry is written under it.
1270	      label: a distinct seam tag so telemetry (log + json filename) shows which
1271	        seam dropped what. ``""`` == the canonical initial seam (byte-identical
1272	        log + ``junk_source_excluded.json`` path).
1273	
1274	    Returns ``(kept_rows, kept_srcs, excluded)`` where ``excluded`` carries the
1275	    dropped row/source url+reason lists. When ``PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN=0`` the
1276	    original list objects are returned unchanged (byte-identical legacy no-op).
1277	    """
1278	    _rows_in = list(rows) if rows is not None else []
1279	    _srcs_in = list(srcs) if srcs is not None else []
1280	    if os.getenv("PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN", "1").strip() == "0":
1281	        # Kill-switch: return the ORIGINAL objects unchanged (no copy, no drops).
1282	        return (
1283	            rows if rows is not None else [],
1284	            srcs if srcs is not None else [],
1285	            {"evidence_rows_excluded": [], "classified_sources_excluded": []},
1286	        )
1287	
1288	    from src.tools.access_bypass import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1289	        is_junk_source as _is_junk_source,
1290	        is_junk_source_host as _is_junk_source_host,
1291	    )
1292	
1293	    _rows_kept: list[Any] = []
1294	    _rows_excluded: list[dict[str, str]] = []
1295	    if rows is not None:
1296	        for _row in _rows_in:
1297	            # Evidence rows carry the fetched body (direct_quote) AND the URL, so
1298	            # both signals apply (host junk + error-shell body + captcha/cookie
1299	            # interstitial span, U20).
1300	            if _is_junk_source(_junk_ev_row_url(_row), _junk_ev_row_text(_row)):
1301	                _rows_excluded.append(
1302	                    {"url": _junk_ev_row_url(_row)[:300], "reason": "junk_source"}
1303	                )
1304	            # U20 (I-deepfix-001): a row whose ACTUAL fetched span is empty/whitespace
1305	            # contributed no grounding and must not count toward breadth. Checked on the
1306	            # raw direct_quote (NOT the statement fallback that _junk_ev_row_text uses),
1307	            # so a phantom row with a model statement but no source span is dropped. This
1308	            # runs at corpus consumption AFTER all retrieval, so direct_quote is settled;
1309	            # faithfulness-neutral (an ungrounded row can never verify anyway).
1310	            elif not _junk_ev_row_direct_quote(_row).strip():
1311	                _rows_excluded.append(
1312	                    {"url": _junk_ev_row_url(_row)[:300], "reason": "empty_quote"}
1313	                )
1314	            else:
1315	                _rows_kept.append(_row)
1316	
1317	    _srcs_kept: list[Any] = []
1318	    _srcs_excluded: list[dict[str, str]] = []
1319	    if srcs is not None:
1320	        for _src in _srcs_in:
1321	            # CorpusSource carries no fetched body — screen on HOST only.
1322	            if _is_junk_source_host(_junk_src_url(_src)):
1323	                _srcs_excluded.append(
1324	                    {"url": _junk_src_url(_src)[:300], "reason": "junk_source_host"}
1325	                )
1326	            else:
1327	                _srcs_kept.append(_src)
1328	
1329	    if (_rows_excluded or _srcs_excluded):
1330	        _seam = f" [{label}]" if label else ""
1331	        if log is not None:
1332	            log(
1333	                f"[junk_screen]{_seam} dropped {len(_rows_excluded)} junk "
1334	                f"evidence_rows ({len(_rows_in)} -> {len(_rows_kept)}) and "
1335	                f"{len(_srcs_excluded)} junk classified_sources "
1336	                f"({len(_srcs_in)} -> {len(_srcs_kept)})"
1337	            )
1338	        if run_dir is not None:
1339	            # Canonical filename for the initial seam (label==""); a distinct,
1340	            # non-clobbering filename for each later seam so per-round drops are
1341	            # not overwritten (the gap round fires repeatedly).
1342	            _fname = (
1343	                "junk_source_excluded.json"
1344	                if not label
1345	                else f"junk_source_excluded_{label}.json"
1346	            )
1347	            try:
1348	                (run_dir / _fname).write_text(
1349	                    json.dumps(
1350	                        {
1351	                            "evidence_rows_excluded": _rows_excluded,
1352	                            "classified_sources_excluded": _srcs_excluded,
1353	                        },
1354	                        indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str,
1355	                    ) + "\n",
1356	                    encoding="utf-8",
1357	                )
1358	            except Exception as _js_write_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry best-effort
1359	                if log is not None:
1360	                    log(f"[junk_screen]{_seam} excluded-telemetry write skipped: "
1361	                        f"{_js_write_exc}")
1362	
1363	    return (
1364	        _rows_kept if rows is not None else [],
1365	        _srcs_kept if srcs is not None else [],
1366	        {
1367	            "evidence_rows_excluded": _rows_excluded,
1368	            "classified_sources_excluded": _srcs_excluded,
1369	        },
1370	    )

1397	def _screen_blocked_references(
1398	    rows: "list[Any] | None",
1399	    srcs: "list[Any] | None",
1400	    registry: "Any",
1401	    *,
1402	    log: "Any" = None,
1403	    run_dir: "Any" = None,
1404	    label: str = "",
1405	) -> "tuple[list[Any], list[Any], dict[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]":
1406	    """I-deepfix-001 B2 (#1346): drop operator-PROHIBITED rows/sources (the per-work
1407	    blocked-reference deny-list) before they reach the generator / bibliography.
1408	
1409	    THE one legitimate HARD DROP in the pipeline (CLAUDE.md §-1.3) — an EXPLICIT operator
1410	    do-not-view prohibition named in the question appendix, NOT a relevance/credibility/tier
1411	    call. Each leg of ``registry.is_blocked`` (canonical url / doi / publisher pii / fuzzy
1412	    title) is checked so a blocked MIRROR that is not literally listed is still caught.
1413	
1414	    Fail-LOUD: every exclusion is logged AND written to a disclosed-exclusion telemetry file
1415	    (``blocked_reference_excluded[_<label>].json``) — never a silent drop. Empty registry
1416	    (no appendix / ``PG_BLOCKED_REFERENCE_DENYLIST=0``) => the ORIGINAL objects are returned
1417	    unchanged (byte-identical no-op). Mirrors ``_screen_junk_evidence``'s shape exactly.
1418	    """
1419	    _rows_in = list(rows) if rows is not None else []
1420	    _srcs_in = list(srcs) if srcs is not None else []
1421	    if registry is None or getattr(registry, "is_empty", True):
1422	        return (
1423	            rows if rows is not None else [],
1424	            srcs if srcs is not None else [],
1425	            {"evidence_rows_excluded": [], "classified_sources_excluded": []},
1426	        )
1427	
1428	    _rows_kept: list[Any] = []
1429	    _rows_excluded: list[dict[str, str]] = []
1430	    if rows is not None:
1431	        for _row in _rows_in:
1432	            _hit, _reason = registry.is_blocked(
1433	                url=_junk_ev_row_url(_row),
1434	                doi=_blocked_ref_row_doi(_row),
1435	                title=_blocked_ref_row_title(_row),
1436	            )
1437	            if _hit:
1438	                _rows_excluded.append(
1439	                    {
1440	                        "url": _junk_ev_row_url(_row)[:300],
1441	                        "reason": f"blocked_reference:{_reason}"[:300],
1442	                    }
1443	                )
1444	            else:
1445	                _rows_kept.append(_row)
1446	
1447	    _srcs_kept: list[Any] = []
1448	    _srcs_excluded: list[dict[str, str]] = []
1449	    if srcs is not None:
1450	        for _src in _srcs_in:
1451	            _hit, _reason = registry.is_blocked(
1452	                url=_junk_src_url(_src),
1453	                title=_blocked_ref_row_title(_src),
1454	            )
1455	            if _hit:
1456	                _srcs_excluded.append(
1457	                    {
1458	                        "url": _junk_src_url(_src)[:300],
1459	                        "reason": f"blocked_reference:{_reason}"[:300],
1460	                    }
1461	                )
1462	            else:
1463	                _srcs_kept.append(_src)
1464	
1465	    if (_rows_excluded or _srcs_excluded):
1466	        _seam = f" [{label}]" if label else ""
1467	        if log is not None:
1468	            log(
1469	                f"[blocked_ref_screen]{_seam} dropped {len(_rows_excluded)} PROHIBITED "
1470	                f"evidence_rows ({len(_rows_in)} -> {len(_rows_kept)}) and "
1471	                f"{len(_srcs_excluded)} PROHIBITED classified_sources "
1472	                f"({len(_srcs_in)} -> {len(_srcs_kept)}) [operator do-not-view deny-list]"
1473	            )
1474	        if run_dir is not None:
1475	            _fname = (
1476	                "blocked_reference_excluded.json"
1477	                if not label
1478	                else f"blocked_reference_excluded_{label}.json"
1479	            )
1480	            try:
1481	                (run_dir / _fname).write_text(
1482	                    json.dumps(
1483	                        {
1484	                            "evidence_rows_excluded": _rows_excluded,
1485	                            "classified_sources_excluded": _srcs_excluded,
1486	                        },
1487	                        indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str,
1488	                    ) + "\n",
1489	                    encoding="utf-8",
1490	                )
1491	            except Exception as _bx_write_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry best-effort
1492	                if log is not None:
1493	                    log(f"[blocked_ref_screen]{_seam} excluded-telemetry write skipped: "
1494	                        f"{_bx_write_exc}")
1495	
1496	    return (
1497	        _rows_kept if rows is not None else [],
1498	        _srcs_kept if srcs is not None else [],
1499	        {
1500	            "evidence_rows_excluded": _rows_excluded,
1501	            "classified_sources_excluded": _srcs_excluded,
1502	        },

1522	# --- I-pipe-010/013/014/012/017/001 (#1235/#1238/#1239/#1237/#1242/#1226) -------------
1523	# Forensic dual Claude+Codex 2026-06-12 (.codex/I-bench-veracity-003-forensic/) surfaced a
1524	# cluster of run-sweep gates that FAIL OPEN on a benchmark run: a skewed corpus is accepted
1525	# as sweep-ready with only a disclosure (#1235), a V30-contract fault silently degrades to
1526	# the legacy generator (#1238), a manifest can read `success` without the 4-role D8 audit
1527	# ever running (#1226), and an enabled-but-empty quantified differentiator passes silently
1528	# (#1237). The shared strict-gate flag below turns those four fail-open paths LOUD on a
1529	# benchmark run (abort / raise / hold) WITHOUT touching strict_verify, the NLI judge, the
1530	# 4-role D8 release policy, or any provenance check. DEFAULT OFF: with the flag unset, every
1531	# `_benchmark_strict_gates_on()`-guarded branch is skipped and the file is behaviorally
1532	# byte-identical to today.
1533	#
1534	# #1239 (empty bibliography locator) and #1242 (tier-mix self-contradiction) get their OWN
1535	# narrow flags (a selection/rendering change and a pure-correctness change respectively), per
1536	# the SAFE-PATTERN gating split — they are NOT folded into the shared strict flag.
1537	_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES_ENV = "PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES"
1538	_V30_ALLOW_LEGACY_FALLBACK_ENV = "PG_V30_ALLOW_LEGACY_FALLBACK"
1539	_BIB_REQUIRE_LOCATOR_ENV = "PG_BIB_REQUIRE_LOCATOR"

1590	# --- I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252): fail-CLOSED faithfulness-slate preflight ----------
1591	# The benchmark certification run must run with EVERY faithfulness gate BINDING, not
1592	# advisory. Forensic dual-audit (#1249/#1252) found the gates could silently fall to
1593	# advisory/fail-open on a benchmark run: PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES unset (gates advisory),
1594	# the NLI entailment judge in 'warn'/'off' (non-binding), the NLI cross-document conflict
1595	# detector OFF, and the entailment model drifted off the locked mirror. This preflight is
1596	# the fail-CLOSED assertion: when the operator asks for strict gates, it REFUSES to start
1597	# the paid run unless the whole slate is correctly set. It STRENGTHENS the hard gate (it can
1598	# only abort a misconfigured run); it never relaxes one. NOT auto-on — it fires only when
1599	# PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES is truthy, so a non-benchmark / OFF run is byte-identical.
1600	_LOCKED_ENTAILMENT_MIRROR_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.1"  # the 4-role MIRROR (CLAUDE.md §9.1.8)
1601	_TRUE_ENV_TOKENS = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})

1603	# ── W1 intent_frame (I-wire-001) integrator knobs (LAW VI — no magic numbers) ──
1604	# The advisory intent-decomposition step uses the GLM-5.2 mirror policy. The model
1605	# is the SAME PG_MIRROR_MODEL seam _iter_llm reads; this default mirrors that one.
1606	_INTENT_FRAME_DEFAULT_MIRROR_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"
1607	_INTENT_FRAME_MAX_TOKENS_ENV = "PG_SCOPE_INTENT_FRAME_MAX_TOKENS"
1608	# §9.1.8 (reasoning + max_tokens ALWAYS go MAX, never starve): request a generous
1609	# completion budget, exactly like the governed quantified-spec call-site
1610	# (PG_QUANTIFIED_SPEC_MAX_TOKENS=32768). This is NOT a target — the shared
1611	# token_limit_resolver.finalize_body chokepoint inside OpenRouterClient._call_impl
1612	# reconciles this DOWN to min(provider_completion_cap, context_length - prompt -
1613	# safety_margin) against the model's REAL OpenRouter cap as the final body mutation,
1614	# so a generous default is free insurance, never an HTTP-400 risk. The prior flat
1615	# 8192 STARVED the GLM-5.2 mirror: it is an _ALWAYS_REASON model that emits a long
1616	# reasoning prelude before content, so 8192 could be consumed entirely by reasoning,
1617	# returning EMPTY content -> IntentFrameError -> fail-closed abort of the query.
1618	_INTENT_FRAME_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 32768  # generous; resolver clamps to real cap (§9.1.8)
1619	# §9.1.8: reasoning EFFORT stays MAX (do NOT force reasoning off), but the reasoning
1620	# POOL is bounded so a runaway prelude on the GLM-5.2 _ALWAYS_REASON mirror cannot
1621	# burn the whole budget and starve content (the documented quantified-spec failure;
1622	# same fix as PG_QUANTIFIED_SPEC_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS). A bounded reasoning budget
1623	# reserves a fixed completion slice for the closing-brace JSON the parser needs.
1624	_INTENT_FRAME_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS_ENV = "PG_SCOPE_INTENT_FRAME_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS"
1625	_INTENT_FRAME_DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = 8192  # leaves ~24K for content (§9.1.8)

1633	def assert_faithfulness_slate_or_fail() -> None:
1634	    """Fail-CLOSED preflight for the strict-gate benchmark slate (I-arch-004 F07).
1635	
1636	    No-op unless PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES is truthy. When strict gates are requested,
1637	    assert the FULL binding-faithfulness slate and RAISE
1638	    FaithfulnessSlatePreflightError listing every misconfiguration, so the run aborts
1639	    before a single generator/judge token is billed against an advisory gate:
1640	
1641	      * PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT == 'enforce'  (the NLI entailment judge is BINDING —
1642	        'warn'/'off' make it advisory: a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict would not drop).
1643	      * PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT truthy              (the cross-document NLI conflict detector
1644	        is ON — the lethal prose-only contradiction class is covered).
1645	      * effective entailment model == the locked MIRROR (z-ai/glm-5.1)  (no stale Gemma
1646	        default; PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL, if set, must equal the mirror).
1647	
1648	    PURE env read; raises or returns None. Re-run on --resume too (a strict run must not
1649	    resume into a non-strict env)."""
1650	    if not _benchmark_strict_gates_on():
1651	        return
1652	    problems: list[str] = []
1653	
1654	    ent_mode = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT", "enforce").strip().lower()
1655	    if ent_mode != "enforce":
1656	        problems.append(
1657	            f"PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT={ent_mode!r} — must be 'enforce' so the NLI "
1658	            f"entailment judge is BINDING (a non-ENTAILED verdict drops the sentence); "
1659	            f"'warn'/'off' make it advisory."
1660	        )
1661	
1662	    nli_conflict = os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT", "").strip().lower()
1663	    if nli_conflict not in _TRUE_ENV_TOKENS:
1664	        problems.append(
1665	            f"PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT={nli_conflict!r} — must be truthy so the cross-document "
1666	            f"NLI contradiction detector runs (the prose-only lethal-miss class)."
1667	        )
1668	
1669	    # The invariant is "entailment judge == the locked 4-role MIRROR" (no stale gemma, no weak
1670	    # encoder). The mirror is configurable per-arm (PG_MIRROR_MODEL) — e.g. the GLM-5.2 comparison
1671	    # arm swaps mirror z-ai/glm-5.1 -> deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro so the glm generator is never
1672	    # self-judged. Derive the EXPECTED mirror from PG_MIRROR_MODEL (fall back to the production
1673	    # default) so the invariant tracks the actual configured mirror. Byte-identical on the
1674	    # production/DeepSeek path (PG_MIRROR_MODEL unset/== glm-5.1). This STRENGTHENS the same gate
1675	    # (entailment must equal the configured strong, family-segregated mirror); it never relaxes it.
1676	    _expected_mirror = (os.environ.get("PG_MIRROR_MODEL", "").strip() or _LOCKED_ENTAILMENT_MIRROR_MODEL)
1677	    # Codex P0 (choke-fix iter2): resolve the entailment model EXACTLY as the RUNTIME does — fall back
1678	    # to entailment_judge._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL (the literal the judge uses when PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL
1679	    # is unset), NOT to _expected_mirror. Defaulting the preflight to _expected_mirror let it BLESS a
1680	    # model the runtime would not use: with PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL unset but PG_MIRROR_MODEL overridden
1681	    # (the GLM arm), the preflight saw ent==mirror and PASSED while the runtime judge still used its
1682	    # hardcoded glm-5.1 default -> a preflight/runtime mismatch (and it could likewise bless a garbage
1683	    # PG_MIRROR_MODEL override). Validating against the REAL runtime default (single source of truth via
1684	    # import) makes the preflight fail LOUDLY unless PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL is set to the mirror explicitly.
1685	    # Byte-identical on production (both unset => runtime default == _LOCKED mirror == 'z-ai/glm-5.1').
1686	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1687	        _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL as _runtime_entailment_default,
1688	    )
1689	    ent_model = os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", _runtime_entailment_default).strip()
1690	    if ent_model != _expected_mirror:
1691	        problems.append(
1692	            f"PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL={ent_model!r} — must equal the configured 4-role MIRROR "
1693	            f"{_expected_mirror!r} (PG_MIRROR_MODEL; CLAUDE.md §9.1.8; NO gemma). The entailment "
1694	            f"judge's runtime default is {_runtime_entailment_default!r}; when overriding "
1695	            f"PG_MIRROR_MODEL you MUST set PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL to the mirror explicitly so the "
1696	            f"preflight validates the model the runtime will actually load."
1697	        )
1698	
1699	    if problems:
1700	        raise FaithfulnessSlatePreflightError(
1701	            "I-arch-004 F07 strict-gate faithfulness preflight FAILED (fail-closed; "
1702	            "refusing to start a paid run with an advisory/misconfigured faithfulness "
1703	            "gate):\n  - " + "\n  - ".join(problems)
1704	        )

1727	def _corpus_skew_blocks_ready(
1728	    strict: bool,
1729	    has_material_deviation: bool,
1730	    *,
1731	    weighted_gate_on: bool = False,
1732	    proceed_on_skew: bool = False,
1733	    corpus_nonempty: bool = False,
1734	) -> bool:
1735	    """#1235: under strict gates, a corpus with a MATERIAL tier deviation must NOT be
1736	    silently accepted as sweep-ready on the weighted-corpus path — it falls through to the
1737	    EXISTING structured-authorization gate (check_auto_approve_allowed, which honors the
1738	    operator override PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL) instead. Returns True iff the caller must
1739	    bypass the weighted-corpus auto-approve. PURE — no faithfulness logic.
1740	
1741	    drb_72 weighted-gate proceed-on-skew (PG_WEIGHTED_GATE_PROCEED_ON_SKEW, default OFF): a hard
1742	    tier-COUNT refusal is itself the §-1.3 "filter-by-number" anti-pattern (it verifies no
1743	    individual claim). When the kill-switch is ON AND the weighted-corpus gate is ON AND the
1744	    corpus-ZERO floor passes (``corpus_nonempty``), a MATERIAL tier skew DISCLOSES-and-PROCEEDS
1745	    via the credibility-weighted path — i.e. this returns False so the caller's weighted
1746	    auto-approve stands. A genuinely EMPTY corpus (``corpus_nonempty`` False) STILL blocks. All
1747	    three extra params default False, so a positional call is byte-identical to the pre-existing
1748	    #1235 behavior (``bool(strict and has_material_deviation)``). The per-claim faithfulness floor
1749	    (strict_verify + 4-role D8) is untouched — this is the pre-generation CORPUS gate only."""
1750	    if not (strict and has_material_deviation):
1751	        return False
1752	    # strict + material deviation: the base #1235 behavior REFUSES. The default-OFF proceed-on-
1753	    # skew kill-switch relaxes that hard tier-COUNT refusal to disclose-and-proceed ONLY when the
1754	    # weighted gate is ON AND the corpus-ZERO floor passes (non-empty). Empty corpus still blocks.
1755	    if weighted_gate_on and proceed_on_skew and corpus_nonempty:
1756	        return False
1757	    return True

1760	def _weighted_corpus_proceed_on_skew_disclosure(
1761	    *,
1762	    strict: bool,
1763	    has_material_deviation: bool,
1764	    weighted_gate_on: bool,
1765	    proceed_on_skew: bool,
1766	    corpus_nonempty: bool,
1767	    tier_counts: "dict | None" = None,
1768	    tier_fractions: "dict | None" = None,
1769	    total_sources: int = 0,
1770	) -> "dict | None":
1771	    """drb_72 weighted-gate proceed-on-skew MUST-DISCLOSE record. Returns the discrete decision
1772	    dict when the PG_WEIGHTED_GATE_PROCEED_ON_SKEW path FIRED (strict + material deviation +
1773	    weighted gate ON + kill-switch ON + non-empty corpus) so the manifest / disclosure honestly
1774	    record that a MATERIAL tier skew was DISCLOSED-and-PROCEEDED rather than refused; None
1775	    otherwise => nothing attached => byte-identical OFF. PURE — no network / LLM / spend; the
1776	    per-claim faithfulness floor (strict_verify + 4-role D8) is untouched (this is the
1777	    pre-generation CORPUS gate). §-1.3: proceed+disclose is WEIGHT-not-FILTER (never drops a
1778	    source)."""
1779	    fired = bool(
1780	        strict
1781	        and has_material_deviation
1782	        and weighted_gate_on
1783	        and proceed_on_skew
1784	        and corpus_nonempty
1785	    )
1786	    if not fired:
1787	        return None
1788	    return {
1789	        "gate": _WEIGHTED_GATE_PROCEED_ON_SKEW_ENV,
1790	        "action": "disclose_and_proceed",

1792	            "Benchmark strict gates plus a MATERIAL tier deviation would, under #1235, hard-"
1793	            "refuse this corpus (abort_corpus_approval_denied) on a tier-COUNT proxy that "
1794	            "verifies no individual claim — itself the §-1.3 filter-by-number anti-pattern. With "
1795	            "PG_WEIGHTED_GATE_PROCEED_ON_SKEW ON and the weighted-corpus gate ON, the material "
1796	            "tier skew is DISCLOSED (see corpus_credibility_disclosure) and the run PROCEEDS; the "
1797	            "corpus-ZERO floor still refuses a genuinely empty corpus, and the per-claim "
1798	            "faithfulness floor (strict_verify + 4-role D8) is unchanged."
1799	        ),
1800	        "had_material_deviation": True,
1801	        "corpus_nonempty": True,
1802	        "total_sources": int(total_sources),
1803	        "tier_counts": dict(tier_counts or {}),

1827	# I-wire-003 B4 (#1317): the typed-status families that distinguish a SILENTLY-BROKEN /
1828	# SKIPPED quantified differentiator (the no-op the readiness gate must FAIL LOUD on) from a
1829	# RAN-AND-LEGITIMATELY-EMPTY one (the Writer honestly declined to model, or every computed
1830	# sentence failed Regime C — an HONEST empty the run discloses but does NOT abort on).
1831	#
1832	# BROKE/SKIPPED (fail readiness): the differentiator did not run cleanly to completion — a
1833	# transport/parse fault (spec_provider raised, non-JSON body, truncated/partial spec), a
1834	# malformed spec rejected by build_quantified_spec, or a sandbox execution failure. These are
1835	# the QUANTIFIED_STATUS_PARSE_ERROR / QUANTIFIED_STATUS_EMPTY_TRANSPORT typed statuses plus the
1836	# matching `firing_status` strings. A10 (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:_q_spec_provider) RAISES on
1837	# every empty/no-JSON/truncated branch, so `no_spec_returned` can ONLY reach here as an EXPLICIT
1838	# Writer decline -> it is NOT in this broke set.
1839	_QUANTIFIED_BROKE_STATUSES = frozenset({
1840	    "parse_error", "empty_transport",            # quantified_status (typed verdict)
1841	    "spec_provider_error", "spec_validation_rejected", "execution_failed",  # firing_status
1842	})
1843	# RAN-HONEST-EMPTY (do NOT fail readiness — disclose only): an explicit Writer decline (no
1844	# defensible model over the sourced numbers) or every computed sentence dropped by Regime C
1845	# (the faithfulness gate doing its job). These are legitimate non-fires, not silent breakage.
1846	_QUANTIFIED_HONEST_EMPTY_STATUSES = frozenset({
1847	    "declined_no_spec",                          # quantified_status (typed verdict)
1848	    "no_spec_returned", "no_verified_sentences",  # firing_status
1849	    # U27 (#1344): every extracted number was scrape-chrome/binary junk after shortlist
1850	    # curation -> a genuine data-shape reason the Writer had nothing clean to model. This
1851	    # is disclosed (never a silent no-op) but is NOT a code/transport break -> honest-empty.
1852	    "no_modelable_numbers",                      # firing_status
1853	})

1856	def _quantified_status_of(telemetry: dict) -> str:
1857	    """I-wire-003 B4 (#1317): the discrete typed status of a quantified-telemetry dict.
1858	    Prefers the machine-readable `quantified_status` (I-pipe-012, default-ON) and falls back to
1859	    the always-present `firing_status` (FIX D-2). Both name WHY the section did or did not land,
1860	    in the same broke-vs-honest-empty taxonomy. Returns '' when neither key is present (an
1861	    older / pre-typed-status telemetry shape — handled by the caller's fired-fallback). PURE."""
1862	    return str(telemetry.get("quantified_status") or telemetry.get("firing_status") or "")
