1865	def _quantified_readiness_failed(
1866	    strict: bool, force_enabled: bool, telemetry: "dict | None"
1867	) -> bool:
1868	    """#1237 + I-wire-003 B4 (#1317): under strict gates, if quantified analysis is
1869	    force-enabled (PG_ENABLE_QUANTIFIED_ANALYSIS=1) but the differentiator SILENTLY BROKE or
1870	    was SKIPPED, fail readiness LOUDLY instead of shipping a differentiator-less run as success.
1871	
1872	    B4 hardening — distinguish "ran + legitimately empty" from "silently skipped" (the #1237
1873	    fired-only gate lumped them, so a clean Writer decline tripped the same abort as a broken
1874	    transport). The gate now consumes the typed status:
1875	
1876	      * telemetry is None .................. force-enabled yet NO telemetry object exists -> the
1877	        block did not run at all (skipped) -> FAIL readiness.
1878	      * status in BROKE set ................ transport/parse/validation/execution fault -> the
1879	        differentiator broke -> FAIL readiness (even though `fired` is also False).
1880	      * status in HONEST-EMPTY set ......... explicit Writer decline OR every sentence dropped by
1881	        Regime C -> RAN and honestly produced nothing -> do NOT fail readiness (disclosed via
1882	        quantified_degradation_disclosure; never an abort).
1883	      * fired True ......................... the differentiator landed -> do NOT fail readiness.
1884	      * no typed status AND not fired ...... older/unknown telemetry shape with no verified
1885	        output -> fall back to the #1237 fired-only behavior (FAIL) so the guard never regresses
1886	        to a silent pass on a shape it cannot classify.
1887	
1888	    Returns True iff readiness must fail. PURE. Off (not strict / not force_enabled) => False =>
1889	    byte-identical to the legacy path. Touches NO faithfulness gate — it only forbids labeling a
1890	    broken/skipped force-on run as success, and only ever DEMOTES a would-be success."""
1891	    if not (strict and force_enabled):
1892	        return False
1893	    if telemetry is None:
1894	        # Force-enabled yet no telemetry object at all == the silent no-op this guards.
1895	        return True
1896	    if bool(telemetry.get("fired")):
1897	        # The differentiator landed (>=1 verified quantified sentence) — ready.
1898	        return False
1899	    status = _quantified_status_of(telemetry)
1900	    if status in _QUANTIFIED_BROKE_STATUSES:
1901	        # Silently broke (transport/parse/validation/execution) — fail loud.
1902	        return True
1903	    if status in _QUANTIFIED_HONEST_EMPTY_STATUSES:
1904	        # Ran and honestly produced nothing (decline / all-dropped) — disclose, do not abort.
1905	        return False
1906	    # No recognizable typed status AND not fired: an older telemetry shape we cannot classify.
1907	    # Fall back to the #1237 fired-only semantics (fail) so we never silently pass an
1908	    # unclassifiable no-op.
1909	    return True
1910	
1911	
1912	def _required_entity_ledger_failed_under_strict(
1913	    strict: bool, ledger_forced_on: bool, ledger_failed: bool
1914	) -> bool:
1915	    """I-arch-004 F27 (#1213 ledger / h3 forensic): under the strict-gate benchmark slate,
1916	    a FAILURE of the FORCE-ON RequiredEntityLedger must FAIL LOUD (release-blocking HOLD)
1917	    instead of the legacy fail-soft (a bare WARN log that silently DROPS the honest
1918	    "Coverage gaps" disclosure — overstating completeness on the very run whose differentiator
1919	    is honest gap-surfacing). Gate-B force-sets BOTH PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_LEDGER=1 and
1920	    PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES=1, so on the benchmark certification run a ledger build/render/
1921	    write exception now holds the run rather than shipping a gap-disclosure-less report as
1922	    success. Returns True iff the strict guard must convert a would-be success into a loud hold.
1923	    PURE — touches NO faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance) and adds
1924	    NO cap/target; it only forbids mislabeling a ledger-failed run as success. Off (no strict
1925	    gates) OR ledger not forced-on OR ledger succeeded => False => byte-identical fail-soft."""
1926	    return bool(strict and ledger_forced_on and ledger_failed)
1927	
1928	
1929	def _apply_required_entity_ledger_hold(
1930	    manifest: dict, error: "str | None"
1931	) -> "tuple[str, str]":
1932	    """I-arch-004 F27 (#1213/h3 forensic): apply the FAIL-LOUD HOLD for a strict-gate
1933	    required-entity-ledger failure to ``manifest`` IN PLACE, and return the new
1934	    ``(summary_status, unified_status)`` for the caller to thread into its local status vars
1935	    (so the success-path re-stamp + the summary mirror both carry the HOLD).
1936	
1937	    Surfacing contract (the F27 ACCEPT: a ledger failure is SURFACED, not a silent ok):
1938	      * manifest['status'] = 'abort_required_entity_ledger_failed' (release-blocking abort)
1939	      * manifest['release_allowed'] = False (a held status can never read as releasable)
1940	      * manifest['strict_gate_required_entity_ledger_failed'] = True (auditable boolean signal)
1941	      * manifest['required_entity_ledger_error'] = the captured exception string
1942	
1943	    PURE wrt faithfulness: touches NO gate; it only converts a would-be success into a loud,
1944	    auditable HOLD. The caller GUARDS this on _required_entity_ledger_failed_under_strict(...)
1945	    AND unified_status == 'success', so OFF / non-strict / non-forced / succeeded => never
1946	    called => byte-identical."""
1947	    summary_status = "abort_required_entity_ledger_failed"
1948	    unified_status = to_unified_status(summary_status)
1949	    manifest["status"] = unified_status
1950	    manifest["release_allowed"] = False
1951	    manifest["strict_gate_required_entity_ledger_failed"] = True
1952	    manifest["required_entity_ledger_error"] = error
1953	    return summary_status, unified_status
1954	
1955	
1956	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1957	# I-deepfix-001 U5 (#1344): SYNTHESIS-FIRES LIVE-CANARY (fail-loud ABORT).
1958	#
1959	# U4 (the consolidation keystone) now unions qualitative baskets, so consolidation
1960	# produces multi-source baskets and the verified-compose multi-cited path
1961	# (verified_compose.compose_basket_multicited_sentence, gated by
1962	# PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED — FORCE-ON in the Gate-B slate) SHOULD render at
1963	# least one CORROBORATED (multi-cited) sentence: a sentence carrying >=2 DISTINCT
1964	# `[#ev:<id>:...]` provenance tokens. The residual U5 risk: if composition regresses
1965	# to a pure span-dump (every kept sentence collapses to exactly ONE evidence id),
1966	# the report is deficient yet still reads `success`. This canary FAILS LOUD instead.
1967	#
1968	# It aborts ONLY when consolidation genuinely produced >=1 multi-source basket
1969	# (verified_support_origin_count>=2) AND the whole composed report has ZERO
1970	# multi-cited sentences. A single-source corpus (0 multi-source baskets) NEVER trips
1971	# it — synthesis EMERGES from real corroboration, it is never FORCED (§-1.3). It is
1972	# armed only when the multi-cite compose path is ON (else single-cite is the expected,
1973	# byte-identical output — asserting on it would be a false abort). It reads only
1974	# POST-verify telemetry (the kept-sentence provenance tokens + the credibility-pass
1975	# basket counts); it touches NO faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 /
1976	# provenance / span-grounding are byte-unchanged) and drops NO source. It does NOT
1977	# re-enable the banned PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS path — it inspects the
1978	# verified-compose multi-source output, never analyst-synthesis.
1979	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1980	
1981	def synthesis_fire_canary_enabled() -> bool:
1982	    """PG_SYNTHESIS_FIRE_CANARY kill-switch (default ON). OFF => the U5 synthesis-fires
1983	    canary never runs and the status decision is byte-identical to pre-fix. Read at CALL
1984	    time (LAW VI) so a slate/operator override after import wins."""
1985	    return os.getenv("PG_SYNTHESIS_FIRE_CANARY", "1").strip().lower() not in (
1986	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
1987	    )
1988	
1989	
1990	def _multicited_compose_on() -> bool:
1991	    """Mirror of verified_compose._multicited_compose_enabled (PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED,
1992	    DEFAULT-OFF). When OFF the compose path emits ONE `[#ev:]` token per sentence by design, so
1993	    zero multi-cited sentences is EXPECTED (not a defect) — the U5 canary must self-skip. Gate-B
1994	    force-ON's this flag, so the canary is armed on the paid benchmark run. Read at CALL time."""
1995	    return os.getenv("PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED", "0").strip().lower() not in (
1996	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
1997	    )

content.text: This is a very long file. The naming here is largely descriptive/professional (env vars, status taxonomy). The recurring concern is the "honest" adjective used pervasively, "true_drop", filename with "r3", and a few informal names like "junk". Let me note that "honest" here is used as a genuine domain concept in this codebase (honest disclosure), but at a strict external-review bar, "honest_sweep" in the filename and "true_drop"/"honest_empty" identifiers read as quality/marketing adjectives. Let me continue reading the rest of the range.

1999	
2000	def _distinct_evidence_ids_in_sentence(sentence: "Any") -> "set[str]":
2001	    """The set of DISTINCT non-blank provenance evidence ids carried by one composed sentence.
2002	
2003	    Reads the sentence's ``tokens`` (each a parsed `[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]` provenance token
2004	    with an ``evidence_id`` attribute) — the SAME per-sentence provenance the NLI annotator and
2005	    strict_verify consume. Pure; tolerant of a token-less or malformed sentence (empty set)."""
2006	    ids: set[str] = set()
2007	    for tok in (getattr(sentence, "tokens", None) or []):
2008	        eid = str(getattr(tok, "evidence_id", "") or "").strip()
2009	        if eid:
2010	            ids.add(eid)
2011	    return ids
2012	
2013	
2014	def count_multi_cited_sentences(sections: "Any") -> int:
2015	    """Number of KEPT (non-dropped) composed sentences that cite >=2 DISTINCT evidence ids
2016	    across the WHOLE report — i.e. the corroborated / "synthesized" sentences the multi-cite
2017	    compose path produces from a >=2-origin basket. A span-dump run (every sentence cites exactly
2018	    ONE evidence id) yields 0. Pure read of ``sections[].kept_sentences_pre_resolve[].tokens``;
2019	    skips sections flagged ``dropped_due_to_failure``. Tolerant of a None / attribute-less shape."""
2020	    n = 0
2021	    for section in (sections or []):
2022	        if getattr(section, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
2023	            continue
2024	        for sentence in (getattr(section, "kept_sentences_pre_resolve", None) or []):
2025	            if len(_distinct_evidence_ids_in_sentence(sentence)) >= 2:
2026	                n += 1
2027	    return n
2028	
2029	
2030	def count_multi_source_baskets(credibility_analysis: "Any") -> int:
2031	    """Number of consolidation baskets carrying >=2 verified DISTINCT-origin SUPPORTS members
2032	    (``verified_support_origin_count`` >= 2) — exactly the baskets that SHOULD each surface a
2033	    multi-cited sentence via ``compose_basket_multicited_sentence``. This is the authoritative
2034	    CONSOLIDATE-leg count (I-arch-010 invariant: isolated-verified distinct origins only), NOT
2035	    ``len(members)``. Pure read of ``credibility_analysis.baskets``; 0 when the credibility pass
2036	    did not run (None) or produced no baskets — so a run with no multi-source corroboration can
2037	    never trip the canary."""
2038	    baskets = getattr(credibility_analysis, "baskets", None) or []
2039	    count = 0
2040	    for basket in baskets:
2041	        try:
2042	            origins = int(getattr(basket, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0)
2043	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
2044	            origins = 0
2045	        if origins >= 2:
2046	            count += 1
2047	    return count
2048	
2049	
2050	def synthesis_did_not_fire(
2051	    *, multi_source_basket_count: int, multi_cited_sentence_count: int,
2052	) -> bool:
2053	    """PURE U5 decision: True iff consolidation produced >=1 multi-source basket but composition
2054	    rendered ZERO multi-cited sentences (the pure span-dump regression). A single-source corpus
2055	    (0 multi-source baskets) => False (synthesis is never FORCED; §-1.3). >=1 multi-cited sentence
2056	    anywhere in the report => False (synthesis fired). Mirrors the run-level M6 firing-canary
2057	    semantics: the WHOLE-run zero-count is the defect signal, not any single basket collapsing."""
2058	    return multi_source_basket_count > 0 and multi_cited_sentence_count == 0
2059	
2060	
2061	def _apply_synthesis_fire_hold(
2062	    manifest: dict, *, multi_source_basket_count: int, multi_cited_sentence_count: int,
2063	) -> "tuple[str, str]":
2064	    """Apply the U5 synthesis-fires ABORT to ``manifest`` in place and return the new
2065	    ``(summary_status, unified_status)``. Mirrors ``_apply_required_entity_ledger_hold``:
2066	
2067	      * manifest['status'] = 'abort_synthesis_did_not_fire' (release-blocking abort — deliberately
2068	        ABSENT from _B18_B19_CONVERTIBLE_HOLDS so always-release can never ship it as a disclosed
2069	        gap; a deficient span-dump report must not read releasable)
2070	      * manifest['release_allowed'] = False
2071	      * manifest['synthesis_fire_canary'] = auditable counts + aborted flag
2072	
2073	    PURE wrt faithfulness: converts a would-be success into a loud, auditable HOLD; touches no
2074	    faithfulness gate. The caller GUARDS this on synthesis_did_not_fire(...) AND
2075	    unified_status == 'success', so it only ever demotes a `success`."""
2076	    summary_status = "abort_synthesis_did_not_fire"
2077	    unified_status = to_unified_status(summary_status)
2078	    manifest["status"] = unified_status
2079	    manifest["release_allowed"] = False
2080	    manifest["synthesis_fire_canary"] = {
2081	        "multi_source_baskets": multi_source_basket_count,
2082	        "multi_cited_sentences": multi_cited_sentence_count,
2083	        "aborted": True,
2084	    }
2085	    return summary_status, unified_status

2088	# I-deepfix-001 A5 (#1344): canonical credibility tiers, ALWAYS disclosed in T1..T7 order so
2089	# the reader sees every band even at 0% (an OMITTED tier reads as "not assessed", and the drb_72
2090	# audit found the Methods "Actual distribution" line dropping absent tiers). Any extra key present
2091	# in the fractions (e.g. "UNKNOWN") is appended after, never dropped.
2092	_CANONICAL_TIER_ORDER = ("T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7")
2093	_TIER_MIX_SUM_TO_100_ENV = "PG_TIER_MIX_SUM_TO_100"
2094

2096	def _largest_remainder_percents(tier_fractions: "dict[str, float]") -> "dict[str, int]":
2097	    """Round each fraction to an INTEGER percent so the integers sum to EXACTLY 100 (Hamilton /
2098	    largest-remainder). Returns ``{}`` when the fractions do not form a positive distribution
2099	    (empty / all-zero) — the caller then discloses 0% for every tier WITHOUT fabricating a
2100	    spurious 100. PURE + deterministic (fractions are >= 0, so ``int(p)`` is the floor; ties in
2101	    the remainder pass are broken by tier key)."""
2102	    percents = {k: float(v) * 100.0 for k, v in (tier_fractions or {}).items()}
2103	    if sum(percents.values()) <= 0.0:
2104	        return {}
2105	    floors = {k: int(p) for k, p in percents.items()}  # p >= 0 => int() == floor()
2106	    remainder = 100 - sum(floors.values())
2107	    if remainder > 0:
2108	        # Hand the leftover points to the tiers with the largest fractional part (positive tiers
2109	        # only), ties broken deterministically by tier key. remainder < number-of-positive-tiers.
2110	        order = sorted(
2111	            (k for k, p in percents.items() if p > 0.0),
2112	            key=lambda k: (percents[k] - floors[k], k),
2113	            reverse=True,
2114	        )
2115	        for i in range(min(remainder, len(order))):
2116	            floors[order[i]] += 1
2117	    return floors
2118	
2119	
2120	def _deliverable_spec_from(compose_proj: "Any") -> "dict[str, Any] | None":
2121	    """Thin ADAPTER: the outline seam's ``deliverable_spec`` view of a compiled
2122	    ``ComposeRenderProjection`` (S4 compose injection).
2123	
2124	    HARD channel -> ``required_sections`` is EXACTLY ``proj.required_titles`` (only
2125	    exact-title-locked, origin-in-HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS titles; a kind-derived title
2126	    NEVER lands here — that would be a force leak). SOFT channel -> ``kind_guidance``
2127	    carries the deliverable-kind framing as a preference line for
2128	    ``build_requirements_block`` (leaving the domain system prompt + default allow-list
2129	    intact). ``audience``/``tone`` ride the existing advisory lines. Returns ``None``
2130	    when the projection is empty/None => the generator's outline seam is byte-identical.
2131	    """
2132	    if compose_proj is None:
2133	        return None
2134	    try:
2135	        required = [str(t).strip() for t in (getattr(compose_proj, "required_titles", []) or []) if str(t).strip()]
2136	        audience = str(getattr(compose_proj, "audience", "") or "").strip()
2137	        tone = str(getattr(compose_proj, "tone", "") or "").strip()
2138	        length_note = str(getattr(compose_proj, "length_note", "") or "").strip()
2139	        # SOFT kind guidance: the doc-type framing directive (preference only). Empty
2140	        # unless a doc_type resolves. This is guidance, never a required-title lock.
2141	        kind_guidance = ""
2142	        _dfn = getattr(compose_proj, "doc_type_directive", None)
2143	        if callable(_dfn):
2144	            kind_guidance = str(_dfn() or "").strip()
2145	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a spec fault => None => byte-identical outline
2146	        return None
2147	    if not (required or audience or tone or length_note or kind_guidance):
2148	        return None
2149	    spec: "dict[str, Any]" = {}
2150	    if required:
2151	        spec["required_sections"] = required
2152	    if audience:
2153	        spec["audience"] = audience
2154	    if tone:
2155	        spec["tone"] = tone
2156	    if length_note:
2157	        spec["length_target"] = length_note
2158	    if kind_guidance:
2159	        spec["kind_guidance"] = kind_guidance
2160	    return spec or None
2161	
2162	
2163	def _scope_spec_from(shape_contract: "Any") -> "dict[str, Any] | None":
2164	    """Thin ADAPTER: the outline seam's ``scope_spec`` view of the pinned contract's
2165	    ALREADY-PARSED scope terms (S4 compose injection). ADVISORY ONLY — it emits the
2166	    same "weight-demoted / prefer in-scope" outline lines ``build_requirements_block``
2167	    already renders; it changes NO retrieval/eligibility behavior (those are enforced
2168	    upstream from the same terms). Returns ``None`` when nothing routable => the outline
2169	    seam is byte-identical. Lossless read of the contract's canonical scope dimensions.
2170	    """
2171	    if shape_contract is None:
2172	        return None
2173	    scope_terms = getattr(shape_contract, "scope", None) or []
2174	    # canonical scope dimension -> build_requirements_block key (data map, not control flow).
2175	    _dim_to_key = {
2176	        "scope.date": "date_window",
2177	        "scope.jurisdiction": "geography",
2178	        "scope.source_types": "source_types",
2179	        "scope.source_languages": "language",
2180	        "scope.named_sources": "authors",
2181	    }
2182	    out: "dict[str, list[str]]" = {}
2183	    try:
2184	        for term in scope_terms:
2185	            dim = str(getattr(term, "dimension", "") or "").strip().lower()
2186	            key = _dim_to_key.get(dim)
2187	            if not key:
2188	                continue
2189	            val = getattr(term, "value", None)
2190	            vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]
2191	            for v in vals:
2192	                sv = str(v or "").strip()
2193	                if sv:
2194	                    out.setdefault(key, []).append(sv)
2195	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: a scope-read fault => None => byte-identical
2196	        return None
2197	    return out or None
2198

2200	def _tier_mix_disclosure_summary(tier_fractions: "dict[str, float]") -> str:
2201	    """#1242: the SINGLE source of truth for the per-tier mix percentage string rendered in
2202	    the report (e.g. ``T1=12%, T2=1%, ...``). The run's Methods "Actual distribution" line AND
2203	    the LLM-authored Limitations quote reference THIS one value (via ``tier_disclosure_override``)
2204	    so two disclosure strings can never quote different denominators/rounding. PURE + deterministic.
2205	
2206	    I-deepfix-001 A5 (#1344): default-ON ``PG_TIER_MIX_SUM_TO_100`` — the integer percents sum to
2207	    EXACTLY 100 via largest-remainder AND every canonical tier T1..T7 is shown (0% when absent),
2208	    so the rendered line can never read e.g. ``101%`` (independent per-tier rounding) or silently
2209	    OMIT a tier. Set the flag to 0 to revert to the prior present-keys-only, independent-rounding
2210	    builder. LABEL/disclosure only — faithfulness-neutral (touches no strict_verify / NLI /
2211	    provenance path; changes no source's tier, drops nothing)."""
2212	    fractions = tier_fractions or {}
2213	    if not _env_flag(_TIER_MIX_SUM_TO_100_ENV, default=True):
2214	        return ", ".join(f"{k}={v * 100:.0f}%" for k, v in sorted(fractions.items()))
2215	    # Canonical T1..T7 always shown, then any extra keys (e.g. UNKNOWN) in sorted order.
2216	    extra = sorted(k for k in fractions if k not in _CANONICAL_TIER_ORDER)
2217	    ordered_keys = list(_CANONICAL_TIER_ORDER) + extra
2218	    rounded = _largest_remainder_percents(fractions)
2219	    return ", ".join(f"{k}={rounded.get(k, 0)}%" for k in ordered_keys)
2220	
2221	
2222	def _bib_entry_has_locator(entry: "dict") -> bool:
2223	    """#1239: True iff a bibliography entry carries a non-blank URL or DOI locator. A CITED bib
2224	    entry (one assigned a citation number) with NO locator is unverifiable by the reader. PURE.
2225	
2226	    M3a (I-deepfix-001): a non-blank PMID is ALSO a resolvable locator
2227	    (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/<pmid>/), so a URL-less, DOI-less primary that still
2228	    carries a real PMID renders that PubMed link instead of the gap line. Only reached when
2229	    PG_BIB_REQUIRE_LOCATOR is ON (the sole call site is the require_locator render branch), so
2230	    the OFF path is byte-identical. Never fabricates — uses only ids the row already carries."""
2231	    url = str(entry.get("url") or "").strip()
2232	    doi = str(entry.get("doi") or "").strip()
2233	    pmid = str(entry.get("pmid") or "").strip()
2234	    return bool(url or doi or pmid)

2236	
2237	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2238	# I-arch-011 #1289 render-boundary screen — defects #2(render)/#3/#5/#10.
2239	#
2240	# The §-1.1 audit of drb_72 found 12 DO_NOT_SHIP defects, ALL in the render /
2241	# consolidation / composition / disclosure layer (the faithfulness engine is
2242	# correct — zero fabricated findings). These helpers screen the ALREADY-
2243	# strict_verify-PASSED body + Key-Findings block at the assemble_report_md
2244	# boundary. They NEVER touch strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding and
2245	# NEVER drop a real source — they remove page-furniture that was cited because
2246	# it is a VERBATIM span of fetched CHROME (a masthead, nav rail, ISSN, share-
2247	# button, ResearchGate metadata), correct a self-disclosure numeric error from
2248	# the actual sidecar, and guard a dangling cross-ref to files that do not exist.
2249	# All env-gated; OFF / no-match yields byte-identical output.
2250	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2251	
2252	# #2 (render side): a CHROME SPAN that survives strict_verify because it IS a
2253	# verbatim fetched-page-furniture span (journal masthead volume/number/pages run,
2254	# ISSN, ResearchGate "CITATIONS n READS n", left-rail nav "same series", raw fetch
2255	# framing "Markdown Content:" / "URL Source:", MIT Sloan "#main-content"). These are
2256	# NOT caught by access_bypass.is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional (which keys on
2257	# WHOLE-LINE boilerplate) because they render as a grammatical-looking fragment. The
2258	# detector targets the SPECIFIC furniture shapes the §-1.1 audit enumerated, so a
2259	# real finding sentence ("AI adoption raised productivity by 14% ...[12]") never
2260	# matches (no volume/ISSN/metadata/fetch-framing token).
2261	_RENDER_CHROME_SPAN_RE = re.compile(
2262	    r"\bVolume\s+\d+\s*,?\s*(?:Number|No\.?|Issue)\s+\d+\b"        # journal masthead volume/number
2263	    r"|\bPages?\s+\d+\s*[-‐-―]\s*\d+\b"                   # masthead page range "Pages 3-30"
2264	    r"|\bISSN\b\s*:?\s*\d"                                          # ISSN
2265	    r"|\bCITATIONS\b\s+\d+\s+\bREADS\b\s+\d+"                       # ResearchGate metadata
2266	    r"|\bMarkdown Content\s*:|\bURL Source\s*:|\bPublished Time\s*:"  # raw fetch framing
2267	    r"|\bNumber of Pages\s*:|\bCite this paper as\b"                # crawl/journal framing
2268	    r"|#main-content|Twitter-intent|twitter\.com/intent"  # nav / share-button UI anchors
2269	    # ILO LEFT-RAIL "same series" NAV: the ILO working-paper left rail lists sibling papers under a
2270	    # "same series" heading followed by "- Working paper <title>" nav rows (the audit's ILO bullet:
2271	    # "...Occupational Exposure same series - Working paper Insights from job vacancy data..."). The
2272	    # FULL nav signature is "same series" IMMEDIATELY followed by a "- Working paper" nav row — a bare
2273	    # mid-sentence "same series" (e.g. a real finding "papers in the same series show declining wages")
2274	    # carries NO "- Working paper" follow-on and is left untouched. Tightened 2026-06-22 (#1289 P1):
2275	    # the prior bare "\bsame series\b" over-stripped real prose; precision beats recall (§-1.1 forbids
2276	    # a pattern that catches legitimate text — over-strip deletes a real finding, worse than a leak).
2277	    r"|same series\s*[-‐-―]\s*working paper"
2278	    # OECD LEFT-RAIL TOPICS NAV: the OECD topics left rail dumps the site's topic taxonomy under a

2375	# I-deepfix-001 B6(b) (#1344): deterministic CLAIM-SHAPE gate for the per-claim corroboration
2376	# HEADER. LAW VI: env-driven knob (default ON), no magic numbers inlined into the call site.
2377	_CLAIM_SHAPE_GATE_ENV = "PG_CLAIM_SHAPE_GATE"

2379	_CLAIM_SHAPE_MIN_CONTENT_WORDS_DEFAULT = 3
2380	# A predicate/verb-ish token signals an assertional claim (vs a bare noun phrase / title fragment).
2381	# Lightweight closed-class list (copulas, auxiliaries, common reporting verbs) PLUS a morphological
2382	# fallback (a token ending in a verb-suffix) so it generalizes beyond the list. Deterministic; no
2383	# model call. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: this gates only the displayed HEADER, never a source/count.
2384	_CLAIM_SHAPE_VERB_TOKENS = frozenset({
2385	    "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being", "has", "have", "had",
2386	    "show", "shows", "showed", "shown", "report", "reports", "reported",
2387	    "found", "find", "finds", "increase", "increased", "increases", "decrease",
2388	    "decreased", "decreases", "reduce", "reduced", "reduces", "associated",
2389	    "demonstrate", "demonstrated", "demonstrates", "suggest", "suggests",
2390	    "suggested", "indicate", "indicates", "indicated", "cause", "causes",
2391	    "caused", "improve", "improved", "improves", "remain", "remains",
2392	    "remained", "occur", "occurs", "occurred", "include", "includes",
2393	    "included", "require", "requires", "required", "can", "may", "will",
2394	    "should", "must", "does", "do", "did",
2395	})
2396	# Morphological verb signal. "ed"/"ing" are strong verb markers; "es"/"s" are dropped — they match
2397	# common PLURAL NOUNS ("guidelines", "outcomes") and would mislabel a noun-phrase title as a claim.
2398	_CLAIM_SHAPE_VERB_SUFFIXES = ("ed", "ing")

2406	def _claim_has_predicate_token(text: str) -> bool:
2407	    """True iff ``text`` carries at least one predicate/verb-ish token — a deterministic signal that
2408	    the string is an ASSERTIONAL claim, not a bare noun phrase / title / nav fragment. Closed-class
2409	    list first, then a morphological suffix fallback so it generalizes. Pure."""
2410	    for tok in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]*", text.lower()):
2411	        if tok in _CLAIM_SHAPE_VERB_TOKENS:
2412	            return True
2413	        if len(tok) > 3 and tok.endswith(_CLAIM_SHAPE_VERB_SUFFIXES):
2414	            return True
2415	    return False

2418	def claim_is_renderable_claim_shape(text: str) -> bool:
2419	    """I-deepfix-001 B6(b) (#1344): the FINAL deterministic CLAIM-SHAPE gate for a corroboration
2420	    HEADER. Returns True iff ``text`` is a renderable assertional claim:

2422	          require_sentence_form=True), AND
2423	      (b) has >= PG_CLAIM_SHAPE_MIN_CONTENT_WORDS content words, AND
2424	      (c) carries >= 1 predicate/verb token (it is a CLAIM, not a noun-phrase label).
2425	    HEADER-TEXT-ONLY: a False here suppresses only the corroboration BLOCK for that basket; the
2426	    sources/counts still live in the bibliography (faithfulness-neutral; §-1.3 no source dropped).
2427	    Pure except the lazy chrome-screen import."""
2428	    s = (text or "").strip()
2429	    if not s:
2430	        return False
2431	    try:
2432	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
2433	            is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable,
2434	        )
2435	        # require_sentence_form=False: catch CHROME / a mid-word truncation fragment, but do NOT
2436	        # demand a terminal period — corroboration claim headers are trimmed summaries (often no
2437	        # closing '.'), so requiring a full-sentence terminator here would over-suppress legitimate
2438	        # claim headers (regressing the I-wire-014 154/155 recovery). Assertional SHAPE is enforced
2439	        # below by the content-word + predicate-token checks instead.
2440	        if is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(s, require_sentence_form=False):

2442	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - weighted_enrichment is stable in-tree
2443	        # Fail-OPEN on an infra import error: do not suppress a header on a tooling failure.
2444	        pass

2446	    try:
2447	        min_words = int(os.getenv(
2448	            _CLAIM_SHAPE_MIN_CONTENT_WORDS_ENV, str(_CLAIM_SHAPE_MIN_CONTENT_WORDS_DEFAULT),
2449	        ))
2450	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
2451	        min_words = _CLAIM_SHAPE_MIN_CONTENT_WORDS_DEFAULT
2452	    if len(content_words) < max(0, min_words):
2453	        return False
2454	    return _claim_has_predicate_token(s)

2536	def _complete_sentence_prefix(text: str, *, min_chars: int = 40, max_chars: int = 260) -> str:
2537	    """Longest leading run of COMPLETE sentence(s) in ``text`` (a leading doc-label like
2538	    'Abstract'/'Summary.' is stripped first), capped at ~``max_chars``. ``''`` when no complete
2539	    sentence is present (a mid-word/mid-sentence-truncated span yields nothing). Abbreviation and
2540	    initial periods (U.S., e.g., p., single-capital initials) do NOT end a sentence; a sentence
2541	    boundary requires the next non-space char to open a new sentence (uppercase/digit/quote) or EOS.
2542	    PURE."""
2543	    # Strip a trailing truncation marker first so 'a clause that is cut ...' yields no false
2544	    # sentence end from the ellipsis' final dot (#1334 Codex P1).
2545	    s = _DOC_LABEL_PREFIX_RE.sub("", str(text or "")).strip()
2546	    s = re.sub(r"\s*(?:\.\.\.+|…)\s*$", "", s).strip()
2547	    if not s:
2548	        return ""
2549	    best = ""
2550	    for m in _SENTENCE_END_ITER_RE.finditer(s):
2551	        if m.start() > 0 and s[m.start() - 1] in ".…":
2552	            continue  # a dot preceded by '.'/'…' is part of an ellipsis, not a sentence end
2553	        tail = s[m.end():]
2554	        if tail and not (tail[:1].isupper() or tail[:1].isdigit() or tail[:1] in "\"'("):
2555	            continue  # lower-case continuation -> not a real sentence boundary
2556	        prefix = s[: m.start()]
2557	        last_word = re.split(r"[\s(]", prefix)[-1].lower().rstrip(".") if prefix else ""
2558	        if last_word in _HEADER_ABBREV or len(last_word) == 1:
2559	            continue  # abbreviation / single-letter initial -> not a sentence end
2560	        cand = s[: m.start() + 1].strip()
2561	        if "..." in cand or "…" in cand:
2562	            continue  # candidate still carries an internal truncation marker -> not complete
2563	        if len(cand) > max_chars:
2564	            break
2565	        if len(cand) >= min_chars:
2566	            best = cand
2567	    return best
2568

2631	def _best_corroboration_header(basket: dict, statement: "str | None") -> str:
2632	    """FIX-A (#1334): the best renderable header for a corroboration basket — a real claim
2633	    SENTENCE, else the clean source TITLE — or ``''`` if nothing renders (caller keeps its
2634	    subject+predicate/ccid last resort). HEADER TEXT ONLY (faithfulness-neutral). PURE except
2635	    the lazy access_bypass import."""
2636	    from src.tools.access_bypass import clean_fetch_body  # noqa: PLC0415
2637	
2638	    # 1. complete-sentence prefix of the basket's representative claim_text
2639	    raw = str(basket.get("claim_text") or "")
2640	    if raw:
2641	        pref = _complete_sentence_prefix(clean_fetch_body(raw).cleaned_text)
2642	        if pref and _header_candidate_is_renderable(pref):
2643	            return _normalize_claim_summary(pref, quote_trim=240)
2644	    # 2. complete-sentence prefix over each member's direct_quote (verified members first).
2645	    #    I-deepfix-001 H3 (#1344): when the default-ON verified-only gate is set, the header claim
2646	    #    sentence may come ONLY from an ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED member — a basket with no verified support
2647	    #    yields no quote-derived header and falls through to the clean source TITLE (neutral). §-1.3
2648	    #    no-source-dropped: every member still renders below; this only restricts the header source.
2649	    members = basket.get("supporting_members") or []
2650	    if cwf_header_verified_only_enabled():
2651	        members = [
2652	            m for m in members
2653	            if str(m.get("member_tier") or "") == "ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED"
2654	        ]
2655	    members = sorted(
2656	        members,
2657	        key=lambda m: 0 if str(m.get("member_tier") or "") == "ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED" else 1,
2658	    )
2659	    for m in members:
2660	        mq = str(m.get("direct_quote") or "")
2661	        if not mq:
2662	            continue
2663	        mp = _complete_sentence_prefix(clean_fetch_body(mq).cleaned_text)
2664	        if mp and _header_candidate_is_renderable(mp):
2665	            return _normalize_claim_summary(mp, quote_trim=240)
2666	    # 3. the clean source TITLE (bibliography statement) — TITLE-lenient screen (a title is a noun
2667	    #    phrase, not a sentence). A trailing trim-marker ("..."/"…") on an already-trimmed title is

2669	    #    (lowercase_with_underscores) / genuinely truncated span still fails the screen and is skipped.

2676	def _placeholder_bib_nums(bibliography: "list[dict]") -> "set[str]":
2677	    """#3: the set of bibliography citation numbers that are CONTRACT-SLOT PLACEHOLDERS, not
2678	    real sources — a row whose ``statement`` is just the ``evidence_id`` slug (e.g.
2679	    "fourth_industrial_revolution_framing"), i.e. a slot that did not bind a real titled source.
2680	    PURE + deterministic. A section whose ONLY citations are placeholders (and whose body is not
2681	    already a gap-disclosure stub) is resting solely on an unbound slot → routed to the gap path.
2682	    This is a PROVENANCE discriminator (slug == slug), not a topic / semantic / magic-number
2683	    heuristic, so it never mis-flags a real section (which cites a real-titled row)."""
2684	    out: set[str] = set()
2685	    for b in bibliography or []:

2687	        stmt = str(b.get("statement") or "").strip()

2707	def _screen_key_findings_chrome(key_findings_md: str) -> str:
2708	    """#2 (render side): drop any Key-Findings BULLET whose lifted span is page-furniture
2709	    chrome (journal masthead / nav / metadata / fetch-framing). build_key_findings lifts the
2710	    first CITED sentence per section; when that sentence is a verbatim chrome span it renders
2711	    as a "- **Key Findings.** onomic Perspectives—Volume 33 ..." masthead bullet. Screens ONLY
2712	    the ``- **...**`` bullet lines; the heading + intro line and any non-bullet content are
2713	    untouched. If every bullet is chrome the whole block is dropped (no empty heading). PURE;
2714	    never drops a real finding (a real finding carries no masthead/nav/metadata token)."""
2715	    if not key_findings_md:
2716	        return key_findings_md
2717	    lines = key_findings_md.split("\n")
2718	    kept: list[str] = []
2719	    bullet_total = 0
2720	    bullet_kept = 0
2721	    for ln in lines:
2722	        if ln.lstrip().startswith("- **"):
2723	            bullet_total += 1
2724	            if _span_is_render_chrome(ln):
2725	                continue  # chrome bullet — drop
2726	            bullet_kept += 1
2727	        kept.append(ln)

2729	    # (heading + intro with zero findings is worse than no block).

2736	# _screen_key_findings_chrome above is a BLIND deterministic-regex pre-filter (the iwire013
2737	# blind-predicate saga); the NEW benchmarked LLM span gate (span_quality_gate.screen_finding_units,
2738	# bake-off F1 0.568, ~2x the best heuristic) is the AUTHORITATIVE second screen that catches the
2739	# glued-mid-prose chrome + cut-word-before-[N] the regex is structurally blind to. It runs on the
2740	# rendered ROLLUP surfaces ONLY (Key-Findings bullets + the Analytical-synthesis depth layer's
2741	# finding lines) — the page-furniture that survived strict_verify (a verbatim chrome span) and
2742	# rendered as a "finding" headline. §-1.3 FLAG-NOT-DROP: a unit the gate flags is_junk=True is
2743	# WITHHELD from the rendered rollup (chrome is not a real finding) but NEVER deleted from the body /
2744	# evidence pool / bibliography — it stays in the full report; only excluded from the summary surface.

2747	# an over-strip. The injected ``screen_fn`` is how the offline test stubs the LLM judge.
2748	_ROLLUP_FINDING_LINE_RE = re.compile(
2749	    r"^\s*-\s+\S"                       # a "- ..." Key-Findings / synthesis bullet
2750	    r"|^\s*\*\*(?:Key Findings|Challenges|Tension)\*\*\s+\S"  # depth-layer per-section finding lines
2751	)

2754	def _is_rollup_finding_line(line: str) -> bool:
2755	    """True iff ``line`` is a candidate FINDING UNIT in a rendered rollup block — a "- ..." bullet
2756	    or a depth-layer ``**Key Findings|Challenges|Tension** <sentence>`` line. The ``##``/``###``
2757	    header and the italic ``_..._`` preamble are NOT finding units and are never screened/withheld.
2758	    PURE."""
2759	    stripped = line.strip()
2760	    if not stripped:
2761	        return False
2762	    if stripped.startswith("#") or stripped.startswith("_"):
2763	        return False
2764	    return bool(_ROLLUP_FINDING_LINE_RE.match(line))

2767	def _screen_rollup_finding_units(block_md: str, *, screen_fn=None) -> str:
2768	    """KEYSTONE authoritative screen over a rendered rollup block (Key-Findings or Analytical
2769	    synthesis). Extracts the candidate FINDING-UNIT lines, runs them through the benchmarked span
2770	    gate (``screen_finding_units``), and WITHHOLDS (drops the line in place) any unit flagged
2771	    is_junk=True — §-1.3 FLAG-NOT-DROP: withheld from the RENDERED rollup only, never from the body
2772	    / evidence. Header + preamble lines pass through untouched. If a block had finding units and ALL
2773	    were withheld, the whole block is dropped (no empty heading — mirrors _screen_key_findings_chrome).
2774	
2775	    The span gate is internally flag-gated (PG_SPAN_QUALITY_GATE) — when OFF it returns every unit
2776	    is_junk=False (zero LLM calls), so this pass is a byte-identical no-op. ``screen_fn`` is injectable
2777	    (defaults to the production span gate) so the offline test stubs the LLM judge. Emits the run-log
2778	    canary with REAL counts (judged / withheld / errors / disabled) so a silent fail-safe (all
2779	    pass-through) is LOUD on the run log — the prove-it-fired surface this keystone exists to guarantee.
2780	    PURE except the lazy span-gate import + the run-log emit."""
2781	    if not block_md or not block_md.strip():
2782	        return block_md
2783	    if screen_fn is None:
2784	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.span_quality_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
2785	            screen_finding_units as screen_fn,
2786	        )
2787	    lines = block_md.split("\n")
2788	    unit_line_indices = [i for i, ln in enumerate(lines) if _is_rollup_finding_line(ln)]
2789	    if not unit_line_indices:
2790	        return block_md  # nothing screenable (e.g. an insufficient-evidence stub) — untouched
2791	    units = [lines[i] for i in unit_line_indices]
2792	    verdicts = screen_fn(units)
2793	    withheld_positions: set[int] = set()
2794	    withheld = 0
2795	    errors = 0
2796	    disabled = 0
2797	    for unit_pos, verdict in zip(unit_line_indices, verdicts):
2798	        src = getattr(verdict, "source", "")
2799	        if src == "error":
2800	            errors += 1
2801	        elif src == "disabled":
2802	            disabled += 1
2803	        if getattr(verdict, "is_junk", False):
2804	            withheld_positions.add(unit_pos)
2805	            withheld += 1
2806	    # Prove-it-fired canary on the RUN log (the span-gate module logs its OWN counts via logging.info;
2807	    # this is the SWEEP-side surface). If the gate is ON but every unit errored / was disabled (the
2808	    # wired-but-dead fail-safe), this line says so LOUDLY. Uses the module logger, NOT the nested run
2809	    # ``_log`` (this is a module-level helper — the caller separately echoes via ``_log`` is unnecessary
2810	    # because logging.basicConfig streams to stdout, the same surface the run log reads).
2811	    logging.getLogger(__name__).info(
2812	        "[span-quality-gate] rollup-screen: units=%d withheld=%d errors=%d disabled=%d",
2813	        len(units), withheld, errors, disabled,
2814	    )
2815	    if not withheld_positions:
2816	        return block_md  # byte-identical when the gate flagged nothing
2817	    kept = [ln for i, ln in enumerate(lines) if i not in withheld_positions]
2818	    # If EVERY finding unit was withheld, drop the now-finding-less block (heading + preamble with
2819	    # zero findings is worse than no block — mirrors _screen_key_findings_chrome).
2820	    if withheld >= len(unit_line_indices):
2821	        return ""
2822	    return "\n".join(kept)

2825	def _screen_offtopic_chrome_sections(
2826	    body_md: str, placeholder_nums: "set[str]"
2827	) -> str:
2828	    """#3 + #2(section bodies): route an OFF-TOPIC / CHROME section through the existing gap
2829	    path by DROPPING it (header + body), leaving the header orphaned so the downstream
2830	    ``dedup_identical_paragraphs`` pass-2 orphan-drop clears it — i.e. the off-topic span
2831	    never renders as a finding while the source is untouched in the bibliography (and its
2832	    real gap, if any, is still disclosed by its own contract-slot section).
2833	
2834	    A ``### Header`` section is dropped when (and only when) it is NOT already a gap-disclosure
2835	    stub AND either:
2836	      (a) every citation in its body points ONLY at placeholder contract-slot rows — an unbound
2837	          slot (the WEF "Five technologies are reshaping water security[7]" Background, whose sole
2838	          cite [7] is the fourth_industrial_revolution_framing slug), OR
2839	      (b) its body, after stripping the header + citation markers, is a SINGLE short span that is
2840	          render-chrome (a masthead/nav fragment standing alone as the section body).
2841	
2842	    Dropping (vs. emitting a stub) avoids fabricating a slug-specific stub and never duplicates an
2843	    existing disclosure. Sections with ANY real (non-placeholder) citation, or a multi-sentence
2844	    body, are left byte-identical. PURE; never drops a SOURCE — only an off-topic/chrome SPAN that
2845	    was never a real finding (the cited row remains in the bibliography)."""
2846	    if not body_md:
2847	        return body_md
2848	    blocks = body_md.split("\n\n")
2849	    out: list[str] = []
2850	    i = 0
2851	    while i < len(blocks):
2852	        block = blocks[i]
2853	        first = block.splitlines()[0] if block.splitlines() else ""
2854	        if not _RENDER_HEADER_RE.match(first.lstrip()):
2855	            out.append(block)
2856	            i += 1
2857	            continue
2858	        # Collect this section's body blocks (until the next header or end).
2859	        body_blocks: list[str] = []
2860	        j = i + 1
2861	        while j < len(blocks):
2862	            nf = blocks[j].splitlines()[0] if blocks[j].splitlines() else ""
2863	            if _RENDER_HEADER_RE.match(nf.lstrip()):
2864	                break
2865	            body_blocks.append(blocks[j])
2866	            j += 1
2867	        body_text = "\n\n".join(b for b in body_blocks if b.strip())
2868	        if not body_text.strip() or _RENDER_GAP_MARKER_RE.search(body_text):
2869	            # empty section, or already a gap stub — leave untouched.
2870	            out.append(block)
2871	            for b in body_blocks:

2873	            i = j
2874	            continue
2875	        cites = set(_RENDER_CITATION_NUM_RE.findall(body_text))
2876	        body_no_cite = _RENDER_CITATION_NUM_RE.sub("", body_text).strip()
2877	        only_placeholder = bool(cites) and cites <= placeholder_nums
2878	        # single-span chrome: <=1 sentence-ending punctuation and render-chrome
2879	        is_short_chrome = (

2881	        )
2882	        if only_placeholder or is_short_chrome:
2883	            # Drop the off-topic/chrome section: emit ONLY the (now orphaned) header, which
2884	            # dedup_identical_paragraphs pass-2 then removes. The body span is dropped.
2885	            out.append(block)
2886	            i = j
2887	            continue
2888	        out.append(block)
2889	        for b in body_blocks:
2890	            out.append(b)
2891	        i = j
2892	    return "\n\n".join(out)

2895	def _strip_dangling_gap_crossref(body_md: str, run_dir: "str | None") -> str:
2896	    """#10: remove the dangling "See manifest.frame_coverage_report and human_gap_tasks.json
2897	    for per-entity detail." cross-ref from the gap-disclosure boilerplate WHEN those files do
2898	    not exist in this run's output directory. The pointer is generated unconditionally by the
2899	    contract-runner but ``human_gap_tasks.json`` / a manifest ``frame_coverage_report`` block
2900	    are not always produced, leaving a broken pointer. Only the dangling SENTENCE is removed;
2901	    the gap disclosure itself ("... curator-actionable gap.") is preserved. If the referenced
2902	    files DO exist, the pointer is left byte-identical. PURE except a guarded os.path.exists."""
2903	    if not body_md or "frame_coverage_report" not in body_md:

2935	#
2936	# The §-1.1 audit of drb_78 found `#`/`##`/`###` header lines whose text is NOT a
2937	# section title but a garbled scraped fragment — a login wall, a URL, or a full
2938	# sentence. The catastrophic one (drb_78 report.md L60):
2939	#
2940	#     # Parkinson's disease affects... - Parkinson's Foundation [Log In](https://www.facebook.com/login/device-based/regular/login/?login_attempt=1&...).[39].[41] It is common ...
2941	#
2942	# i.e. a facebook login wall rendered as an H1 heading, carrying a URL, a
2943	# `[Log In]` CTA, `[N]` citation tokens, and a trailing sentence. A second
2944	# (L128): `## Adverse events The model accounted for both treatment-speci [...]
2945	# associated with increasing postural instability.` — a truncated sentence as a
2946	# header. Once the junk-host SOURCE screen drops the facebook posts these will
2947	# not re-form, but a KEPT real source can still produce a header carrying a `[N]`
2948	# citation / URL / truncation marker, so this is the backstop.
2949	#
2950	# HIGH-PRECISION (the §-1.1/§-1.3 over-strip ban): a header is dropped ONLY when
2951	# it carries an UNAMBIGUOUS chrome/garble signal — a URL (`http`), a login-CTA
2952	# (`[Log In]`/`login_attempt`/`device-based`), a `[N]` citation token, or a
2953	# `[...]` truncation marker. Real section titles in this corpus run to 13 words
2954	# (e.g. "Research Gaps, Policy Implications, and Future Directions for
2955	# Family-Centered PD and DBS Care") and carry NONE of those signals, so WORD
2956	# COUNT is NEVER a standalone drop trigger (it would kill a legitimate long
2957	# title). The `# Research report: <question>` H1 title is EXEMPT — it legitimately
2958	# carries the verbatim research question (often a long, `?`-ending sentence) and
2959	# is structural, not scraped chrome.
2960	#
2961	# FAITHFULNESS: a POST-strict_verify RENDER cleanup. It removes a garbled non-

2963	# drops a real source, and never touches strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-

2969	_HEADER_SANITY_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^(#{1,6})\s+(.+?)\s*$")

2979	)
2980	_HEADER_SANITY_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")

3033	def _contradiction_magnitude_range(contradictions_path: "str | None") -> "tuple[str, str] | None":
3034	    """#5: compute the REAL min/max relative-difference range from the run's
3035	    contradictions.json. Each entry's ``relative_difference`` is a fraction (1.5543 == 155.43%).
3036	    Returns (min_pct_str, max_pct_str) formatted as human percentages, or None when the sidecar
3037	    is missing / has no numeric entries. Reads the ACTUAL entries — never hardcodes. PURE
3038	    except the guarded file read."""
3039	    if not contradictions_path:
3040	        return None
3041	    import os as _os  # noqa: PLC0415
3042	    import json as _json  # noqa: PLC0415
3043	    if not _os.path.exists(contradictions_path):
3044	        return None
3045	    try:
3046	        with open(contradictions_path, encoding="utf-8") as _cf:
3047	            entries = _json.load(_cf)
3048	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — unreadable sidecar => cannot correct, leave prose as-is
3049	        return None
3050	    rels = [
3051	        e.get("relative_difference")
3052	        for e in (entries or [])
3053	        if isinstance(e, dict) and isinstance(e.get("relative_difference"), (int, float))
3054	    ]
3055	    if not rels:
3056	        return None
3057	    return (_format_percent(min(rels) * 100.0), _format_percent(max(rels) * 100.0))

3060	def _format_percent(pct: float) -> str:
3061	    """#5: format a percentage for the Limitations magnitude-range prose. Small values get one
3062	    decimal (33.3%); very large values use a magnitude word (96.9 trillion %) rather than a
3063	    20-digit integer. PURE + deterministic. The huge endpoint is a real divide-by-near-zero
3064	    artifact in the data — we render WHAT THE DATA SAYS, never filter the outlier."""
3065	    if pct < 0:
3066	        pct = abs(pct)
3067	    scales = [
3068	        (1e12, "trillion"),
3069	        (1e9, "billion"),
3070	        (1e6, "million"),
3071	        (1e3, "thousand"),
3072	    ]
3073	    for factor, word in scales:
3074	        if pct >= factor:
3075	            return f"{pct / factor:.1f} {word} %".replace(".0 ", " ")
3076	    if pct >= 100:
3077	        return f"{pct:,.0f}%"
3078	    return f"{pct:.1f}%"

3081	def _correct_contradiction_magnitude_range(body_md: str, contradictions_path: "str | None") -> str:
3082	    """#5: replace the WRONG contradiction-magnitude range in the Limitations prose with the
3083	    REAL min/max computed from contradictions.json. The composer emitted
3084	    "... ranging from 155.4% to over 165 million percent" — 155.4% is merely the FIRST entry
3085	    (not the min) and "165 million percent" is a hallucinated max; ground truth is min 33.3%,
3086	    max ~96.9 trillion %. Targets ONLY the "ranging from X to Y" clause that follows a
3087	    "relative differences" / "disagree on magnitude" anchor, so the adjacent "30 contradictions"
3088	    / "15%" / tier numbers in the same sentence are never touched. No-op (byte-identical) when
3089	    the anchor/clause is absent or the sidecar yields no range. PURE except the guarded read."""
3090	    if not body_md:
3091	        return body_md
3092	    rng = _contradiction_magnitude_range(contradictions_path)
3093	    if rng is None:
3094	        return body_md
3095	    lo, hi = rng
3096	    # Anchor on "relative difference(s) ... ranging from <X> to <Y>" and replace the WHOLE
3097	    # endpoints clause. The clause terminator is an em/en-dash, " though"/" but", a newline, or a
3098	    # period that ENDS the sentence (period + space + capital / end-of-text) — NOT a decimal point
3099	    # inside "155.4%" (so the value clause is captured in full, not truncated at the first ".").
3100	    _term = r"(?:\s*[—–]|\s+though\b|\s+but\b|\n|\.\s+[A-Z]|\.\s*$)"
3101	    pattern = re.compile(
3102	        r"(relative difference[s]?\s+(?:of\s+|by\s+)?ranging\s+from\s+)"
3103	        r"(?:(?!" + _term + r").)+",
3104	        re.IGNORECASE,
3105	    )
3106	    replacement = rf"\g<1>{lo} to {hi}"
3107	    new_body, n = pattern.subn(replacement, body_md, count=1)
3108	    if n:
3109	        return new_body
3110	    # Fallback: anchor on "disagree on magnitude ... ranging from X to Y".
3111	    pattern2 = re.compile(
3112	        r"(disagree[s]?\s+on\s+magnitude[^\n]*?ranging\s+from\s+)"
3113	        r"(?:(?!" + _term + r").)+",
3114	        re.IGNORECASE,
3115	    )
3116	    new_body2, n2 = pattern2.subn(rf"\g<1>{lo} to {hi}", body_md, count=1)
3117	    return new_body2 if n2 else body_md
3118

3183	    source of truth = the member's ``authority_score`` (the canonical per-source credibility the

3186	    low-authority weight and displays as "0.00" — it is NOT treated as absence, so the corroboration

3188	    the SAME number and a tier label can never sit on a divergent credibility_weight. Codex diff-gate
3189	    P1 (#1344): the prior ``> 0.0`` guard wrongly fell back to credibility_weight on a present 0.0,
3190	    re-introducing the two-surface divergence S6 exists to remove. PURE.
3191	
3192	    I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#11): when the caller threads ``weight_by_url`` (the disclosure per-URL
3193	    authority-weight map, ``PG_S11_SEC8_DISCLOSURE_WEIGHT`` default ON) and it carries the member's

3195	    domain-aware ``authority_score`` the ``corpus_credibility_disclosure`` recorded, never the flat
3196	    per-tier prior the credibility-pass fallback stamped on the member (drb_72: §8 showed T1->0.95
3197	    while the disclosure showed 0.4654 for the SAME PMC url). Falls back to the member's own
3198	    authority_score when the URL is absent from the map (byte-identical)."""
3199	    if enabled is None:
3200	        enabled = s6_authority_single_source_enabled()
3201	    # #11: the disclosure per-URL authority weight wins when present (one source of truth with §10 +
3202	    # the sidecar). Guarded so OFF / no-map / URL-not-in-map is byte-identical to the S6 render.
3203	    if weight_by_url and s11_sec8_disclosure_weight_enabled():
3204	        _url = _normalize_disclosure_url(m.get("source_url"))
3205	        if _url:
3206	            _hit = weight_by_url.get(_url)
3207	            if isinstance(_hit, dict) and isinstance(_hit.get("weight"), (int, float)) \
3208	                    and not isinstance(_hit.get("weight"), bool):

3210	    if enabled:
3211	        _a = m.get("authority_score")
3212	        # Present + numeric (including 0.0) => authority_score is the single source of truth.
3213	        if isinstance(_a, (int, float)) and not isinstance(_a, bool):
3214	            return f"{float(_a):.2f}"

3247	    member as verified SUPPORT on its ISOLATED span self-verify alone, even when the binding 4-role
3248	    D8 release verdict settled the member's only claim FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED (byte-identical HEAD)."""
3249	    return os.environ.get("PG_S12_SEC8_D8_DEMOTE", "1").strip().lower() not in (

3254	def _d8_demote_eids_from_verdicts(
3255	    final_verdicts: "dict | None", audit_map: "dict | None",
3256	) -> "set[str]":
3257	    """I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#12): the set of evidence_ids the 4-role D8 gate JUDGED and settled
3258	    non-VERIFIED in EVERY claim they support — i.e. their ONLY claim(s) were FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED,
3259	    never VERIFIED in any claim. These are the members §8 must NOT count as verified support.
3260	
3261	    Join: ``final_verdicts`` (claim_id -> verdict) x ``audit_map`` (claim_id -> {evidence_ids}). An
3262	    evidence_id VERIFIED in ANY D8 claim is load-bearing and is NEVER demoted (fail-open toward
3263	    keeping support). An evidence_id present ONLY in non-VERIFIED D8 claims is demote-eligible. An
3264	    evidence_id the D8 gate never judged (absent from every claim's evidence_ids) is left untouched
3265	    (we demote only on an explicit D8 non-VERIFIED verdict, never on absence). PURE."""
3266	    fv = final_verdicts or {}
3267	    am = audit_map or {}
3268	    if not isinstance(fv, dict) or not isinstance(am, dict):
3269	        return set()
3270	    verified_eids: set[str] = set()
3271	    nonverified_eids: set[str] = set()
3272	    for cid, verdict in fv.items():
3273	        row = am.get(cid) if isinstance(am, dict) else None
3274	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
3275	            continue
3276	        eids = row.get("evidence_ids") or []
3277	        _is_verified = str(verdict).strip().upper() == "VERIFIED"
3278	        for e in eids:
3279	            es = str(e or "")
3280	            if not es:
3281	                continue
3282	            if _is_verified:
3283	                verified_eids.add(es)
3284	            else:
3285	                nonverified_eids.add(es)
3286	    return nonverified_eids - verified_eids

3292	def _apply_corroboration_d8_demotion_post_gate(
3293	    report_path,
3294	    multi,
3295	    final_verdicts: "dict | None",
3296	    audit_map: "dict | None",
3297	    weight_by_url: "dict[str, dict] | None",
3298	    log,
3299	    *,
3300	    journal_preference_active: bool = False,
3301	    protocol: "dict | None" = None,
3302	    document_type_by_url: "dict | None" = None,
3303	) -> None:
3304	    """I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#12): AFTER the binding 4-role D8 gate settles, re-render the §8
3305	    "Source corroboration (per claim)" block with the D8 FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED verdicts propagated,
3306	    and replace it in report.md. §8 is rendered upstream BEFORE D8 runs (the gate reads the finished
3307	    report), so a member whose only claim D8 settled FABRICATED could ship as clean verified SUPPORT;
3308	    this post-gate pass demotes those to GROUNDED-BUT-WEAK (never counted as support, never dropped —
3309	    the source still lives in the numbered Bibliography). Mirrors _apply_source_necessity_post_gate.
3310	
3311	    Faithfulness-STRENGTHENING (only demotes on an explicit D8 non-VERIFIED verdict) and no-drop.
3312	    Fail-open: when nothing is demote-eligible, or the §8 section is not located verbatim, report.md
3313	    is left byte-identical. Default-ON kill-switch (PG_S12_SEC8_D8_DEMOTE)."""
3314	    if not s12_sec8_d8_demote_enabled():
3315	        return
3316	    demote = _d8_demote_eids_from_verdicts(final_verdicts, audit_map)
3317	    if not demote:
3318	        return
3319	    try:
3320	        if report_path is None or not report_path.is_file():
3321	            return
3322	        text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
3323	        start = text.find(_SEC8_HEADER)
3324	        if start < 0:
3325	            return
3326	        # Section runs from the header to the next "## " heading (or EOF).
3327	        nxt = text.find("\n## ", start + len(_SEC8_HEADER))
3328	        end = nxt if nxt >= 0 else len(text)
3329	        bib = getattr(multi, "bibliography", None) or []
3330	        ordered = _corroboration_bib_order(
3331	            bib,
3332	            journal_preference_active=journal_preference_active,
3333	            protocol=protocol,
3334	            document_type_by_url=document_type_by_url,
3335	        )
3336	        rerendered = _basket_corroboration_block(
3337	            ordered, weight_by_url=weight_by_url, d8_demote_eids=demote,
3338	        )
3339	        if not rerendered:
3340	            return
3341	        # _basket_corroboration_block returns "\n\n## Source corroboration ...". Strip the leading
3342	        # blank lines; the located span already begins at the header, and we preserve the exact
3343	        # inter-section spacing that preceded it.
3344	        new_section = rerendered.lstrip("\n")
3345	        new_text = text[:start] + new_section + text[end:]
3346	        if new_text != text:
3347	            report_path.write_text(new_text, encoding="utf-8")
3348	            log(
3349	                f"[sec8-d8-demote] propagated D8 non-VERIFIED verdicts into §8: "
3350	                f"{len(demote)} evidence_id(s) demoted from verified SUPPORT to grounded-but-weak "
3351	                "(no source dropped; still in the numbered Bibliography)"
3352	            )
3353	    except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive disclosure; never abort the report
3354	        log(f"[sec8-d8-demote] post-gate skipped (fail-open): {_exc}")

3357	def _basket_corroboration_block(
3358	    bibliography: "list[dict]",
3359	    *,
3360	    weight_by_url: "dict[str, dict] | None" = None,
3361	    d8_demote_eids: "set[str] | None" = None,
3362	) -> str:
3363	    """I-arch-011 PR-b (#1268): render the Argus keep-all per-claim basket-corroboration
3364	    block from the basket dicts ALREADY projected onto each bibliography row
3365	    (``row["baskets"]`` from ``provenance_generator._basket_for_biblio`` — present only
3366	    when ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` is ON). Pure read; recomputes NO verdict and
3367	    issues NO LLM call.
3368	
3369	    We iterate ``row["baskets"]`` rather than each biblio row because a weak
3370	    DETERMINISTIC_ONLY member is NOT guaranteed to have its own bibliography row (rows
3371	    come from cited tokens + SUPPORTS corroborators only), so a per-row walk could never
3372	    surface it. The same basket is attached to every member's row, so we DEDUP by
3373	    ``claim_cluster_id`` and render each unique basket exactly once.
3374

3376	    ``verified_support_origin_count`` — the I-arch-010 invariant guarantees it counts only
3377	    ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED members, NEVER ``len(supporting_members)``), each member's
3378	    credibility WEIGHT + tier, the SUPPORT label (``member_tier ==
3379	    ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED``), the cluster-level CONTRADICT label
3380	    (``basket_verdict == "contested"`` / ``refuter_cluster_ids`` — there is no per-source
3381	    −1 edge, so contradict stays cluster-level), and — consuming the I-arch-010 seam —
3382	    ``member_tier == DETERMINISTIC_ONLY`` members surfaced LABELED-weak
3383	    (grounded-but-entailment-unverified), DISTINCT from verified support and NEVER counted
3384	    as support. ``member_tier == UNVERIFIED`` (deterministic garbage) members are NOT
3385	    surfaced (the member_tier contract, credibility_pass.py:152 — the members still live
3386	    in the basket upstream; we choose not to render garbage, not to drop a source).
3387	    Returns "" when no basket data is present => caller appends nothing."""
3388	    # I-deepfix-001 F1 (#1344): the per-claim block trusted a pre-bibliography
3389	    # ``verified_support_origin_count`` computed from each member's ISOLATED span_verdict.
3390	    # Chrome self-entails (text == its own span), so a phantom corroborator that earned NO
3391	    # numbered bibliography entry (its span grounded no cited sentence => inline ``[N]`` already
3392	    # denied it) was still counted here, inflating "2 verified independent source(s)" to a
3393	    # chrome x chrome basket. Filter verified members to the set the report ACTUALLY cites (the
3394	    # bibliography arg) and recompute the count from survivors. RENDER/provenance-binding ONLY:
3395	    # strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span-grounding / provenance are untouched; this REDUCES
3396	    # an inflated count (never inflates breadth) and the sources still live in the numbered
3397	    # Bibliography. Default-ON kill-switch (LAW VI) => OFF is byte-identical.
3398	    _biblio_eids = {str(b.get("evidence_id") or "") for b in bibliography if str(b.get("evidence_id") or "")}

3400

3403	            "1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled",
3404	        )
3405	
3406	    def _is_biblio_present(m: dict) -> bool:

3408	            str(m.get("evidence_id") or "") in _biblio_eids

3433	    # byte-identical (legacy distinct-origin count). Faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI /
3434	    # 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED — this only reduces an inflated count.
3435	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.finding_dedup import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3436	        _same_work_key as _same_work_identity_key,
3437	    )

3439	    def _same_work_dedup_enabled() -> bool:

3799	                    f"{str(m.get('source_url') or m.get('evidence_id') or '')} "
3800	                    f"(tier {str(m.get('source_tier') or '')}, weight {_ws})"
3801	                )
3802	            if contested:
3803	                lines.append(
3804	                    "  - CONTRADICTED: this claim has >=1 refuting cluster "
3805	                    "(see contradiction/both-sides block)"
3806	                )
3807	            _block = "\n".join(lines)
3808	            # U18 (#1344): skip a byte-identical duplicate block (render-text-only; §-1.3-safe —
3809	            # see corroboration_block_dedup_enabled). Sources still live in the Bibliography.
3810	            if _dedup_blocks:
3811	                if _block in seen_blocks:
3812	                    continue
3813	                seen_blocks.add(_block)
3814	            blocks.append(_block)
3815	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): the min-cite-set ACTIVATION fire marker — ONE aggregated line per
3816	    # corroboration block. Emitted ONLY when PG_MIN_CITE_SET is ON so OFF is byte-identical (no line).
3817	    # minimized = inline ``[N]`` citations kept; demoted_to_weight = corroborators moved to the weight
3818	    # channel (keep-all, none dropped); build_ok=false surfaces any fail-open minimizer fault. minimized=0
3819	    # with the flag ON (e.g. the layer-2 dependency OFF so the minimizer never ran) is the eligible-yet-zero

3821	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.citation_set_minimizer import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3822	        min_cite_set_enabled as _min_cite_set_enabled_marker,
3823	    )

3827	            _mc_minimized, _mc_demoted, _mc_faults == 0,
3828	        )
3829	    if not blocks:
3830	        return ""
3831	    return (
3832	        "\n\n## Source corroboration (per claim)\n\n"
3833	        "Each verified claim is shown against its WHOLE basket of supporting sources — "
3834	        "the count of independently verified sources, each source's credibility weight, "
3835	        "and support/contradict labels. Grounded-but-weak candidates are disclosed "
3836	        "separately and are NEVER counted as verified support.\n\n"
3837	        + "\n".join(blocks)

3842	def _m2_journal_pref_active(protocol: "dict | None") -> bool:
3843	    """I-deepfix-001 M2: the journal-only document-type-preference double gate, fail-safe.
3844	
3845	    True iff PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT=1 AND the raw scope template declares
3846	    ``document_type_preference: journal_article``. Any import/eval error => False (OFF =>
3847	    byte-identical render)."""
3848	    try:
3849	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3850	            document_type_weighting_active,
3851	        )

3857	# I-deepfix-001 WS-8 (D4): publication-year recency leg for the journal-only genre re-rank. A very old
3858	# (e.g. 1986 pre-AI) source must not HEADLINE a recent-topic review just because its tier is high — but it
3859	# STAYS in the list at a lower rank (WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE, never a drop; §-1.3). Constants are env-overridable
3860	# (LAW VI); the reference year is CORPUS-RELATIVE (the newest source in the same bibliography), so the leg is
3861	# deterministic + testable and never reaches for a wall-clock. Default-ON via PG_M2_RECENCY_RERANK; OFF or an
3862	# unknown/absent year => factor 1.0 => byte-identical ordering (never guess a year, never penalize on missing).
3863	_M2_RECENCY_RERANK_ENV = "PG_M2_RECENCY_RERANK"
3864	_M2_RECENCY_DECAY_PER_YEAR_ENV = "PG_M2_RECENCY_DECAY_PER_YEAR"
3865	_M2_RECENCY_GRACE_YEARS_ENV = "PG_M2_RECENCY_GRACE_YEARS"
3866	_M2_RECENCY_FLOOR_ENV = "PG_M2_RECENCY_FLOOR"
3867	_M2_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:19|20)\d{2}\b")
3868

3870	def _m2_recency_rerank_enabled() -> bool:
3871	    return os.getenv(_M2_RECENCY_RERANK_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
3872

3874	def _m2_publication_year(b: dict) -> "int | None":
3875	    """The row's publication year: an explicit year field first, else the FIRST plausible 4-digit year
3876	    parsed from the statement/title/url/doi. Returns None when none is found (=> no recency penalty —
3877	    never guessed)."""
3878	    for k in ("year", "publication_year", "pub_year"):
3879	        v = b.get(k)
3880	        if v is None:
3881	            continue
3882	        try:
3883	            y = int(str(v).strip()[:4])
3884	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
3885	            continue
3886	        if 1500 <= y <= 2100:
3887	            return y
3888	    for k in ("statement", "title", "url", "doi"):
3889	        m = _M2_YEAR_RE.search(str(b.get(k) or ""))
3890	        if m:
3891	            y = int(m.group(0))
3892	            if 1500 <= y <= 2100:
3893	                return y
3894	    return None

3897	def _m2_recency_factor(b: dict, reference_year: "int | None") -> float:
3898	    """A display re-rank multiplier in [floor, 1.0]: 1.0 for a source within ``grace`` years of the
3899	    corpus-newest source, decaying linearly by ``decay`` per year older, floored at ``floor``. Never 0
3900	    (an old source is DEMOTED, never dropped — §-1.3). Missing/unknown year or flag OFF => 1.0."""
3901	    if reference_year is None or not _m2_recency_rerank_enabled():
3902	        return 1.0
3903	    y = _m2_publication_year(b)
3904	    if y is None:
3905	        return 1.0
3906	    try:
3907	        grace = int(os.getenv(_M2_RECENCY_GRACE_YEARS_ENV, "5"))
3908	        decay = float(os.getenv(_M2_RECENCY_DECAY_PER_YEAR_ENV, "0.02"))
3909	        floor = float(os.getenv(_M2_RECENCY_FLOOR_ENV, "0.25"))
3910	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
3911	        grace, decay, floor = 5, 0.02, 0.25
3912	    age = max(0, int(reference_year) - y - grace)
3913	    return max(floor, 1.0 - decay * age)
3914	
3915	
3916	def _m2_reference_year(bibliography: "list[dict]") -> "int | None":
3917	    """The corpus-relative reference year = the NEWEST publication year across the bibliography rows
3918	    (None when no row carries a parseable year)."""
3919	    years = [y for y in (_m2_publication_year(b) for b in (bibliography or [])) if y is not None]
3920	    return max(years) if years else None

3923	# ── I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#17): bibliography tier/peer-review coherence + URL-slug title guard ───
3924	def _s17_bib_coherence_enabled() -> bool:
3925	    """#17 kill-switch (LAW VI). Default ON; OFF => the genre tag asserts peer-review from the
3926	    document-type classifier ALONE (contradicting the tier) and a URL-slug title renders raw,
3927	    byte-identical to HEAD."""
3928	    return os.environ.get("PG_S17_BIB_COHERENCE", "1").strip().lower() not in (
3929	        "", "0", "false", "no", "off",
3930	    )
3931	
3932	
3933	# POLARIS taxonomy: T1/T2 are the recognized peer-reviewed-journal tiers (the tier classifier
3934	# assigns them from the peer-reviewed-journal host list); T3+ are not peer-reviewed-journal tiers.
3935	_S17_PEER_REVIEWED_TIERS = frozenset({"T1", "T2"})
3936	
3937	
3938	def _s17_tier_is_peer_reviewed(tier: Any) -> bool:
3939	    """True iff ``tier`` is a POLARIS peer-reviewed-journal tier (T1/T2). PURE."""
3940	    return str(tier or "").strip().upper() in _S17_PEER_REVIEWED_TIERS
3941	
3942	
3943	_S17_URL_SLUG_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9]+(?:[-_][a-z0-9]+){2,}(?:\.[a-z0-9]+)?$")
3944	
3945	
3946	def _s17_looks_like_url_slug(text: Any) -> bool:
3947	    """True iff ``text`` is a raw URL slug (e.g. ``ai-and-the-future-of-work``) rather than a real
3948	    title: no whitespace AND 3+ lowercase-alnum tokens joined by '-'/'_'. Conservative — a real
3949	    title carries spaces, so this never clobbers one. PURE."""
3950	    s = str(text or "").strip()
3951	    if not s or " " in s or "\t" in s:
3952	        return False
3953	    return bool(_S17_URL_SLUG_RE.match(s))
3954

3956	def _s17_reconciled_genre_tag(dt_is_peer: bool, dt_value: str, tier: Any) -> str:
3957	    """#17: a genre tag COHERENT with the tier. POLARIS T1/T2 == peer-reviewed-journal tiers. When
3958	    the document-type peer-review flag and the tier AGREE the tag is byte-identical to the pre-fix
3959	    text; when they DISAGREE the tag states BOTH signals honestly instead of a false absolute (a T4
3960	    'peer-reviewed journal article', or a T1 'not peer-reviewed'). PURE."""
3961	    _tier = str(tier or "").strip().upper() or "n/a"
3962	    _tier_peer = _s17_tier_is_peer_reviewed(tier)
3963	    _dt_label = (dt_value or "").lower().replace("_", " ") or "unknown"
3964	    if dt_is_peer and _tier_peer:
3965	        return " — [peer-reviewed journal article]"
3966	    if not dt_is_peer and not _tier_peer:
3967	        return f" — [document type: {_dt_label} — not a peer-reviewed journal article]"
3968	    if dt_is_peer and not _tier_peer:
3969	        # doc-type says journal but the tier is not a peer-reviewed-journal tier — do not assert an
3970	        # unqualified 'peer-reviewed journal article'.
3971	        return (
3972	            f" — [document type: journal article; tier {_tier} — "
3973	            "not classified as a peer-reviewed-journal tier]"
3974	        )
3975	    # not dt_is_peer and _tier_peer: the tier recognizes a peer-reviewed venue the doc-type classifier
3976	    # did not label a journal article.
3977	    return (
3978	        f" — [tier {_tier}: recognized peer-reviewed venue; "
3979	        f"document type: {_dt_label}]"
3980	    )

3983	# ── I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#15): honest "Corroborated" section label vs actual origin count ───────
3984	def _s15_corroborated_label_enabled() -> bool:
3985	    """#15 kill-switch (LAW VI). Default ON; OFF => a "Corroborated Findings" section header stays
3986	    even when its own body is single-origin (or empty), byte-identical to HEAD."""
3987	    return os.environ.get("PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL", "1").strip().lower() not in (
3988	        "", "0", "false", "no", "off",
3989	    )

3993	    r"^(#{1,6})\s*((?:Additional\s+)?Corroborated(?:\s+Weighted)?\s+Findings)\b(.*)$"
3994	)

3996	_S15_ANY_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^(#{1,6})\s")

content.text: Continuing. The pattern is clear: "honest" appears repeatedly in comments and identifiers (`_s15_corroborated_label_enabled` docstring "honest", `PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL`). Let me keep reading the remaining chunks.

content.content:
3997	
3998	
3999	def _relabel_uncorroborated_findings_headers(report_text: str) -> str:
4000	    """#15: relabel a "Corroborated (Weighted) Findings[: facet]" section header when the section's
4001	    own body cites FEWER than 2 distinct origins — the report's reliability header itself states 0
4002	    claims are multi-source corroborated, yet single-origin (or empty) enrichment sections were
4003	    titled "Corroborated". A section body citing 0 distinct ``[N]`` is relabeled "Uncorroborated";
4004	    1 distinct ``[N]`` -> "Single-source"; >=2 distinct -> left unchanged (genuinely multi-cited).
4005	    HEADER-TEXT-ONLY, faithfulness-neutral (no source/claim/verdict touched; every finding + its
4006	    citations stay). Section scope = the header down to the next header of the SAME-or-higher level,
4007	    so a multi-cited parent keeps its label while a single-origin subsection is relabeled. PURE;
4008	    fail-open (returns the input on any error). Default-ON kill-switch."""
4009	    if not _s15_corroborated_label_enabled() or not report_text:
4010	        return report_text
4011	    try:
4012	        lines = report_text.split("\n")
4013	        n = len(lines)
4014	        out = list(lines)
4015	        for i, line in enumerate(lines):
4016	            m = _S15_HEADER_RE.match(line)
4017	            if not m:
4018	                continue
4019	            level = len(m.group(1))
4020	            # Section body = i+1 .. next header of same-or-higher level (or EOF).
4021	            j = i + 1
4022	            while j < n:
4023	                hm = _S15_ANY_HEADER_RE.match(lines[j])
4024	                if hm and len(hm.group(1)) <= level:
4025	                    break
4026	                j += 1
4027	            body = "\n".join(lines[i + 1:j])
4028	            distinct = set(_S15_CITE_RE.findall(body))
4029	            if len(distinct) >= 2:
4030	                continue
4031	            honest = "Single-source" if len(distinct) == 1 else "Uncorroborated"
4032	            # Replace ONLY the leading "Corroborated" label word (keep "Weighted"/"Findings"/facet).
4033	            out[i] = line.replace("Corroborated", honest, 1)
4034	        return "\n".join(out)
4035	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive relabel; never break the render
4036	        return report_text
4037
