4039	def _m2_bib_genre(
4040	    b: dict, *, protocol: "dict | None", document_type_by_url: "dict | None",
4041	    reference_year: "int | None" = None,
4042	):
4043	    """I-deepfix-001 M2: resolve a bibliography row's document GENRE + a DISPLAY-only adjusted
4044	    weight (``tier_prior(tier) * document_type_weight * recency_factor``) for the genre tag +
4045	    corroboration re-rank.
4046	
4047	    Genre resolves from the carried-through url->document_type join when present, else a
4048	    deterministic offline classify from the row's url/title. Pure; no network/LLM. Returns
4049	    ``(DocumentType, adjusted_weight)``. The adjusted weight is a display re-rank multiplier only —
4050	    no source is dropped, every bibliography entry stays in the list. WS-8 (D4): the recency_factor
4051	    (default-ON, corpus-relative) demotes a very-old source for headline ordering; ``reference_year``
4052	    None => factor 1.0 => byte-identical to the pre-WS-8 weight."""
4053	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4054	        DocumentType,
4055	        classify_document_type,
4056	        resolve_document_type_weight,
4057	    )
4058	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.weighted_corpus_gate import _tier_prior  # noqa: PLC0415
4059	
4060	    url = str(b.get("url") or "")
4061	    dt_str = (document_type_by_url or {}).get(url)
4062	    if dt_str and dt_str in DocumentType._value2member_map_:
4063	        dt = DocumentType(dt_str)
4064	    else:
4065	        # I-deepfix-001 A5 (#1344) Codex P1: pass the row's DOI so the journal DOI-prefix
4066	        # allowlist (10.1086 UChicago-Press journals, 10.1126/science… AAAS family, 10.1257
4067	        # AEA, 10.1093/qje…) actually reaches the bibliography/CWF render. Previously a DOI-only
4068	        # row (blank/non-journal url) classified as UNKNOWN → Science/JPE rendered "unknown".
4069	        # HIGH-PRECISION positive evidence only; fail-open (no DOI match => UNKNOWN, never dropped).
4070	        dt, _ = classify_document_type(
4071	            url=url,
4072	            title=str(b.get("statement") or ""),
4073	            doi=str(b.get("doi") or ""),
4074	        )
4075	    w = resolve_document_type_weight(dt, protocol)
4076	    return dt, _tier_prior(str(b.get("tier") or "")) * w * _m2_recency_factor(b, reference_year)

4079	def _render_bibliography_lines(
4080	    bibliography: "list[dict]", *, require_locator: bool, corroboration_render: bool = False,
4081	    journal_preference_active: bool = False, protocol: "dict | None" = None,
4082	    document_type_by_url: "dict | None" = None,
4083	    weight_by_url: "dict[str, dict] | None" = None,
4084	) -> str:
4085	    """#1239: render the report Bibliography. Default (require_locator=False) is byte-identical
4086	    to the prior inline loop. When PG_BIB_REQUIRE_LOCATOR is ON, a CITED entry whose URL AND DOI
4087	    are both blank is NOT emitted as a numbered citation pointing at nothing — it is relabeled to
4088	    a NON-cited evidence gap (`[gap]` prefix, no resolvable locator). This NEVER fabricates a
4089	    locator and NEVER drops the reader's awareness of the entry; it stops a citation marker from
4090	    pointing at an empty URL. The upstream resolution (resolve the locator, or stop assigning a
4091	    citation number to a locator-less row) lives in multi_section_generator.py and is deferred.
4092	
4093	    I-arch-011 PR-b (#1268): when ``corroboration_render`` (PG_BASKET_CORROBORATION_RENDER) is ON,
4094	    a per-claim Argus keep-all source-corroboration block is APPENDED after the bibliography
4095	    lines (count + per-source weights + support/contradict labels + LABELED-weak
4096	    DETERMINISTIC_ONLY candidates). Default-OFF keyword => byte-identical legacy render."""
4097	    out = "\n\n## Bibliography\n"
4098	    for b in bibliography:
4099	        # I-beatboth-011 b2 (#1289): screen the rendered source TITLE/label for crawl chrome BEFORE
4100	        # truncating. In the banked v3 corpora the numbered-entry `statement` was already clean
4101	        # (0/0 chrome across 5 recent bibliographies — the inline reader/journal chrome surfaced in
4102	        # the per-claim corroboration HEADER, fixed above), but the displayed title is render-hygiene-
4103	        # sensitive, so we defensively run the same INPUT-hygiene cleaner (clean_fetch_body applies
4104	        # the proven idx46/68 inline regexes + the Markdown-Content preamble drop). Clean the FULL
4105	        # title first, THEN truncate, so a leading chrome run can't shove the real title past [:200].
4106	        # When the title is WHOLLY chrome (nothing real survives), fall back to the URL, then a
4107	        # domain-derived label, then `source <num>` — NEVER blank, and the citation NUMBER and the
4108	        # bibliography ENTRY itself are untouched, so the body's [N] markers still resolve and no
4109	        # source is ever removed (faithfulness-neutral; §-1.3 no-source-dropped).
4110	        from src.tools.access_bypass import clean_fetch_body  # noqa: PLC0415
4111	        from urllib.parse import urlsplit  # noqa: PLC0415
4112	        _raw_statement = str(b.get("statement", ""))
4113	        url = b.get("url", "")
4114	        tier = b.get("tier", "")
4115	        # Use clean_fetch_body ALONE here (NOT _normalize_claim_summary): clean_fetch_body leaves a real
4116	        # clean title byte-identical, whereas _normalize_claim_summary would also strip a statement that
4117	        # legitimately starts with a number ("1. Introduction ...") or carries a bare URL, clobbering a
4118	        # real title. The numbered-entry statement field is already chrome-free in the banked corpora
4119	        # (0/0), so this is purely defensive render-hygiene; keeping it to clean_fetch_body preserves the
4120	        # #1239 "byte-identical legacy render" invariant for require_locator=False.
4121	        _cleaned_statement = (
4122	            clean_fetch_body(_raw_statement).cleaned_text.strip() if _raw_statement else ""
4123	        )
4124	        # I-beatboth-011 b2 (#1289, P2): also strip a COMPLETE-then-TRUNCATED `![Image...` masthead
4125	        # whose closing `)` was capped off. clean_fetch_body removes complete images but not a capped
4126	        # fragment, so a real title PREFIX + dangling image would otherwise be screened as chrome and
4127	        # fall back to the domain, losing the real title. Complete-image strip runs first (same order
4128	        # as _normalize_claim_summary) so a `![img](url) <real title>` keeps the real title. A clean
4129	        # title carries no `![...` tail, so _destripped == _cleaned_statement and this is byte-identical
4130	        # for clean titles (the #1239 invariant is preserved — only a dangling-image title is altered).
4131	        if _cleaned_statement:
4132	            _destripped = _DANGLING_IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE.sub(
4133	                " ", _IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE.sub(" ", _cleaned_statement)
4134	            )
4135	            if _destripped != _cleaned_statement:
4136	                _cleaned_statement = _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", _destripped).strip()
4137	        # I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#17): a raw URL slug ("ai-and-the-future-of-work") is not a real
4138	        # title — fall back to the honest domain/DOI/`source N` label the same way a chrome title
4139	        # does. Default-ON kill-switch; a real title (which carries spaces) never matches so OFF and
4140	        # the common path are byte-identical.
4141	        _is_slug_title = _s17_bib_coherence_enabled() and _s17_looks_like_url_slug(_cleaned_statement)
4142	        if _cleaned_statement and not _title_has_named_chrome(_cleaned_statement) and not _is_slug_title:
4143	            statement = _cleaned_statement[:200]
4144	        else:
4145	            # Wholly-chrome (or empty, or URL-slug) title: fall back to a non-chrome label so the
4146	            # entry never renders as a blank/chrome/slug title. domain (human-readable) > DOI >
4147	            # `source <num>`.
4148	            _url_s = str(url or "").strip()
4149	            _domain = urlsplit(_url_s).netloc.strip() if _url_s else ""
4150	            _doi = str(b.get("doi") or "").strip()
4151	            statement = (_domain or _doi or f"source {b.get('num', '')}".strip())[:200]
4152	        # I-deepfix-001 M2: append a per-citation document-GENRE tag when the journal-only
4153	        # document-type preference is active (PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT=1 AND the template field).
4154	        # Default-OFF keyword => empty tag => byte-identical legacy render. Pure label; the entry,
4155	        # its citation number, and the source are untouched (WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE, no drop).
4156	        _genre_tag = ""
4157	        if journal_preference_active:
4158	            try:
4159	                from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4160	                    is_peer_reviewed_journal_article,
4161	                )
4162	                _dt, _ = _m2_bib_genre(
4163	                    b, protocol=protocol, document_type_by_url=document_type_by_url
4164	                )
4165	                _dt_peer = is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(_dt)
4166	                # I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#17): reconcile the peer-review assertion with the tier so
4167	                # the bibliography can't show a T4 "peer-reviewed journal article" or a T1 "not
4168	                # peer-reviewed". When tier + doc-type AGREE the tag is byte-identical to the pre-fix
4169	                # text; only the contradictory rows change. OFF => the pre-fix doc-type-only tag.
4170	                if _s17_bib_coherence_enabled():
4171	                    _genre_tag = _s17_reconciled_genre_tag(_dt_peer, _dt.value, tier)
4172	                elif _dt_peer:
4173	                    _genre_tag = " — [peer-reviewed journal article]"
4174	                else:
4175	                    _genre_tag = (
4176	                        f" — [document type: {_dt.value.lower().replace('_', ' ')} "
4177	                        "— not a peer-reviewed journal article]"
4178	                    )

4185	            # [num] AND honestly disclose the missing locator. _bib_entry_has_locator already ruled
4186	            # out a non-blank URL or DOI, so there is genuinely no resolvable locator to show.

4194	            # (https://doi.org/<doi>) instead of an empty string. Never fabricates — only uses a

4230	# ── T2 (I-deepfix-001 #1344) cited reference list vs corpus-ledger audit appendix ─────────────────
4231	# DeepTRACE uncited-sources #4 + source-necessity #6: the rendered "## Bibliography" conflated the
4232	# CITED reference set with the full corpus credibility ledger, so every listed-but-uncited corpus row
4233	# read as padding (worst-in-class #4/#6). T2 TYPES the two sets: the reference list is the sources the
4234	# report actually cites (a body ``[N]`` marker resolves to them); every remaining corpus row moves to
4235	# a clearly-typed "corpus ledger" AUDIT APPENDIX. NOTHING is dropped — every source still ships in
4236	# report.md, fully disclosed, just typed as a non-reference audit row. Because the cited set is
4237	# computed from the WHOLE assembled report (body citations only, EXCLUDING the bibliography's own
4238	# entry lines), a genuinely-cited source can NEVER be demoted to the ledger (no coverage loss). PURE
4239	# string surgery on already-rendered lines — re-renders no row, invents/deletes no citation, touches
4240	# no faithfulness verdict. LAW VI kill-switch (default ON); OFF => the single conflated Bibliography,
4241	# byte-identical to pre-fix.
4242	_CITED_REFERENCE_TYPING_ENV = "PG_CITED_REFERENCE_TYPING"
4243	_BIB_ENTRY_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^\[(\d+)\]\s")
4244	_INLINE_CITE_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
4245	_CORPUS_LEDGER_HEADER = "## Corpus ledger (audit appendix — not cited references)"
4246	
4247	
4248	def _cited_reference_typing_enabled() -> bool:
4249	    """T2 kill-switch. Default ON; OFF => the conflated single Bibliography (pre-fix)."""
4250	    return os.environ.get(_CITED_REFERENCE_TYPING_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in (
4251	        "", "0", "false", "no", "off",
4252	    )

4254	
4255	def cited_reference_numbers(report_text: str) -> "set[int]":
4256	    """The set of bibliography ``[N]`` numbers the report BODY actually cites. PURE.
4257	
4258	    A body citation is any ``[N]`` that is NOT the leading marker of a bibliography ENTRY line
4259	    (``^[N] ...``) — those entry lines are the reference list itself, not a citation OF a source.
4260	    Computing the cited set from the whole report MINUS the entry lines guarantees a source cited
4261	    anywhere in the body (key findings / sections / depth / abstract / conclusion / disclosures) is
4262	    counted, so T2 can never demote a genuinely-cited source to the ledger."""
4263	    cited: set[int] = set()
4264	    for line in (report_text or "").split("\n"):
4265	        if _BIB_ENTRY_LINE_RE.match(line):
4266	            continue  # a reference-list entry line, not a body citation
4267	        for m in _INLINE_CITE_MARKER_RE.finditer(line):
4268	            try:
4269	                cited.add(int(m.group(1)))
4270	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
4271	                continue
4272	    return cited
4273

4275	def s2_cited_bibliography_records(
4276	    bibliography: "list[dict] | None", report_body_text: str
4277	) -> "list[dict]":
4278	    """S2 (I-deepfix-001 #1344): the bibliography rows the report BODY actually CITES (``[N]`` present
4279	    in the body), for the required-entity citation-coverage credit. PURE.
4280	
4281	    Codex diff-gate P1 (#1344): the credit must come ONLY from cited evidence of the report's VERIFIED
4282	    claims, NOT from every ``multi.bibliography`` row. The full bibliography can carry retrieved-but-
4283	    UNCITED corpus-ledger rows (T2 types them into a non-reference appendix); an uncited row whose URL
4284	    happens to match a required entity's DOI/url_pattern would then FALSELY mark that entity covered and
4285	    SUPPRESS a real Coverage-gaps disclosure — over-claiming completeness, the lethal direction. Keying
4286	    on ``num in cited_reference_numbers(body)`` restricts the credit to genuinely-cited sources (the
4287	    body ``[N]`` markers live in strict_verify-PASSED prose). When the body is empty/unreadable this
4288	    returns [] — a fail-safe UNDER-credit (disclose the gap) that never suppresses a gap on missing input."""
4289	    cited_nums = cited_reference_numbers(report_body_text)
4290	    if not cited_nums:
4291	        return []
4292	    out: list[dict] = []
4293	    for b in bibliography or []:
4294	        if not isinstance(b, dict):
4295	            continue
4296	        n = b.get("num")
4297	        if isinstance(n, int) and not isinstance(n, bool) and n in cited_nums:
4298	            out.append(b)
4299	    return out

4302	def split_bibliography_section_by_citation(
4303	    biblio_section_text: str, cited_nums: "set[int]"
4304	) -> str:
4305	    """T2: retype an already-rendered "## Bibliography" block into a CITED reference list plus a typed
4306	    corpus-ledger AUDIT APPENDIX. PURE (no I/O) so a behaviour test can assert the split directly.
4307	
4308	    An entry line (``^[N] ...``) whose N is in ``cited_nums`` stays under "## Bibliography"; an entry
4309	    line whose N is uncited moves VERBATIM under the corpus-ledger appendix header. Non-entry content
4310	    (the section header, any appended per-claim corroboration block, blank lines) stays with the cited
4311	    reference list. Every entry is preserved exactly once — nothing is dropped or re-rendered. Returns
4312	    the retyped block; if there is nothing to move (all entries cited, or no entries) returns the input
4313	    unchanged (byte-identical)."""
4314	    lines = (biblio_section_text or "").split("\n")
4315	    kept: list[str] = []
4316	    ledger: list[str] = []
4317	    for line in lines:
4318	        m = _BIB_ENTRY_LINE_RE.match(line)
4319	        if m and int(m.group(1)) not in cited_nums:
4320	            ledger.append(line)
4321	        else:
4322	            kept.append(line)
4323	    if not ledger:
4324	        return biblio_section_text  # every entry is cited => byte-identical
4325	    kept_block = "\n".join(kept).rstrip()
4326	    ledger_block = "\n".join(ledger).strip()
4327	    return (
4328	        kept_block
4329	        + "\n\n"
4330	        + _CORPUS_LEDGER_HEADER
4331	        + "\n\n"
4332	        + "_These corpus sources were retrieved and credibility-weighted but are NOT cited by any "
4333	        "report claim. They are disclosed here for audit completeness and are not part of the cited "
4334	        "reference list._\n\n"
4335	        + ledger_block
4336	        + "\n"
4337	    )
4338

4340	def _extract_bibliography_block(report_text: str) -> "tuple[str, int, int] | None":
4341	    """Locate the rendered "## Bibliography" section in a finished report.md. PURE.
4342	
4343	    Returns ``(block_text, start_index, end_index)`` where ``report_text[start:end] == block_text``,
4344	    OR ``None`` when the report carries no "## Bibliography" heading. The block runs from the
4345	    heading line up to (but NOT including) the NEXT top-level markdown heading ("## " or "# " at
4346	    line start) — so any per-claim corroboration content appended under the heading is captured,
4347	    while the following section (e.g. the T2 corpus-ledger appendix or the Conclusion) is not.
4348	    Used by the post-gate source-necessity seam (finding #7) to re-type the bibliography against the
4349	    four-role D8 verdicts without re-parsing the whole report."""
4350	    if not report_text:
4351	        return None
4352	    marker = "## Bibliography"
4353	    start = report_text.find("\n" + marker)
4354	    if start >= 0:
4355	        start += 1  # step over the leading newline so the block starts at the heading
4356	    elif report_text.startswith(marker):
4357	        start = 0
4358	    else:
4359	        return None
4360	    # End = the next top-level heading line after the heading line, else EOF.
4361	    scan = report_text.find("\n", start)
4362	    end = len(report_text)
4363	    while scan != -1:
4364	        nxt = report_text.find("\n", scan + 1)
4365	        line_end = nxt if nxt != -1 else len(report_text)
4366	        line = report_text[scan + 1:line_end]
4367	        if line.startswith("## ") or line.startswith("# "):
4368	            end = scan + 1
4369	            break
4370	        scan = nxt
4371	    return report_text[start:end], start, end

4374	def _apply_source_necessity_post_gate(
4375	    report_path,
4376	    multi,
4377	    final_verdicts: "dict | None",
4378	    audit_map: "dict | None",
4379	    log,
4380	) -> None:
4381	    """I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 (#1344, finding #7): apply the DeepTRACE-#6 source-necessity re-type to
4382	    the FINISHED report.md, reconciled against the four-role D8 SETTLED verdicts.
4383	
4384	    Runs AFTER the four-role gate so a body-cited source that D8 settled VERIFIED is credited as
4385	    load-bearing even when its isolated-span basket is span-unverified — it is NEVER quarantined out
4386	    of the numbered bibliography (no dangling [N] marker) and it COUNTS toward the necessity ratio.
4387	    The D8-VERIFIED-cited allowlist is derived from ``final_verdicts`` (claim_id -> verdict) joined to
4388	    ``audit_map`` (claim_id -> evidence_ids) via the bibliography's evidence_id->num map. Faithfulness-
4389	    neutral (pure disclosure/render surgery; reads already-settled verdicts, never re-verifies). Fail-
4390	    open + kill-switch (``PG_SOURCE_NECESSITY_QUARANTINE``); any error leaves report.md untouched."""
4391	    try:
4392	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.source_necessity import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4393	            quarantine_enabled as _sn_enabled,
4394	            compute_source_necessity as _sn_compute,
4395	            zero_support_bib_nums as _sn_zero,
4396	            retype_bibliography_by_source_necessity as _sn_retype,
4397	        )
4398	        if not _sn_enabled():
4399	            return
4400	        if report_path is None or not report_path.is_file():
4401	            return
4402	        report_text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
4403	        extracted = _extract_bibliography_block(report_text)
4404	        if extracted is None:
4405	            return  # no bibliography (e.g. a degraded/withheld body) — nothing to re-type
4406	        biblio_block, _b_start, _b_end = extracted
4407	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4408	            _basket_for_biblio as _sn_proj,
4409	            build_basket_supports_by_cluster as _sn_supports_by_cluster,
4410	        )
4411	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.depth_synthesis import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4412	            bib_num_by_evidence_id as _sn_eid_to_num,
4413	        )
4414	        _sn_cred = getattr(multi, "credibility_analysis", None)
4415	        _sn_baskets = getattr(_sn_cred, "baskets", None) or []
4416	        _sn_bib = getattr(multi, "bibliography", None) or []
4417	        _sn_num_by_eid = _sn_eid_to_num(_sn_bib)
4418	        _sn_cited_nums = cited_reference_numbers(report_text)
4419	        # num -> {claim_cluster_id (statement) it span-verified SUPPORTS} (isolated-span basket layer).
4420	        _sn_by_cluster = {}
4421	        for _sn_b in _sn_baskets:
4422	            _sn_ccid = str(getattr(_sn_b, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
4423	            if _sn_ccid:
4424	                _sn_by_cluster[_sn_ccid] = _sn_proj(_sn_b)
4425	        _sn_supports = _sn_supports_by_cluster(_sn_by_cluster)
4426	        _sn_support_by_num: dict = {}
4427	        for _sn_ccid, _sn_eids in _sn_supports.items():
4428	            for _sn_eid in _sn_eids:
4429	                _sn_n = _sn_num_by_eid.get(str(_sn_eid))
4430	                if isinstance(_sn_n, int):
4431	                    _sn_support_by_num.setdefault(_sn_n, set()).add(_sn_ccid)
4432	        # D8-VERIFIED-cited allowlist (finding #7): the numbers cited-in-body by a claim the four-role
4433	        # engine SETTLED VERIFIED. Join final_verdicts (claim_id -> verdict) to audit_map (claim_id ->
4434	        # evidence_ids), map each evidence_id to its bib num, keep those that are actually cited.
4435	        _sn_d8_cited: set = set()
4436	        _fv = final_verdicts or {}
4437	        _am = audit_map or {}
4438	        for _cid, _verdict in _fv.items():
4439	            if str(_verdict).strip().upper() != "VERIFIED":
4440	                continue
4441	            _row = _am.get(_cid) if isinstance(_am, dict) else None
4442	            if not isinstance(_row, dict):
4443	                continue
4444	            for _eid in _row.get("evidence_ids", []) or []:
4445	                _num = _sn_num_by_eid.get(str(_eid))
4446	                if isinstance(_num, int) and _num in _sn_cited_nums:
4447	                    _sn_d8_cited.add(_num)
4448	        _sn_listed = sorted(_sn_cited_nums)
4449	        _sn_support_by_src = {
4450	            n: sorted(_sn_support_by_num.get(n, set())) for n in _sn_listed
4451	        }
4452	        _sn_necessity = _sn_compute(_sn_support_by_src, _sn_listed, _sn_d8_cited)
4453	        # I-wire-001 F1 (dangling-citation prune): protect EVERY number whose [N] appears in the
4454	        # rendered body (``_sn_cited_nums`` is exactly that set) from quarantine — a body-cited
4455	        # source's numbered bibliography entry MUST stay resolvable, so it is never moved to the
4456	        # audit ledger (the drb_72 defect: body cites [85]/[104]/[110] but the bibliography skips
4457	        # them). The necessity ratio is still computed + DISCLOSED honestly (weight-and-disclose,
4458	        # never a dangling drop); only the physical MOVE of a body-cited entry is suppressed.
4459	        _sn_zero_nums = _sn_zero(
4460	            _sn_support_by_num, _sn_cited_nums, _sn_d8_cited, body_cited_nums=_sn_cited_nums
4461	        )
4462	        _sn_retyped = _sn_retype(
4463	            biblio_block, _sn_zero_nums, _sn_necessity, _sn_d8_cited,
4464	            body_cited_nums=_sn_cited_nums,
4465	        )
4466	        if _sn_retyped != biblio_block:
4467	            new_report = report_text[:_b_start] + _sn_retyped + report_text[_b_end:]
4468	            report_path.write_text(new_report, encoding="utf-8")
4469	            log(
4470	                "[source-necessity] "
4471	                f"{_sn_necessity.necessary_sources}/{_sn_necessity.listed_sources} listed "
4472	                f"references necessary (ratio {_sn_necessity.necessity_ratio:.4f}); "
4473	                f"quarantined {len(_sn_zero_nums)} zero-support cited entries to audit ledger "
4474	                f"({len(_sn_d8_cited)} D8-VERIFIED cited refs protected; no source dropped)"
4475	            )
4476	    except Exception as _t3_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive disclosure; never abort the report
4477	        log(f"[source-necessity] post-gate seam skipped (fail-open): {_t3_exc}")

4488	def _cwf_disclosed_block(
4489	    disclosed: "list[dict] | None",
4490	    *,
4491	    cited_urls: "set[str] | None" = None,
4492	    cited_eids: "set[str] | None" = None,
4493	    weight_by_url: "dict[str, dict] | None" = None,
4494	) -> str:
4495	    """I-deepfix-001 (#1344 M5): render the PROMOTION-ELIGIBILITY disclosed-only surface.
4496	
4497	    The unbound-SUPPORTS enrichment partitions its span-verified members into PROMOTED (each
4498	    earns a standalone numbered finding) vs DISCLOSED-ONLY (kept + disclosed, never a standalone
4499	    cited claim) — a low-weight, single-origin, non-journal source no longer anchors a
4500	    top-level finding (drb_72's cognifit / inboundlogistics / procom / protolabs / wsu-blog /
4501	    IZA-WP / predatory-DOI / off-topic-DOI). A demoted source still lives in ``evidence_pool`` AND
4502	    ``corpus_credibility_disclosure.json``; it left the per-claim corroboration block only because
4503	    it is no longer cited, so this dedicated block re-surfaces it and NET disclosure is preserved.
4504	
4505	    §-1.3: this is keep-and-DISCLOSE, never a drop. Pure read of ``multi.cwf_disclosed_sources``
4506	    (no LLM, no verdict, no faithfulness gate). Returns "" when nothing was demoted (the caller
4507	    appends nothing => byte-identical).
4508	
4509	    I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#18): the §10 list CONTRADICTED §8 — it named sources that §8 cites as
4510	    numbered verified SUPPORT (stlouisfed [9], library.kab [8]) as "did not EARN a top-level cited
4511	    claim", AND it called T1/T3 sources "near-zero credibility" while showing a column that was not
4512	    the named credibility weight. When ``PG_S18_LOWWEIGHT_RECONCILE`` is ON (default) the caller
4513	    threads the CITED numbered-reference identities (``cited_urls`` / ``cited_eids``) so an
4514	    already-cited source is EXCLUDED from this low-weight list (it cannot be both "cited support"
4515	    and "did not earn a cited claim"), and the disclosure per-URL authority weight
4516	    (``weight_by_url``) so the shown column is the SAME authority weight §8/the sidecar surface,
4517	    labeled honestly. RENDER-TEXT-ONLY, faithfulness-neutral; no source is dropped from the
4518	    evidence pool or the corpus credibility disclosure — an EXCLUDED source is simply not
4519	    double-listed here because it already appears as a numbered reference. OFF => byte-identical."""
4520	    rows = [d for d in (disclosed or []) if d.get("source_url") or d.get("evidence_id")]
4521	    _reconcile = _s18_lowweight_reconcile_enabled()
4522	    if _reconcile:
4523	        # Exclude any source that IS an already-cited numbered reference (§8/bibliography). A source
4524	        # cited as verified SUPPORT must NOT also read as "did not EARN a top-level cited claim".
4525	        _cu = {_normalize_disclosure_url(u) for u in (cited_urls or set()) if u}
4526	        _ce = {str(e) for e in (cited_eids or set()) if e}
4527	        rows = [
4528	            d for d in rows
4529	            if _normalize_disclosure_url(d.get("source_url")) not in _cu
4530	            and str(d.get("evidence_id") or "") not in _ce
4531	        ]
4532	    if not rows:
4533	        return ""
4534	    _wbu = weight_by_url or {}
4535	    lines = []
4536	    for d in rows:
4537	        # #18: prefer the disclosure per-URL authority weight (the single source of truth §8 + the
4538	        # sidecar surface) so the shown weight is the NAMED authority weight, not a divergent score.
4539	        _disc = _wbu.get(_normalize_disclosure_url(d.get("source_url"))) if _reconcile else None
4540	        if isinstance(_disc, dict) and isinstance(_disc.get("weight"), (int, float)):
4541	            ws = f"{float(_disc['weight']):.2f}"
4542	        else:
4543	            w = d.get("credibility_weight")
4544	            ws = f"{float(w):.2f}" if isinstance(w, (int, float)) and not isinstance(w, bool) else "n/a"
4545	        tier = d.get("source_tier") or "n/a"
4546	        label = d.get("source_url") or d.get("evidence_id")
4547	        _wlabel = "authority weight" if _reconcile else "weight"
4548	        lines.append(f"- {label} (tier {tier}, {_wlabel} {ws})")
4549	    if _reconcile:
4550	        _prose = (
4551	            f"{len(rows)} on-topic source(s) were KEPT in the corpus and disclosure but NOT "
4552	            "promoted to a standalone numbered finding: each is single-origin (uncorroborated) and "
4553	            "was not promoted to a top-level cited claim. The authority weight shown is the same "
4554	            "per-source weight surfaced in the corpus credibility disclosure (its tier is shown "
4555	            "alongside). They remain in the evidence pool and the corpus credibility disclosure; "
4556	            "sources already cited as numbered references are listed there, not here.\n\n"
4557	        )
4558	    else:
4559	        _prose = (
4560	            f"{len(rows)} on-topic source(s) were KEPT in the corpus and disclosure but NOT "
4561	            "promoted to a standalone numbered finding: each is single-origin (uncorroborated), "
4562	            "carries near-zero credibility weight, and is not a recognized journal venue. They "
4563	            "remain in the evidence pool and the corpus credibility disclosure; they did not EARN "
4564	            "a top-level cited claim.\n\n"
4565	        )
4566	    return (
4567	        "\n\n## Disclosed single-origin low-weight sources\n\n"
4568	        + _prose
4569	        + "\n".join(lines)
4570	        + "\n"
4571	    )

4574	def _normalize_claim_summary(text: str, *, quote_trim: int) -> str:
4575	    """BB5-P02: reduce a scraped row's raw text to a short, trimmed claim summary.
4576	
4577	    Strips numbered bibliographies, figure/image markdown, markdown links (keeps
4578	    the visible label), and bare URLs, collapses whitespace, then trims to
4579	    ``quote_trim`` chars (adding an ellipsis when truncated). Pure string op."""
4580	    if not text:
4581	        return ""
4582	    # I-beatboth-011 b2 (#1289): strip COMPLETE inline images FIRST, THEN a TRUNCATED/dangling
4583	    # `![...` masthead whose closing `)` was capped off (the complete-image regex cannot match a
4584	    # fragment). Order matters: running the end-anchored dangling regex first would eat a complete
4585	    # `![img](url) <real claim>` all the way to EOL, dropping the real claim (prior gate P1). With
4586	    # complete images removed first, any remaining `![...$` is genuinely unclosed, so the real claim
4587	    # before/around a complete image is preserved.
4588	    cleaned = _IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE.sub(" ", text)
4589	    cleaned = _DANGLING_IMAGE_MARKDOWN_RE.sub(" ", cleaned)
4590	    cleaned = _LINK_MARKDOWN_RE.sub(r"\1", cleaned)
4591	    cleaned = _BIBLIO_LINE_RE.sub(" ", cleaned)
4592	    cleaned = _BARE_URL_RE.sub(" ", cleaned)
4593	    cleaned = _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", cleaned).strip()
4594	    if quote_trim > 0 and len(cleaned) > quote_trim:
4595	        # I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 1: back the cosmetic trim up to the last WORD boundary so the "…"
4596	        # never manufactures a mid-word cut ("...indeed su…" -> "...indeed…"). Only falls back to a
4597	        # hard cut when there is no space in the back half (a single very long token).
4598	        cut = cleaned[:quote_trim].rstrip()
4599	        sp = cut.rfind(" ")
4600	        if sp > quote_trim // 2:
4601	            cut = cut[:sp].rstrip()
4602	        cleaned = cut + "…"
4603	    return cleaned

4606	def render_qualitative_disclosure(
4607	    qualitative_records: list,
4608	    *,
4609	    is_clinical: bool,
4610	    review_inline: bool | None = None,
4611	    sidecar_filename: str = _QUAL_SIDECAR_FILENAME,
4612	) -> str:
4613	    """BB5-P01: render the qualitative present-vs-absent safety-conflict block.
4614	
4615	    - Gates the clinical-safety detector output to the clinical domain: on a
4616	      non-clinical question the detector mis-fires (drb_90 ADAS "drug_interaction"
4617	      rows; drb_72 labor eligibility flags), so this returns "" for non-clinical.
4618	    - Collapses identical (subject, predicate, status-signature) rows: drb_75's
4619	      246 lines were duplicate rows.
4620	    - Hard CONFLICT rows (severity high/medium) render inline (auto-fired,
4621	      adjudicated). REVIEW flags collapse to a one-line count + sidecar pointer
4622	      unless ``review_inline`` (env ``PG_SWEEP_QUAL_REVIEW_INLINE``) is ON.
4623	
4624	    Pure: takes the records list + the clinical flag; no I/O. Returns the markdown
4625	    block (possibly "") appended to the report Methods. Qualitative records are
4626	    NOT PT08-gated, so dropping/collapsing review rows is faithfulness-safe."""
4627	    if not qualitative_records or not is_clinical:
4628	        return ""
4629	    if review_inline is None:
4630	        review_inline = _env_flag(_QUAL_REVIEW_INLINE_ENV, default=False)
4631	
4632	    hard = [r for r in qualitative_records if getattr(r, "severity", "") in ("high", "medium")]
4633	    review = [r for r in qualitative_records if getattr(r, "severity", "") == "review"]
4634	
4635	    def _status_signature(record) -> tuple:
4636	        # BB5-P01 iter-2 (#1179, Codex P2): dedup on (subject, predicate, status)
4637	        # EXCLUDING evidence_id. drb_75's 246 duplicate rows shared
4638	        # (subject, predicate, assertion_status) but cited different evidence ids,
4639	        # so keying on evidence_id missed them. Qualitative records are NOT
4640	        # PT08-gated (see the module docstring + the gate call at line ~633), so
4641	        # collapsing rows that differ only by which evidence id they cite — for the
4642	        # hard-CONFLICT path as well as the [REVIEW] path — is faithfulness-safe.
4643	        statuses = tuple(
4644	            cl.get("assertion_status", "?")
4645	            for cl in getattr(record, "claims", []) or []
4646	        )
4647	        return (getattr(record, "subject", ""), getattr(record, "predicate", ""), statuses)
4648	
4649	    def _dedup(records: list) -> list:
4650	        seen: set = set()
4651	        out: list = []
4652	        for record in records:
4653	            sig = _status_signature(record)
4654	            if sig in seen:
4655	                continue
4656	            seen.add(sig)
4657	            out.append(record)
4658	        return out
4659	
4660	    hard = _dedup(hard)
4661	    review = _dedup(review)
4662	
4663	    if not hard and not review:
4664	        return ""
4665	
4666	    out = (
4667	        f"\n## Qualitative safety-conflict disclosures\n"
4668	        f"The qualitative detector flagged {len(hard)} present-vs-absent "
4669	        f"clinical-safety conflict(s) (contraindication / drug-interaction / "
4670	        f"eligibility / warning / adverse-event causation) and {len(review)} "
4671	        f"review-flagged item(s) requiring human adjudication. Status is shown as "
4672	        f"asserted PRESENT/ABSENT/INDETERMINATE, not a numeric value; review flags "
4673	        f"are NOT adjudicated conflicts.\n\n"
4674	    )
4675	    for r in hard:
4676	        statuses = " vs ".join(
4677	            f"{cl.get('assertion_status', '?')} "
4678	            f"[ev={cl.get('evidence_id', '')}, tier={cl.get('source_tier', '')}]"
4679	            for cl in getattr(r, "claims", []) or []
4680	        )
4681	        out += (
4682	            f"- [CONFLICT] {getattr(r, 'subject', '')} / "
4683	            f"{getattr(r, 'predicate', '')}: {statuses} — "
4684	            f"{getattr(r, 'conflict_reason', '')}\n"
4685	        )
4686	    if review:
4687	        if review_inline:
4688	            for r in review:
4689	                statuses = " vs ".join(
4690	                    f"{cl.get('assertion_status', '?')} "
4691	                    f"[ev={cl.get('evidence_id', '')}, tier={cl.get('source_tier', '')}]"
4692	                    for cl in getattr(r, "claims", []) or []
4693	                )
4694	                out += (
4695	                    f"- [REVIEW] {getattr(r, 'subject', '')} / "
4696	                    f"{getattr(r, 'predicate', '')}: {statuses} — "
4697	                    f"{getattr(r, 'conflict_reason', '')}\n"
4698	                )
4699	        else:
4700	            out += (
4701	                f"- {len(review)} review-flagged item(s) collapsed to keep the "
4702	                f"report readable; full per-flag rows are in the `{sidecar_filename}` "
4703	                f"sidecar (filter `type=\"qualitative\"`, `severity=\"review\"`).\n"
4704	            )
4705	    return out

4708	def qualitative_records_disclosed_for_pt08(
4709	    qualitative_records: list,
4710	    *,
4711	    is_clinical: bool,
4712	    review_inline: bool | None = None,
4713	) -> list:
4714	    """I-arch-004 F08 (#1249): the EXACT subset of qualitative records whose
4715	    subject+predicate ``render_qualitative_disclosure`` writes into the report.
4716	
4717	    PT08 (run_external_evaluation) holds release iff every contradiction record it
4718	    receives has its ``subject`` AND ``predicate`` substring present in report_text.
4719	    A high present-vs-absent qualitative conflict (e.g. a contraindication asserted
4720	    by one source, denied by another) was DETECTED but never reached PT08, so it
4721	    could never block release — a clinical-safety hole. This returns the SAME records
4722	    the renderer renders inline (so PT08 gates a real undisclosed conflict, never
4723	    false-fails on a record that was intentionally collapsed):
4724	
4725	    * empty / non-clinical -> [] (the renderer returns "" -> nothing in the report,
4726	      so PT08 must not demand a disclosure that does not exist);
4727	    * hard CONFLICT rows (severity high/medium) are ALWAYS rendered -> always eligible;
4728	    * REVIEW flags are rendered inline only when ``PG_SWEEP_QUAL_REVIEW_INLINE`` is on
4729	      (otherwise collapsed to a count) -> eligible only in that case.
4730	
4731	    This STRENGTHENS the faithfulness gate (adds a release-block for a previously
4732	    ungated conflict class). It never relaxes any check: a record that is NOT rendered
4733	    is excluded, so PT08 never fails for a disclosure the report legitimately omits.
4734	    Mirrors the dedup + gating in ``render_qualitative_disclosure`` exactly."""
4735	    if not qualitative_records or not is_clinical:
4736	        return []
4737	    if review_inline is None:
4738	        review_inline = _env_flag(_QUAL_REVIEW_INLINE_ENV, default=False)
4739	
4740	    hard = [r for r in qualitative_records if getattr(r, "severity", "") in ("high", "medium")]
4741	    review = [r for r in qualitative_records if getattr(r, "severity", "") == "review"]
4742	
4743	    def _status_signature(record) -> tuple:
4744	        statuses = tuple(
4745	            cl.get("assertion_status", "?")
4746	            for cl in getattr(record, "claims", []) or []
4747	        )
4748	        return (getattr(record, "subject", ""), getattr(record, "predicate", ""), statuses)
4749	
4750	    def _dedup(records: list) -> list:
4751	        seen: set = set()
4752	        out: list = []
4753	        for record in records:
4754	            sig = _status_signature(record)
4755	            if sig in seen:
4756	                continue
4757	            seen.add(sig)
4758	            out.append(record)
4759	        return out
4760	
4761	    eligible = _dedup(hard)
4762	    if review_inline:
4763	        eligible += _dedup(review)

4767	def render_semantic_disclosure(
4768	    semantic_records: list,
4769	    *,
4770	    inline_cap: int | None = None,
4771	    quote_trim: int | None = None,
4772	    sidecar_filename: str = _QUAL_SIDECAR_FILENAME,
4773	) -> str:
4774	    """BB5-P02: render the cross-document NLI semantic-contradiction block.
4775	
4776	    Semantic records ARE PT08-gated (subject + predicate substring must appear in
4777	    the report), so subject + predicate of EVERY record render inline. Only the
4778	    payload is trimmed: each side becomes a short normalized claim summary
4779	    (bibliographies / figure-markdown / image URLs stripped, trimmed to
4780	    ``quote_trim`` chars). Beyond ``inline_cap`` records, the remainder collapse to
4781	    a one-line pointer to the sidecar — but every subject/predicate is still
4782	    emitted so PT08 holds. Pure: no I/O."""
4783	    if not semantic_records:
4784	        return ""
4785	    if inline_cap is None:
4786	        inline_cap = _env_int(_SEMANTIC_INLINE_CAP_ENV, _SEMANTIC_INLINE_CAP_DEFAULT)
4787	    if quote_trim is None:
4788	        quote_trim = _env_int(_SEMANTIC_QUOTE_TRIM_ENV, _SEMANTIC_QUOTE_TRIM_DEFAULT)
4789	
4790	    out = (
4791	        f"\n## Semantic contradiction disclosures (cross-document NLI)\n"
4792	        f"An NLI pass over same-subject evidence pairs flagged {len(semantic_records)} "
4793	        f"prose-only directional contradiction(s) that carry no shared number and no "
4794	        f"rule-cue (and so are not caught by the numeric or qualitative detectors). "
4795	        f"Each is shown with a short normalized claim summary per side; full source "
4796	        f"text is in the `{sidecar_filename}` sidecar (filter `type=\"semantic\"`).\n\n"
4797	    )
4798	    # PT08 contract: subject + predicate of EVERY record must appear in the report.
4799	    # The first `inline_cap` render with trimmed quotes; the rest render
4800	    # subject/predicate only (still satisfying PT08) on a compact pointer line.
4801	    for idx, r in enumerate(semantic_records):
4802	        subject = getattr(r, "subject", "")
4803	        predicate = getattr(r, "predicate", "")
4804	        if idx < inline_cap:
4805	            claims = getattr(r, "claims", None) or []
4806	
4807	            def _sem_side(cl: dict) -> str:
4808	                # I-deepfix-001 B4-render #22 (#1344): quote the substantive sentence per side, but
4809	                # replace a chrome-only span (copyright/title/masthead) with a neutral pointer so the
4810	                # panel never renders furniture AS a conflicting claim. subject/predicate + ev/tier
4811	                # still render (PT08 + traceability intact); the record stays in the sidecar.
4812	                raw = cl.get("text") or ""
4813	                q = _normalize_claim_summary(raw, quote_trim=quote_trim)
4814	                loc = f"[ev={cl.get('evidence_id', '')}, tier={cl.get('tier', '')}]"
4815	                # #22 iter-2 P1: run the chrome/furniture check on the UNTRIMMED normalized text
4816	                # (quote_trim=0), NOT on ``q``. Our own quote-trim appends a trailing "…" (and can end
4817	                # on a back-off word boundary), which trips the shared detector's TRUNCATION leg — so a
4818	                # real substantive quote longer than the cap was mislabeled "source furniture". Checking
4819	                # the pre-trim text keys the drop on genuine chrome only; a real long quote renders its
4820	                # trimmed "…" form. A genuinely chrome-only side still flags (its raw text IS chrome).
4821	                raw_norm = _normalize_claim_summary(raw, quote_trim=0)
4822	                if (
4823	                    _contradiction_render_honest_enabled()
4824	                    and raw_norm
4825	                    and _contradiction_side_text_is_chrome(raw_norm)
4826	                ):
4827	                    return f"(no substantive quote — source furniture; see sidecar) {loc}"
4828	                return f"\"{q}\" {loc}"
4829	
4830	            summary = " VS ".join(_sem_side(cl) for cl in claims)
4831	            out += (
4832	                f"- [SEMANTIC] {subject} / {predicate} "
4833	                f"(NLI confidence {getattr(r, 'nli_confidence', 0.0):.2f}): {summary}\n"
4834	            )
4835	        else:
4836	            out += (
4837	                f"- [SEMANTIC] {subject} / {predicate} "
4838	                f"(NLI confidence {getattr(r, 'nli_confidence', 0.0):.2f}) — full pair "
4839	                f"in `{sidecar_filename}`.\n"
4840	            )
4841	    return out

4844	# ── I-deepfix-001 B4-render #22 (#1344): honest contradiction render ──────────────────────────────
4845	# The drb_72 §5/§6 contradiction panel rendered a SEMANTIC (prose) contradiction as "0.0 [ev_042]
4846	# VS 0.0 [ev_051]" — a meaningless numeric pair, because a semantic NLI record carries the 0.0 value
4847	# SENTINEL on both sides (it has no shared number) — AND it quoted ev_051's copyright/title CHROME
4848	# ("© 2024 International Monetary Fund WP/24/65 … The Economic Impact") instead of the substantive
4849	# "empirical findings are inconclusive …" sentence, so the reader could not see WHY the two conflict.
4850	# This leg (a) suppresses the numeric "X VS Y" field for a semantic record (quote the prose instead),
4851	# and (b) excludes a chrome-only span from a contradiction side. RENDER-only / faithfulness-neutral:
4852	# it drops NO source (every record stays in the contradictions.json sidecar — §-1.3) and never
4853	# fabricates a conflict the detectors did not find. Default-ON LAW VI kill-switch
4854	# PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST; OFF reverts byte-identically.
4855	def _contradiction_render_honest_enabled() -> bool:
4856	    return os.environ.get("PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST", "1").strip().lower() in (
4857	        "1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled",
4858	    )

4861	def _contradiction_side_text_is_chrome(text: str) -> bool:
4862	    """True iff a contradiction-side quote is page-furniture chrome (copyright footer / bot-challenge
4863	    / masthead / editor-affiliation), so it must NOT be surfaced as one side of a conflict. Reuses
4864	    the B4 #13 ``is_block_page_chrome_sentence`` (copyright-footer rule + shared render-chrome
4865	    detector) — broader than the render-seam predicate alone, which is blind to a bare copyright
4866	    footer. Fail-safe: any import/detector error => False (never over-suppress a real side). PURE."""
4867	    if not text or not str(text).strip():
4868	        return False
4869	    try:
4870	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.block_page_chrome_scrub import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4871	            is_block_page_chrome_sentence,
4872	        )
4873	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover — block_page_chrome_scrub is stable in-tree
4874	        return False
4875	    try:
4876	        return bool(is_block_page_chrome_sentence(str(text)))
4877	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover — detector is pure in-tree

4881	def _contradict_side(claim: dict, *, is_semantic: bool = False) -> str:
4882	    """I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 4: one side of a contradiction — a short value / assertion-status /
4883	    prose summary plus its source ``[ev=…, tier=…]``. PURE. "" when the claim has no usable text.
4884	
4885	    I-deepfix-001 B4-render #22 (#1344): with ``is_semantic=True`` under the honest-render flag, the
4886	    numeric ``value`` (the 0.0 semantic sentinel) is NOT rendered — the substantive quote is used
4887	    instead (no "0.0 VS 0.0"). A chrome-only quote returns "" so the caller drops that side."""
4888	    honest = _contradiction_render_honest_enabled()
4889	    _suppress_numeric = honest and is_semantic
4890	    ev = str(claim.get("evidence_id") or "").strip()
4891	    tier = str(claim.get("source_tier") or claim.get("tier") or "").strip()
4892	    loc = f" [ev={ev}, tier={tier}]" if (ev or tier) else ""
4893	    if claim.get("value") is not None and not _suppress_numeric:
4894	        unit = str(claim.get("unit") or "").strip()
4895	        return f"{claim.get('value')}{unit}{loc}"
4896	    status = str(claim.get("assertion_status") or "").strip()
4897	    if status and not _suppress_numeric:
4898	        return f"{status}{loc}"
4899	    _raw_src = str(claim.get("context_snippet") or claim.get("text") or claim.get("raw_text") or "")
4900	    text = _normalize_claim_summary(_raw_src, quote_trim=120)
4901	    # #22 iter-2 P1: check the UNTRIMMED normalized text (quote_trim=0), NOT the 120-char-trimmed
4902	    # ``text``. The trim appends a trailing "…" that trips the shared detector's TRUNCATION leg, which
4903	    # would mislabel a real substantive quote >120 chars as chrome and drop it — collapsing the caller
4904	    # to its generic "see sidecar" fallback. Keying the drop on the pre-trim text drops only genuine
4905	    # chrome-only sides; a real long quote renders its trimmed "…" form (the record stays in the sidecar).
4906	    if honest and text:
4907	        _raw_norm = _normalize_claim_summary(_raw_src, quote_trim=0)
4908	        if _raw_norm and _contradiction_side_text_is_chrome(_raw_norm):
4909	            return ""  # chrome-only side — drop it from the headline (record stays in the sidecar)
4910	    return f"\"{text}\"{loc}" if text else ""

4913	def _select_opposing_pair(claims: list[dict]) -> "tuple[dict, dict] | None":
4914	    """I-wire-011 (#1325) gate iter-1 P1: pick the two GENUINELY OPPOSING claims from a
4915	    contradiction record's basket — the maximal-disagreement endpoints — instead of the
4916	    positional first two (``claims[:2]``), which can surface the WRONG two sides.
4917	
4918	    The selection key is each claim's finite ``value`` float, which every detector populates:
4919	    a NUMERIC claim carries the real measured value; a QUALITATIVE claim carries the stance
4920	    value (PRESENT 1.0 vs ABSENT 0.0 vs INDETERMINATE 0.5); a SEMANTIC claim carries the 0.0
4921	    sentinel and always arrives as a detector-decided pair of exactly two. The min-value and
4922	    max-value claims are therefore the true opposing poles (the numeric poles, or the
4923	    support-vs-refuter stance poles).
4924	
4925	    Returns ``(low_claim, high_claim)`` when a real value spread exists. For an exactly-2-claim
4926	    record (the detector's OWN pair — e.g. every semantic NLI record, where both values are the
4927	    0.0 sentinel) the two claims are returned as-is, since they are not an arbitrary choice. When
4928	    a record carries >2 claims with no value spread the pair is ambiguous, so ``None`` is returned
4929	    and the caller DISCLOSES the contradiction without fabricating an arbitrary pair.
4930	
4931	    PURE + deterministic. Render-only: it reads the detectors' own values and never asserts a
4932	    conflict the detectors did not find (faithfulness-neutral)."""
4933	    if not claims or len(claims) < 2:
4934	        return None
4935	    valued: list[tuple[float, dict]] = []
4936	    for claim in claims:
4937	        try:
4938	            valued.append((float(claim.get("value")), claim))
4939	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
4940	            continue  # non-numeric/missing value -> not a usable pole; skip
4941	    if len(valued) >= 2:
4942	        low = min(valued, key=lambda t: t[0])
4943	        high = max(valued, key=lambda t: t[0])
4944	        if low[0] != high[0]:  # guard ties: min/max return first-on-equal -> would be A VS A
4945	            return low[1], high[1]
4946	    # No usable value spread. Only an exactly-2-claim record is a NON-arbitrary pair (the
4947	    # detector decided exactly those two conflict — e.g. a semantic NLI pair at 0.0/0.0).
4948	    if len(claims) == 2:
4949	        return claims[0], claims[1]
4950	    return None
4951

4953	def _is_unconfirmed_metric_mismatch(record: "Any") -> bool:
4954	    """I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3b: True iff a numeric ContradictionRecord carries the
4955	    ``[possible_metric_mismatch]`` marker — the B9 same-metric-axes downgrade the detector applies
4956	    when it could NOT positively confirm the two numbers measure the SAME quantity (different
4957	    comparator / population / time-window). Such a record is NOT a settled contradiction.
4958	
4959	    A record returning True is SUPPRESSED from BOTH the report's ``## Contradiction disclosures``
4960	    prose AND the PT08 disclosure gate (``run_external_evaluation``) — kept in lock-step so the run
4961	    never aborts ``abort_evaluator_critical`` over an undisclosed row it deliberately withheld. Every
4962	    claim still lives in the ``contradictions.json`` sidecar (never dropped — §-1.3); only the
4963	    misleading "settled disagreement" framing is withheld. Mirrors the already-merged
4964	    ``_render_contradicts_block`` skip (iter-3a) and the ``qualitative_records_disclosed_for_pt08``
4965	    disclosed-subset pattern. Accepts a record object OR a serialized dict. Pure, never raises."""
4966	    predicate = getattr(record, "predicate", None)
4967	    if predicate is None and isinstance(record, dict):
4968	        predicate = record.get("predicate")
4969	    return POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER in str(predicate or "")

4974	# the disclosures prose vs manifest.contradictions_found=3 vs a Limitations "Two contradictions").

5036	def _render_contradicts_block(contradictions_path: "str | None") -> str:
5037	    """I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 4: a DISTINCT inline ``CONTRADICTS`` both-sides block read from the
5038	    run's ``contradictions.json``. One ``- CONTRADICTS:`` line per record that carries two opposing
5039	    sides: the subject/predicate, a short summary of EACH side with its source ``[ev/tier]``, and
5040	    (numeric records) the relative difference. This is the 'contradiction/both-sides block' the
5041	    per-claim corroboration line points to, so a contested claim is never presented as settled.
5042	
5043	    Reads the ACTUAL detector entries — it NEVER hardcodes and NEVER asserts a conflict the
5044	    detectors did not find (a record with <2 renderable sides is skipped). PURE except the guarded
5045	    read; "" when the sidecar is missing / unreadable / holds no 2-sided record (no empty heading)."""
5046	    if not contradictions_path or not os.path.exists(contradictions_path):
5047	        return ""
5048	    try:
5049	        with open(contradictions_path, encoding="utf-8") as _cf:
5050	            entries = json.load(_cf)
5051	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — unreadable sidecar => nothing to render, never abort
5052	        return ""
5053	    lines: list[str] = []
5054	    for e in entries or []:
5055	        if not isinstance(e, dict):
5056	            continue
5057	        claims = [c for c in (e.get("claims") or []) if isinstance(c, dict)]
5058	        if len(claims) < 2:
5059	            continue
5060	        subject = str(e.get("subject") or "").strip()
5061	        predicate = str(e.get("predicate") or "").strip()
5062	        # I-wire-013 (#1327): a predicate carrying the possible_metric_mismatch marker is the
5063	        # detector declaring it could NOT confirm a shared metric — these numbers may not measure
5064	        # the same quantity (different comparator / population / time-window). Rendering it under a
5065	        # CONTRADICTS both-sides heading would present an UNCONFIRMED mismatch as a settled
5066	        # contradiction (a fabricated disagreement — lethal in clinical context, §-1.1). Skip it
5067	        # from the headline block; every claim stays disclosed in the contradictions.json sidecar
5068	        # (never dropped — §-1.3). Render ONLY confirmed shared-metric disagreements. The existing
5069	        # not_comparable skip below stays.
5070	        if POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER in predicate:
5071	            continue
5072	        head = " / ".join(p for p in (subject, predicate) if p) or "same-subject claims"
5073	        # I-wire-011 (#1325) gate iter-1 P1 (A17 commensurability guard): a not_comparable record
5074	        # is the detector DECLARING there is NO genuine contradiction — its claims compare
5075	        # different quantity kinds (e.g. a 0.5° yaw angle bucketed with a 100 m radar distance),
5076	        # a category error kept OUT of the headline count. Selecting its maximal-value pair would
5077	        # render the single MOST misleading "0.5 VS 100" under a CONTRADICTS heading — exactly the
5078	        # category-error framing the detector rejected (lethal in clinical context, §-1.1). Disclose
5079	        # it as incommensurable instead; never as a contested-vs-settled pair. (Only a numeric
5080	        # ContradictionRecord that POSITIVELY fired A17 carries this key — qualitative/semantic
5081	        # records asdict without the field, so this never false-triggers.)
5082	        if e.get("not_comparable"):
5083	            lines.append(
5084	                f"- INCOMMENSURABLE: {head} — claims compare different quantity kinds, so no "
5085	                f"contradiction is asserted; see the `{_QUAL_SIDECAR_FILENAME}` sidecar."
5086	            )
5087	            continue
5088	        rel = e.get("relative_difference")
5089	        rel_md = (
5090	            f" (relative difference {_format_percent(rel * 100.0)})"
5091	            if isinstance(rel, (int, float)) else ""
5092	        )
5093	        # I-wire-011 (#1325) gate iter-1 P1: render the GENUINELY OPPOSING endpoints (the
5094	        # maximal-disagreement pair), NOT the positional first two — ``claims[:2]`` can surface
5095	        # the wrong two sides on a >2-claim basket. ``_select_opposing_pair`` returns the
5096	        # min-value vs max-value claim (numeric poles / PRESENT-vs-ABSENT stance poles / the
5097	        # 2-claim semantic judge pair). Deterministic + faithfulness-neutral (render-only).
5098	        # I-deepfix-001 B4-render #22 (#1344): a semantic (cross-document NLI) record carries the 0.0
5099	        # value sentinel on both sides — render its prose, never "0.0 VS 0.0".
5100	        _is_semantic = str(e.get("type") or "").strip().lower() == "semantic"
5101	        pair = _select_opposing_pair(claims)
5102	        if pair is not None:
5103	            side_a = _contradict_side(pair[0], is_semantic=_is_semantic)
5104	            side_b = _contradict_side(pair[1], is_semantic=_is_semantic)
5105	            if side_a and side_b:
5106	                lines.append(f"- CONTRADICTS: {head} — {side_a} VS {side_b}{rel_md}")
5107	                continue
5108	        # No genuine opposing pair (ambiguous >2-claim basket, or a chosen pole had no
5109	        # renderable text): DISCLOSE the contradiction the detectors found WITHOUT fabricating an
5110	        # arbitrary [:2] pair, so a contested claim is still never presented as settled.
5111	        lines.append(
5112	            f"- CONTRADICTS: {head} — {len(claims)} same-subject claims disagree; see the "
5113	            f"`{_QUAL_SIDECAR_FILENAME}` sidecar for all sides.{rel_md}"
5114	        )
5115	    if not lines:
5116	        return ""
5117	    return (
5118	        "\n## Contradictions (both sides)\n\n"
5119	        "Same-subject claims the contradiction detectors found to disagree are shown here with BOTH "
5120	        "sides and their sources, so a contested claim is never presented as settled. Full detail "
5121	        f"is in the `{_QUAL_SIDECAR_FILENAME}` sidecar.\n\n"
5122	        + "\n".join(lines)
5123	        + "\n"
5124	    )

5127	# I-wire-011 (#1325) Codex iter-2 P1: a dedicated single-worker pool for the POST-RELEASE NLI
5128	# annotation wall. ``annotate_nli_entailment`` is an ``async def`` that wraps a SYNCHRONOUS, BLOCKING
5129	# ``judge.judge()`` loop with NO await/yield (``nli_benchmark_annotator.py:147-151``); awaiting it
5130	# directly on the event loop runs that blocking loop ON the loop, which never yields, so
5131	# ``asyncio.wait_for`` can NEVER fire its timeout — the I-wire-010 wall was a no-op. We OFFLOAD the
5132	# blocking annotator onto this worker thread and wall the THREAD future, so the loop stays free and the
5133	# wall actually fires. ``max_workers=1`` — the annotation runs once per query as the run's last step
5134	# (one-query-per-VM, so cross-query serialization is a non-issue here).
5135	#
5136	# Exit-safety (I-wire-011 iter-4, Codex iter-3 P1): a truly-wedged annotator must NEVER block process
5137	# exit on ANY entrypoint. The prior offload used a module-level ``concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor``
5138	# whose workers are NON-DAEMON; the only thing that force-exited past a wedged ``concurrent.futures``
5139	# atexit join was the ``PG_TEARDOWN_WALL`` watchdog — and that watchdog is armed ONLY by
5140	# ``run_honest_sweep.main()``. The paid relaunch path reaches this code via the Gate-B / iwire002
5141	# ``run_one_query`` entrypoint (``scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py`` -> ``run_gate_b_query`` ->
5142	# ``asyncio.run``), which did NOT arm it, so a wedged worker could hang the interpreter at exit. FIX:
5143	# offload onto a per-call DAEMON ``threading.Thread`` (built inside ``_nli_annotation_with_wall`` below).
5144	# A daemon thread is killed at interpreter shutdown and is NEVER joined by ``threading._shutdown`` /
5145	# ``concurrent.futures._python_exit``, so a wedged worker can never block exit regardless of entrypoint.
5146	# The I-wire-008 per-call entailment deadline still bounds each ``judge.judge()`` call, and the
5147	# ``PG_TEARDOWN_WALL`` watchdog — now also armed on the Gate-B path (run_gate_b.py __main__) — remains a
5148	# belt-and-suspenders for OTHER non-daemon lingering pools; this NLI worker no longer depends on either
5149	# to be exit-safe.
5150	
5151	
5152	async def _nli_annotation_with_wall(
5153	    pairs: list, wall_s: int, annotate,
5154	) -> "tuple[dict, bool]":
5155	    """I-wire-010 (#1324) / I-wire-011 (#1325): bound the POST-RELEASE advisory NLI annotation with a
5156	    wall so a hung entailment judge cannot wedge the run before manifest.json is written.
5157	
5158	    By the time this runs the BINDING faithfulness gate (4-role D8) has already passed
5159	    (``release_allowed=True``); this NLI pass is ADVISORY benchmark metadata. The entailment judge is a
5160	    network call — if its provider hangs, an unbounded wait blocks forever, an ``except`` CANNOT catch a
5161	    hang, and the downstream manifest.json write never lands: the run wedges (the observed futex
5162	    deadlock).
5163	
5164	    **I-wire-011 fix (Codex iter-2 P1).** ``annotate_nli_entailment`` is an ``async def`` that wraps a
5165	    SYNCHRONOUS, BLOCKING ``judge.judge()`` loop with NO await/yield (``nli_benchmark_annotator.py``
5166	    :147-151). The old ``await asyncio.wait_for(annotate(pairs), ...)`` ran that blocking loop ON the
5167	    event loop, which never yields control, so ``wait_for`` could NEVER fire its timeout — the wall was a
5168	    no-op and the deadlock was NOT fixed. The wall is only effective when the blocking work runs OFF the
5169	    loop: we offload ``annotate(pairs)`` onto a per-call DAEMON worker thread (built below) that publishes
5170	    its outcome on a ``concurrent.futures.Future``, and wall that THREAD future. The coroutine then
5171	    ``await``s the future via ``asyncio.wrap_future`` (a REAL suspension point), the loop stays free, and
5172	    ``asyncio.wait_for`` fires. The worker is a DAEMON thread (NOT a ``ThreadPoolExecutor`` — its workers
5173	    are non-daemon and can wedge the interpreter at exit on the Gate-B entrypoint; see the module note
5174	    above), so a truly-wedged annotator can never block process exit.
5175	
5176	    **Budget / cost.** The worker runs under a SNAPSHOT of the parent task context
5177	    (``contextvars.copy_context()``), so the judge's ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` (per-run budget) and
5178	    ``_CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX`` (ledger session id) are visible. Because the copy is independent, the judge's
5179	    ``_add_run_cost`` increments land in the COPY, so we reconcile the spend DELTA back to the parent in
5180	    ``finally`` (mirrors the proven ``_topic_llm`` offload, ~L9061) — the run-budget gate stays inclusive
5181	    on success AND on a propagated ``BudgetExceededError`` (partial bill). Only ``asyncio.TimeoutError``
5182	    is caught, so ``BudgetExceededError`` (Core Invariant §9.1.6 — a cap breach must abort) and
5183	    ``NliUnavailableError`` PROPAGATE unchanged to the caller's existing handlers.
5184	
5185	    Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: skipping an ADVISORY annotation never relaxes a verdict (the 4-role D8 gate
5186	    already decided release). Returns ``(result_dict, timed_out)``."""
5187	    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
5188	    # Lazy import (cheap — openrouter_client is already loaded): the per-run cost accumulator the judge
5189	    # bills against. copy_context() also snapshots _CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX so the ledger session id carries.
5190	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import _RUN_COST_CTX  # noqa: PLC0415
5191	
5192	    _parent_cost_before = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
5193	    _worker_cost_after: list[float] = [_parent_cost_before]
5194	    _parent_ctx = contextvars.copy_context()
5195	
5196	    async def _annotate_capturing() -> dict:
5197	        try:
5198	            return await annotate(pairs)
5199	        finally:
5200	            # Read INSIDE the copied context so the judge's _add_run_cost() increments are captured
5201	            # (they landed in this copy, not the parent task's context). Runs even when annotate raises
5202	            # BudgetExceededError, so the partial spend is reconciled by the outer finally below.
5203	            _worker_cost_after[0] = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
5204	
5205	    def _worker() -> dict:
5206	        # Drive the async annotator to completion on this worker thread (OFF the event loop) under the
5207	        # parent's copied context. asyncio.run() spins a fresh loop here; the blocking judge.judge() loop
5208	        # now blocks THIS thread, not the caller's event loop — which is what frees the wall to fire.
5209	        return _parent_ctx.run(asyncio.run, _annotate_capturing())
5210	
5211	    # Drive _worker on a per-call DAEMON thread and publish its outcome on a concurrent.futures.Future,
5212	    # then await that future via asyncio.wrap_future — exactly what loop.run_in_executor does internally,
5213	    # minus the non-daemon pool. set_running_or_notify_cancel() is required before set_result/
5214	    # set_exception (Future contract); we mirror the executor's surface-ALL-outcomes semantics so
5215	    # BudgetExceededError / NliUnavailableError propagate unchanged and only asyncio.TimeoutError is
5216	    # caught below. Daemon => a wedged worker can never block process exit (Codex iter-3 P1 fix).
5217	    _result_future: "concurrent.futures.Future" = concurrent.futures.Future()
5218	
5219	    def _runner() -> None:
5220	        if not _result_future.set_running_or_notify_cancel():
5221	            return
5222	        try:
5223	            _result_future.set_result(_worker())
5224	        except BaseException as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - mirror executor: every outcome reaches the awaiter
5225	            _result_future.set_exception(_exc)
5226	
5227	    threading.Thread(target=_runner, name="nli_wall", daemon=True).start()
5228	    try:
5229	        result = await asyncio.wait_for(
5230	            asyncio.wrap_future(_result_future, loop=loop), timeout=wall_s,
5231	        )
5232	        return result, False
5233	    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
5234	        # The wall fired BECAUSE the blocking work is on the worker thread (the loop was free to time
5235	        # out). The abandoned DAEMON worker keeps draining — bounded by the I-wire-008 per-call entailment
5236	        # deadline — and, being a daemon, is killed at interpreter exit without ever blocking it (no
5237	        # atexit join), so control returns here and the caller reaches the manifest write. ADVISORY skip:
5238	        # never relaxes a verdict (4-role D8 already decided release).
5239	        return (
5240	            {
5241	                "nli_status": "skipped_wall_timeout",
5242	                "sentences_checked": 0,
5243	                "advisory": True,
5244	                "wall_timeout_s": wall_s,
5245	            },
5246	            True,
5247	        )
5248	    finally:
5249	        # Reconcile the worker's _RUN_COST_CTX spend back to the parent so PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN stays
5250	        # inclusive (mirrors _topic_llm). On the success / BudgetExceededError paths _annotate_capturing's
5251	        # finally already captured the worker's final cost; on the wall-timeout path the worker has not
5252	        # finished so the delta is 0 here (its later spend is still tracked in the process-global ledger
5253	        # accumulator) — correct, since this is the run's last step before the manifest write.
5254	        _delta = _worker_cost_after[0] - _parent_cost_before
5255	        if _delta:
5256	            _RUN_COST_CTX.set(_RUN_COST_CTX.get() + _delta)

5310	def assemble_report_md(
5311	    title_md: str,
5312	    abstract_md: str,
5313	    body_md: str,
5314	    conclusion_md: str,
5315	    *,
5316	    dedup_enabled: bool,
5317	) -> str:
5318	    """Assemble the final report.md (I-arch-011 PR-d #1268).
5319	
5320	    The Abstract (front) + Conclusion (end) are INTENTIONAL verbatim re-presentations of body
5321	    sentences (a summary repeats the body — that is the point), so they MUST be EXEMPT from
5322	    paragraph-dedup (``dedup_identical_paragraphs`` keeps the FIRST content-identical paragraph and
5323	    would otherwise empty the ``## Conclusion`` / drop the body copy). So when a summary renders, dedup
5324	    the BODY ONLY and sandwich the abstract (after the title) + conclusion (end) around it.
5325	
5326	    When NO summary renders (``abstract_md == conclusion_md == ""``), the EXACT pre-PR-d path is used —
5327	    ``dedup_identical_paragraphs(title_md + body_md)`` over the whole thing — so the default-OFF render
5328	    is BYTE-IDENTICAL (including the pass-2 orphaned-title-header behaviour, where a title immediately
5329	    followed by ``## Key Findings`` is dropped). Pure."""
5330	    if abstract_md or conclusion_md:
5331	        body = dedup_identical_paragraphs(body_md) if dedup_enabled else body_md
5332	        return title_md + abstract_md + body + conclusion_md
5333	    whole = title_md + body_md
5334	    return dedup_identical_paragraphs(whole) if dedup_enabled else whole

5337	def dedup_identical_paragraphs(report_text: str) -> str:
5338	    """BB5-P03: drop later body paragraphs that are content-identical (after
5339	    citation-marker stripping + whitespace/case normalization) to an earlier
5340	    paragraph; remove any header left ORPHANED (a header immediately followed by
5341	    another header or end-of-document) by that drop; then MERGE any repeated header
5342	    so the report never renders two headers with the same text (e.g. two
5343	    '### Limitations'). Keeps the FIRST occurrence in every case.
5344	
5345	    Content-identity ONLY for the body drop — the title-based blanket suppress was
5346	    rejected because the outline 'Limitations' (verified prose) and the appended
5347	    synthesized ``limitations_text`` (corpus-skew / contradiction meta-disclosures) are
5348	    different content in the general case; suppressing by title silently drops the
5349	    latter's unique disclosure (the §-1.1 silent-downgrade the operator flagged).
5350	
5351	    Two real drb_90 shapes:
5352	      1. outline-Implications body == outline-Limitations body (citation NUMBERS
5353	         differ only) → pass 1 drops the duplicate body, pass 2 clears the now-empty
5354	         '### Limitations' header.
5355	      2. a second '### Limitations' carries a DISTINCT synthesized body (corpus-skew
5356	         disclosure) → pass 1/2 leave both, so pass 3 MERGES: the distinct body is
5357	         relocated under the first '### Limitations' and the duplicate header line is
5358	         dropped. The body is KEPT (no silent downgrade) — only the redundant header
5359	         disappears. This fires even when the two headers are non-adjacent (an
5360	         intervening Trial Summary / Analyst Synthesis / quantified block can sit
5361	         between the outline Limitations and the appended one — see lines ~5553-5847);
5362	         in the real drb_90 artifact they are adjacent, so pass 3 is defensive there
5363	         but correct in both layouts.
5364	
5365	    Exact-equality only (no fuzzy match): a paragraph that merely shares phrasing is
5366	    never dropped. Pure."""
5367	    if not report_text:
5368	        return report_text
5369	    blocks = report_text.split("\n\n")
5370	
5371	    # Pass 1 — drop content-identical body paragraphs (keep first). Headers and blank
5372	    # blocks are never deduped here (a header is collapsed only if orphaned in pass 2).
5373	    seen_paragraphs: set = set()
5374	    kept: list[str] = []
5375	    for block in blocks:
5376	        if _is_markdown_header(block) or not block.strip():
5377	            kept.append(block)
5378	            continue
5379	        normalized = _strip_citation_markers(block)
5380	        if normalized and normalized in seen_paragraphs:
5381	            continue
5382	        if normalized:
5383	            seen_paragraphs.add(normalized)
5384	        kept.append(block)
5385	
5386	    # Pass 2 — drop orphaned headers: a header block whose next non-blank block is
5387	    # another header or which has no following body (end-of-document).
5388	    def _next_nonblank_idx(blocks_in: list[str], start: int) -> int:
5389	        j = start + 1
5390	        while j < len(blocks_in) and not blocks_in[j].strip():
5391	            j += 1
5392	        return j
5393	
5394	    after_orphan: list[str] = []
5395	    for i, block in enumerate(kept):
5396	        if _is_markdown_header(block):
5397	            nxt = _next_nonblank_idx(kept, i)
5398	            if nxt >= len(kept) or _is_markdown_header(kept[nxt]):
5399	                continue  # orphaned header — drop
5400	        after_orphan.append(block)
5401	
5402	    # Pass 3 — merge repeated headers (relocate-to-first): when a header's normalized
5403	    # text was already KEPT, append THIS header's body block(s) under the first
5404	    # occurrence and drop the duplicate header line — never render two identical
5405	    # headers. "Record on KEEP, not on orphan-drop": a header dropped as orphaned in
5406	    # pass 2 never reaches here, so the byte-identical drb_90 case (one empty + one
5407	    # bodied Limitations) collapses to exactly one header rather than zero. Runs LAST
5408	    # so the orphan pass has already cleared empty duplicates. A report with no
5409	    # repeated header is returned byte-for-byte unchanged (the round-trip invariant).
5410	    kept_header_keys: set = set()
5411	    out_blocks: list[str] = []
5412	    i = 0
5413	    while i < len(after_orphan):
5414	        block = after_orphan[i]
5415	        if _is_markdown_header(block):
5416	            key = _strip_citation_markers(block)
5417	            if key in kept_header_keys:
5418	                # Duplicate header — relocate its body block(s) under the first
5419	                # occurrence's body, then skip the header line itself.
5420	                body_blocks = _collect_header_body_blocks(after_orphan, i)
5421	                _relocate_blocks_after_header(out_blocks, key, body_blocks)
5422	                i = _next_header_or_end_idx(after_orphan, i)
5423	                continue
5424	            kept_header_keys.add(key)
5425	        out_blocks.append(block)
5426	        i += 1
5427	    return "\n\n".join(out_blocks)
5428	
5429	
5430	def _collect_header_body_blocks(blocks_in: list[str], header_idx: int) -> list[str]:
5431	    """BB5-P03 pass-3 helper: the non-blank body blocks owned by the header at
5432	    ``header_idx`` — i.e. up to (but not including) the next header or end-of-doc.
5433	    Blank separator blocks are dropped (re-inserted on relocation). Pure."""
5434	    body: list[str] = []
5435	    j = header_idx + 1
5436	    while j < len(blocks_in) and not _is_markdown_header(blocks_in[j]):
5437	        if blocks_in[j].strip():
5438	            body.append(blocks_in[j])
5439	        j += 1
5440	    return body
5441	
5442	
5443	def _next_header_or_end_idx(blocks_in: list[str], header_idx: int) -> int:
5444	    """BB5-P03 pass-3 helper: index of the next header after ``header_idx`` (so the
5445	    duplicate header AND its body are skipped together), or ``len`` at end-of-doc."""
5446	    j = header_idx + 1
5447	    while j < len(blocks_in) and not _is_markdown_header(blocks_in[j]):
5448	        j += 1
5449	    return j
5450	
5451	
5452	def _relocate_blocks_after_header(
5453	    out_blocks: list[str], header_key: str, body_blocks: list[str]
5454	) -> None:
5455	    """BB5-P03 pass-3 helper: insert ``body_blocks`` into ``out_blocks`` immediately
5456	    after the LAST body block already filed under the first header whose normalized
5457	    text == ``header_key`` (so the relocated distinct body is merged under that one
5458	    header, in order). Mutates ``out_blocks`` in place. Pure logic (no I/O)."""
5459	    if not body_blocks:
5460	        return
5461	    # Find the first occurrence of the header.
5462	    header_pos = -1
5463	    for idx, block in enumerate(out_blocks):
5464	        if _is_markdown_header(block) and _strip_citation_markers(block) == header_key:
5465	            header_pos = idx
5466	            break
5467	    if header_pos < 0:  # defensive: header not found — append at end
5468	        for body in body_blocks:
5469	            out_blocks.append(body)
5470	        return
5471	    # Walk to the end of that header's existing body (stop at the next header).
5472	    insert_pos = header_pos + 1
5473	    while insert_pos < len(out_blocks) and not _is_markdown_header(out_blocks[insert_pos]):
5474	        insert_pos += 1
5475	    for offset, body in enumerate(body_blocks):
5476	        out_blocks.insert(insert_pos + offset, body)
5477

5482	    classified_sources: list[Any],

5484	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None,
5485	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
5486	    max_ev: int,

5489	    floor-selected base (one corroboration-counted representative per finding), THEN re-run the
5490	    tier-balanced top-``max_ev`` selection — so the no-cap relevance-floor pool is bounded to
5491	    PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN (#1070's cap holds). Returns the capped ``EvidenceSelection`` so callers can
5492	    BOTH read ``.selected_rows`` AND surface the capped object in ``manifest['evidence_selection']``
5493	    (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2-1 — stale telemetry otherwise). Pure-logic (no model); §8.4-safe.
5494	
5495	    Factored into a module helper (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1-1) so EVERY selection path uses it —
5496	    the initial selection AND the saturation gap-round reselect — closing the gap where a planner /
5497	    saturation-expansion run could still send an uncapped base to the generator.
5498	    """
5499	    from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data
5500	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (

5507	    return select_evidence_for_generation(
