5518	def _prune_plan_to_sufficient_sections(research_plan, sufficiency_report):
5519	    """I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988): build a PRUNED `ResearchPlan` whose outline
5520	    contains ONLY the SUFFICIENT sections, so the generator (which fixes its
5521	    output structure to `research_plan.outline`) structurally CANNOT render an
5522	    under-covered section in a `partial_saturation` report.
5523	
5524	    The plan is index-based (`SectionOutlineItem.sub_query_indices` point into
5525	    `plan.sub_queries`), and the whole-plan facet-union invariant requires
5526	    `union(retained sub_query_indices) == range(len(pruned.sub_queries))`. So the
5527	    prune MUST:
5528	      (1) drop the under-covered `SectionOutlineItem`s;
5529	      (2) drop the now-ORPHANED `sub_queries` (mapped by no retained section);
5530	      (3) REMAP the retained sections' `sub_query_indices` to the compacted
5531	          `sub_queries` list, so all indices stay in-range and the union invariant
5532	          holds on the pruned plan.
5533	
5534	    Returns `(pruned_plan, dropped_section_titles)`. `pruned_plan` is None when
5535	    ZERO sections are sufficient (caller aborts `abort_corpus_inadequate`).
5536	    """
5537	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (
5538	        ResearchPlan,
5539	        SectionOutlineItem,
5540	    )
5541	
5542	    sub_queries = list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
5543	    outline = list(getattr(research_plan, "outline", []) or [])
5544	    suff_by_unit = {
5545	        u.unit_id: bool(getattr(u, "sufficient", False))
5546	        for u in getattr(sufficiency_report, "per_unit", []) or []
5547	    }
5548	
5549	    retained: list = []
5550	    dropped_titles: list[str] = []
5551	    for sec_idx, section in enumerate(outline):
5552	        unit_id = f"section_{sec_idx}"
5553	        if suff_by_unit.get(unit_id, False):
5554	            retained.append(section)
5555	        else:
5556	            dropped_titles.append(getattr(section, "title", "") or unit_id)
5557	
5558	    if not retained:
5559	        return None, dropped_titles
5560	
5561	    # (2) collect the sub_query indices any retained section maps; (3) build a
5562	    # compaction map old_idx -> new_idx over the retained-and-in-range indices.
5563	    used_old_indices = sorted({
5564	        idx
5565	        for section in retained
5566	        for idx in (getattr(section, "sub_query_indices", []) or [])
5567	        if 0 <= idx < len(sub_queries)
5568	    })
5569	    remap = {old: new for new, old in enumerate(used_old_indices)}
5570	    pruned_sub_queries = [sub_queries[old] for old in used_old_indices]
5571	
5572	    pruned_outline: list = []
5573	    for section in retained:
5574	        new_indices = [
5575	            remap[idx]
5576	            for idx in (getattr(section, "sub_query_indices", []) or [])
5577	            if idx in remap
5578	        ]
5579	        pruned_outline.append(
5580	            SectionOutlineItem(
5581	                archetype=getattr(section, "archetype", "") or "Background",
5582	                title=getattr(section, "title", "") or "",
5583	                evidence_target=int(getattr(section, "evidence_target", 0) or 0),
5584	                sub_query_indices=new_indices,
5585	            )
5586	        )
5587	
5588	    pruned_plan = ResearchPlan(
5589	        research_question=getattr(research_plan, "research_question", ""),
5590	        frame=getattr(research_plan, "frame", None),
5591	        sub_queries=pruned_sub_queries,
5592	        outline=pruned_outline,
5593	    )
5594	    return pruned_plan, dropped_titles
5595

5604	        if tier:

5606	    return ", ".join(parts) or "per scope template"

5609	def _retrieval_caps_disclosure(retrieval) -> dict:
5610	    """BUG-4 (#1262): surface the fetch/search caps that silently throttle breadth so a
5611	    CAPPED run is VISIBLE + auditable in the manifest, instead of only appearing as
5612	    transient log lines ("pre_filter=1299 fetched=623", "truncating 855->100 academic",
5613	    "academic cap reached"). DISCLOSURE ONLY — this reads counts already on the retrieval
5614	    object plus the ACTIVE env-cap values (LAW VI: the caps stay env-overridable and are NOT
5615	    removed or changed here). §-1.3 DNA: weight/consolidate not filter — making a cap visible
5616	    is the honest disclose-don't-silently-drop move, never a new cap/target/thinner.
5617	
5618	    Faithfulness-safe: pure read-only telemetry. It never touches a span, provenance,
5619	    strict_verify / NLI / 4-role check, or any verdict.
5620	
5621	    Fields:
5622	      - candidates_discovered: the full discovered pool BEFORE the fetch budget bit
5623	        (total_candidates_pre_filter, falling back to candidates_total).
5624	      - fetched: how many of those were actually fetched (the post-cap survivors).
5625	      - dropped_pre_fetch: discovered - fetched - failed (the breadth lost to caps/filters
5626	        BEFORE fetch), clamped to >= 0; this is the silent-throttle magnitude.
5627	      - corpus_truncated: the fail-loud post-fetch loop-deadline truncation flag (#958).
5628	      - drop_reasons: the per-reason pre-fetch loss aggregate (already on the object).
5629	      - search_truncations: a list of the ACTIVE caps that could throttle breadth, each
5630	        naming the cap, its configured value, and (when derivable) whether it BIT this run.
5631	    All getattr with defaults so pre-existing / stub retrieval objects never KeyError."""
5632	    discovered = int(getattr(retrieval, "total_candidates_pre_filter", 0) or 0)
5633	    if discovered <= 0:
5634	        discovered = int(getattr(retrieval, "candidates_total", 0) or 0)
5635	    fetched = int(getattr(retrieval, "candidates_fetched", 0) or 0)
5636	    failed = int(getattr(retrieval, "candidates_failed_fetch", 0) or 0)
5637	    dropped_pre_fetch = max(0, discovered - fetched - failed)
5638	
5639	    # The ACTIVE env-driven caps (LAW VI). Read here for DISCLOSURE only — the retrieval
5640	    # backends own + apply them; this block never enforces or alters a cap.
5641	    # I-deepfix-001 D3 (#1344, Codex P2a): the disclosure default MUST track the retriever's
5642	    # real default — `live_retriever.DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP` (raised 40->200 in the D3 breadth fix).
5643	    # A hardcoded 40 here reported the WRONG cap + WRONG `bit` truncation flag whenever
5644	    # PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP was unset. Reading the imported constant as the fallback means this
5645	    # disclosure can NEVER drift from the retriever's literal default again (it auto-tracks any
5646	    # future change to DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP), while still re-reading the env at call time (LAW VI).
5647	    fetch_cap = _env_int("PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP", DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP)
5648	    serper_total = _env_int("PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY", 0)
5649	    serper_pages = _env_int("PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES", 3)
5650	    search_truncations: list[dict] = [
5651	        {
5652	            "cap": "PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP",
5653	            "value": fetch_cap,
5654	            # The fetch budget BIT when more candidates were discovered than the cap allowed
5655	            # through to fetch (the dominant silent breadth throttle).
5656	            "bit": bool(fetch_cap > 0 and discovered > fetch_cap),
5657	        },
5658	        {
5659	            "cap": "PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES",
5660	            "value": serper_pages,
5661	            # Cannot derive per-run "bit" from the result object alone — disclosed as configured.
5662	            "bit": None,
5663	        },
5664	    ]
5665	    if serper_total > 0:
5666	        search_truncations.append(
5667	            {"cap": "PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY", "value": serper_total, "bit": None}
5668	        )
5669	    return {
5670	        "candidates_discovered": discovered,
5671	        "fetched": fetched,
5672	        "failed": failed,
5673	        "dropped_pre_fetch": dropped_pre_fetch,
5674	        "corpus_truncated": bool(getattr(retrieval, "corpus_truncated", False)),
5675	        "drop_reasons": getattr(retrieval, "drop_reasons", {}) or {},
5676	        "search_truncations": search_truncations,
5677	    }
5678

5681	    """#958 (S2): the SINGLE source of truth for a manifest's "retrieval"

5717	        "fetch_success_rate": getattr(retrieval, "fetch_success_rate", None),
5718	        "parallel_completion_rate": getattr(
5719	            retrieval, "parallel_completion_rate", None,
5720	        ),
5721	        "fetch_workers": getattr(retrieval, "fetch_workers", None),
5722	        "distinct_hosts": getattr(retrieval, "distinct_hosts", None),
5723	        # I-ready-017 Task 2a (#1204): ADDITIVE source-funnel telemetry. These
5724	        # persist counts already computed inside run_live_retrieval so the
5725	        # ~90% pre-fetch source loss is MEASURABLE on a fresh run. Pure
5726	        # read-only mirroring — retrieval behavior is byte-identical.
5727	        #
5728	        # kept_by_offtopic makes the pre-fetch funnel explicit: with pre_filter
5729	        # and candidates_total already emitted above, the
5730	        #   candidates_total -> (off-topic filter) -> kept_by_offtopic -> (cap) -> ...
5731	        # split is now visible. The field already existed on
5732	        # LiveRetrievalResult; it was simply never written to the manifest.
5733	        "kept_by_offtopic": getattr(
5734	            retrieval, "candidates_kept_by_offtopic", 0,
5735	        ),
5736	        # The off-topic filter's kept/rejected/threshold (the dominant ~90%
5737	        # drop). None when the filter is disabled or only seeds are present —
5738	        # honestly absent rather than a faked count.
5739	        "prefetch_offtopic": getattr(retrieval, "prefetch_offtopic", None),
5740	        # I-deepfix-001 D3 (#1344, Codex P1): the B4 relevance-ORDER + fetch-budget
5741	        # telemetry (RelevanceGateResult.to_dict()), mirrored into the DURABLE manifest
5742	        # exactly like prefetch_offtopic above. WHY THIS IS REQUIRED, not nice-to-have:
5743	        # the §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP proof lives in `relevance_gate.demoted_fetched_to_fill`
5744	        # (below-floor candidates the fetch BUDGET reached + FETCHED — proof the floor is
5745	        # no longer a pre-fetch hard cut). In the fetched-to-fill SUCCESS case BOTH tail
5746	        # drop_reasons (`relevance_budget_tail`, `relevance_below_floor_tail`) are 0, so the
5747	        # demote proof would be DROPPED from the on-disk manifest if it were only carried on
5748	        # the in-memory result. None when the B4 gate is OFF / only seeds present — honestly
5749	        # absent, never a faked count. DISCLOSURE ONLY (read-only getattr, faithfulness-neutral).
5750	        "relevance_gate": getattr(retrieval, "relevance_gate", None),
5751	        # Per-reason drop aggregate (offtopic / rerank_not_selected /
5752	        # fetch_failed / content_starved) so each stage's loss is attributable.
5753	        "drop_reasons": getattr(retrieval, "drop_reasons", {}),
5754	        # The fetched -> finding-row extraction stage (the 2nd-biggest drop,
5755	        # previously uncounted). finding_rows is the number of evidence rows
5756	        # EXTRACTED from fetched content at retrieval RETURN time. Codex diff-gate
5757	        # iter-1 P1: read the FROZEN `extraction_finding_rows` snapshot, NOT
5758	        # len(retrieval.evidence_rows) — run_one_query mutates evidence_rows after
5759	        # run_live_retrieval returns (expansion/deepener/agentic lanes), so a

5761	        # extraction yield this key names. finding_dedup.raw_row_count is a
5762	        # separate POST-selection dedup-gated count (absent when dedup is OFF).
5763	        "extraction_yield": {
5764	            "fetched": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_fetched", 0),
5765	            "finding_rows": getattr(retrieval, "extraction_finding_rows", 0),
5766	            # I-perm-011 (#1205): the frozen `finding_rows` above is a MAIN-LANE
5767	            # snapshot (counted at run_live_retrieval return, BEFORE the additive
5768	            # agentic/STORM merges), so it is neither the true pre-select pool nor
5769	            # the final generator count. `total_extracted_rows` is the TRUE
5770	            # post-merge pre-selection pool (`len(retrieval.evidence_rows)` after
5771	            # all lanes). Added only when PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR is on so a
5772	            # flag-OFF / slate-absent run keeps a byte-identical `extraction_yield`
5773	            # dict (the existing exact-shape telemetry tests stay green).
5774	            **(
5775	                {"total_extracted_rows": len(
5776	                    getattr(retrieval, "evidence_rows", []) or []
5777	                )}
5778	                if _env_flag("PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR", default=False)
5779	                else {}
5780	            ),
5781	        },
5782	    }

5785	def _base_manifest_envelope(
5786	    *,
5787	    run_id: str,
5788	    q: dict,
5789	    retrieval=None,
5790	    run_cost: float = 0.0,
5791	) -> dict:
5792	    """BUG-SCHEMA-R8d fix: every manifest (success AND abort) carries
5793	    the same envelope so downstream consumers get consistent keys
5794	    regardless of exit path.
5795	
5796	    Caller adds status-specific fields on top (adequacy, generator,
5797	    evaluator_gate, scope, error, etc.). Using this helper prevents
5798	    the envelope from drifting between exit paths.
5799	    """

5849	def filter_verified_sections(sections) -> list:
5850	    """Codex round 1 B-3: the single-source-of-truth predicate for
5851	    "did this section survive Phase-4 strict_verify?". A section
5852	    qualifies only if it was NOT dropped AND has non-empty verified_text
5853	    AND is not a gap-stub.
5854	
5855	    I-gen-006 (#1178) BB5-C07: a section that produced ZERO verified sentences is a
5856	    gap DISCLOSURE, not verified prose — whether it is the new legacy gap stub
5857	    (``is_gap_stub=True``) or a V30 contract slot's gap disclosure (``is_gap_stub``
5858	    stays False but ``sentences_verified == 0``). Both render for display with
5859	    ``dropped_due_to_failure=False`` + non-empty ``verified_text`` (the disclosure
5860	    text), so the UNIVERSAL survivor signal is ``sentences_verified > 0``. Excluding
5861	    every 0-verified gap here ensures a report whose every section is a gap
5862	    disclosure correctly hits ``abort_no_verified_sections`` instead of shipping as
5863	    success (Codex iter-3 P1: is_gap_stub alone missed the V30 gap class).
5864	    """
5865	    return [
5866	        sr for sr in sections
5867	        if not getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", True)
5868	        and getattr(sr, "verified_text", "")
5869	        and getattr(sr, "sentences_verified", 1) > 0
5870	        and not getattr(sr, "is_gap_stub", False)
5871	    ]

5875	# default was fail-open — a mostly-gap-stubbed clinical report shipped green unless
5876	# an operator opted in). The governed default requires a MAJORITY of the attempted
5877	# sections to produce verified prose: a report whose body is more gap disclosures
5878	# than verified findings must NOT ship as success. Enforced WITHOUT any env opt-in;
5879	# the env var only lets an operator move the floor (with sign-off, per the verdict
5880	# artifact's "Suggested next steps"). A malformed value falls back to the STRICT
5881	# default (never to 0.0 / fail-open). It STRENGTHENS the success gate only — it never
5882	# relaxes any faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / D8 are upstream).
5883	DEFAULT_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION = 0.5

5886	def min_verified_section_fraction() -> float:
5887	    """F03 (A3): the success floor on the fraction of attempted sections that
5888	    must produce verified prose. ``PG_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION`` is a FLOAT
5889	    in [0, 1]; default ``DEFAULT_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION`` (0.5) ⇒ the floor
5890	    is ENFORCED BY DEFAULT (a mostly-gap-stubbed report aborts with no env opt-in).
5891	    A malformed value falls back to the STRICT default (NOT 0.0 — never fail-open to
5892	    shipping a gap-stubbed report) and is logged. An EXPLICIT value of 0 or below
5893	    (e.g. ``0.0`` or a negative) disables the floor — a deliberate operator override,
5894	    never the default."""
5895	    raw = os.getenv("PG_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION")
5896	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
5897	        return DEFAULT_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION
5898	    try:
5899	        value = float(raw)
5900	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
5901	        logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
5902	            "PG_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION=%r is not a float; using the strict "
5903	            "default %.2f (floor stays ENFORCED, never fail-open)",
5904	            raw, DEFAULT_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION,
5905	        )
5906	        return DEFAULT_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION
5907	    # Clamp to [0, 1]; a value > 1 would abort EVERY run (no report can exceed
5908	    # 100% verified sections), which is a misconfiguration, not a policy. A negative
5909	    # value <= 0 (0.0 or negative) is the only way to explicitly DISABLE the floor
5910	    # (is_excessive_gap treats min_fraction <= 0 as inert) — a deliberate override.
5911	    if value <= 0.0:
5912	        logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
5913	            "PG_MIN_VERIFIED_SECTION_FRACTION=%r <= 0 explicitly DISABLES the F03 "
5914	            "clinical-safety floor (operator override).", raw,
5915	        )
5916	        return 0.0
5917	    if value > 1.0:
5918	        return 1.0
5919	    return value

5922	def is_excessive_gap(
5923	    verified_count: int, total_sections: int, min_fraction: float,
5924	) -> bool:
5925	    """F03 (A3): pure predicate — does this report fall BELOW the verified-section
5926	    floor? Mirrors ``filter_verified_sections`` so the policy is unit-testable
5927	    without running the giant ``run_one_query``.
5928	
5929	    ``verified_count`` = ``len(filter_verified_sections(sections))`` (sections
5930	    that produced verified prose); ``total_sections`` = ``len(sections)`` (every
5931	    section the generator ATTEMPTED, including gap stubs). Returns True only when
5932	    the floor is ACTIVE (``min_fraction > 0``), at least one section was attempted,
5933	    AND ``verified_count / total_sections < min_fraction``. The zero-verified case
5934	    (``verified_count == 0``) is handled by the existing ``abort_no_verified_sections``
5935	    gate, so this predicate covers the "mostly-gap-stubbed but not totally empty"
5936	    band that previously shipped as success."""
5937	    if min_fraction <= 0.0 or total_sections <= 0:
5938	        return False
5939	    return (verified_count / total_sections) < min_fraction

6113	    run body produces PERMANENT silence — a `finally` cannot fire on a hang — so the
6114	    call site wraps the whole `run_one_query` in `asyncio.wait_for(..., this)` and
6115	    the B11 finalizer emits a labeled timeout artifact on TimeoutError.

6118	    or unparseable value falls back to the generous default (never disables the guard
6119	    silently — a disabled wall-clock would re-open the permanent-silence class)."""
6120	    raw = os.environ.get(_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_ENV, "").strip()

6168	async def _periodic_heartbeat_ticker(hb, stage: str, interval_s: float) -> None:
6169	    """BUG-3 (#1262): re-stamp the heartbeat ``stage`` every ``interval_s`` until cancelled.
6170	
6171	    A background ``asyncio.Task`` the caller starts around a long single ``await`` (generation)
6172	    and cancels in a ``finally``. Each tick calls ``hb(stage)`` so run_status.json's
6173	    ``last_update_utc`` / ``elapsed_s`` / ``running_cost_usd`` keep advancing — the frozen-stage
6174	    signal BUG-3 fixes (``hb`` recomputes elapsed + live cost on every write). The ``hb`` closure
6175	    already swallows every IO error, so a write failure cannot perturb the run; ``CancelledError``
6176	    is re-raised so the task ends cleanly. Faithfulness-safe: pure observability — no gate, span,
6177	    or verdict is read or written here."""
6178	    if interval_s <= 0:
6179	        return
6180	    try:
6181	        while True:
6182	            await asyncio.sleep(interval_s)
6183	            hb(stage)
6184	    except asyncio.CancelledError:
6185	        raise

6188	# B20 P1-1 (Codex iter-2): the run-scoped wall-clock DEADLINE (a monotonic-clock instant).
6189	# The call site (main_async) sets this to ``time.monotonic() + wall`` immediately BEFORE wrapping
6190	# run_one_query in ``asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=wall)``. When wait_for times out it CANCELS
6191	# run_one_query, so run_one_query's OWN inner `finally` (the B11 finalizer call) runs DURING the
6192	# CancelledError unwind — BEFORE control returns to the call-site `except TimeoutError` handler.
6193	# Without a signal, that inner finalizer would write a *degraded* report.md (status="started"),
6194	# and the call-site timeout finalizer would then NO-OP on the now-present report.md -> the run is
6195	# mislabeled `degraded` instead of `timeout`. ``run_wall_clock_cancellation_active()`` lets the
6196	# inner finally DETECT the wall-clock cancellation and pass ``timed_out=True``, so a HUNG run that
6197	# never produced a real report.md gets a deterministically TIMEOUT-labeled artifact. A
6198	# ContextVar is per-Task isolated (the v6 Dramatiq worker runs sibling runs in the same process),
6199	# so one run's deadline can never leak into another's finalizer decision.
6200	_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_DEADLINE_CTX: "contextvars.ContextVar[float | None]" = contextvars.ContextVar(
6201	    "pg_run_wall_clock_deadline", default=None
6202	)

6216	# this only stops the per-lane CLOCK RESET; it caps no breadth and drops no source (the
6217	# existing wall hands off the partial corpus with disclosure), and touches no faithfulness
6218	# gate.
6219	_QUESTION_RETRIEVAL_WALL_ENV = "PG_RETRIEVAL_QUESTION_WALL_SECONDS"

6222	def _per_question_retrieval_deadline() -> "float | None":
6223	    """Absolute ``time.monotonic()`` instant bounding the WHOLE per-question retrieval
6224	    phase, or ``None`` when ``PG_RETRIEVAL_QUESTION_WALL_SECONDS`` is unset / garbage /
6225	    non-positive (=> the proven per-invocation default; byte-identical). Anchored ONCE
6226	    per question by ``run_one_query`` and SHARED across all retrieval lanes so the wall
6227	    cannot be reset per lane."""
6228	    raw = os.getenv(_QUESTION_RETRIEVAL_WALL_ENV, "").strip()
6229	    if not raw:
6230	        return None
6231	    try:
6232	        seconds = float(raw)
6233	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
6234	        return None
6235	    # Mirror live_retriever._env_float: a non-finite (inf/nan) or non-positive wall is
6236	    # rejected (a broken deadline is worse than the per-invocation default).
6237	    import math as _math  # local: keep module import-time cost unchanged
6238	    if not _math.isfinite(seconds) or seconds <= 0:
6239	        return None
6240	    return time.monotonic() + seconds

6243	def _question_retrieval_deadline_passed(deadline: "float | None") -> bool:
6244	    """I-deepfix-001 BUG-A (#1344): True iff the SHARED per-question retrieval wall
6245	    (the instant anchored ONCE by ``_per_question_retrieval_deadline``) has passed.
6246	
6247	    Pure monotonic comparison against the ALREADY-anchored deadline — it does NOT
6248	    re-read the env (that would re-anchor the wall per call and defeat the shared
6249	    deadline). Mirrors ``live_retriever``'s inner search-fan-out guard
6250	    (``time.monotonic() > _retrieval_deadline`` at 4353/5075) — STRICT ``>`` so the
6251	    boundary instant itself is not yet 'passed'.
6252	
6253	    ``deadline is None`` (``PG_RETRIEVAL_QUESTION_WALL_SECONDS`` unset / garbage /
6254	    non-positive — the DEFAULT) => always ``False`` => the outer loops are unbounded
6255	    exactly as before (byte-identical). When set, the CRAG corrective ``while`` and
6256	    each additive retrieval lane consult this so, once the wall passes, retrieval
6257	    STOPS ADDING rounds and the corpus gathered so far HANDS OFF to generation —
6258	    rather than the run-level ``asyncio.wait_for`` wall guillotining the whole
6259	    question with no report. §-1.3: stops adding query rounds; drops NO gathered
6260	    source; touches no faithfulness gate."""
6261	    return deadline is not None and time.monotonic() > deadline

6264	def run_wall_clock_cancellation_active() -> bool:
6265	    """B20 P1-1: True iff the CURRENT asyncio task is being cancelled AND the run-scoped
6266	    wall-clock deadline has elapsed — i.e. this CancelledError unwind is the B20 wall-clock
6267	    timeout (not a user cancel, which returns normally without asyncio-cancelling the task).
6268	
6269	    Two ANDed signals make the classification provably specific (so the inner finalizer can
6270	    never mislabel a non-timeout exit as a timeout):
6271	      1. ``asyncio.current_task().cancelling() > 0`` — a cancellation request is in flight
6272	         (3.11+; wait_for calls ``task.cancel()`` on timeout). The ONLY asyncio-cancel of
6273	         run_one_query is the call-site wait_for (``_abort_if_cancelled`` returns a bool; it
6274	         never raises/cancels), so this alone is already specific — the deadline AND is belt-
6275	         and-suspenders, not a crutch.
6276	      2. ``time.monotonic() >= deadline`` — the run-scoped wall-clock instant has passed.
6277	    Defensive: any failure (no running loop, no current task, 3.10 without ``cancelling``)
6278	    returns False — the finalizer then falls back to its normal status-driven body, which is
6279	    still NEVER silent (it just may carry the degraded label, the pre-fix behavior — fail safe)."""
6280	    try:
6281	        deadline = _RUN_WALL_CLOCK_DEADLINE_CTX.get()
6282	        if deadline is None or time.monotonic() < deadline:
6283	            return False
6284	        import asyncio as _asyncio  # local: keep module import-time cost unchanged
6285	        task = _asyncio.current_task()
6286	        if task is None:
6287	            return False
6288	        cancelling = getattr(task, "cancelling", None)
6289	        if cancelling is None:  # pragma: no cover — 3.13 has it; guard for <3.11
6290	            return False
6291	        return cancelling() > 0
6292	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a finalizer-helper must never raise into a `finally`
6293	        return False

6326	    returns "" for None / a non-dict / an empty dict, so a base run is byte-identical (no prepend).
6327

6329	    NEVER spliced into ``final_report`` (the text the evaluator/PT11 + judge read), so its integer
6330	    counts can never be mistaken for uncited numeric CLAIMS and trip an evaluator gate. It is a
6331	    reader-facing disclosure SIGNAL (corroboration strength), not an asserted finding."""
6332	    if not isinstance(header, dict) or not header:

6344	    #       header — header["claims_*"] is the credibility_analysis basket accounting);

6543	def _insert_banner_after_h1(report_md: str, banner: str) -> str:
6544	    """FIX 4 (U6): insert the D8-unadjudicated ``banner`` AFTER the report's H1 title block so the
6545	    report opens on the ``# `` title, not on a disclosure blockquote. The banner STRING is placed
6546	    VERBATIM (byte-identical — no builder edit). "After the H1 title block" = immediately after the
6547	    first line that starts with ``# `` and its trailing blank line(s). Falls back to a PREPEND when
6548	    no H1 is present (the banner must still ship — a disclosure is never silently dropped). PURE."""
6549	    if not report_md:
6550	        return banner + report_md
6551	    lines = report_md.split("\n")
6552	    h1_idx = None
6553	    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
6554	        if ln.startswith("# ") and not ln.startswith("## "):
6555	            h1_idx = i
6556	            break
6557	    if h1_idx is None:
6558	        return banner + report_md  # no H1 => fail-safe prepend (banner still ships)
6559	    # Advance past the H1 line and any immediately-following blank lines so the banner sits in the
6560	    # gap between the title and the first body block.
6561	    insert_at = h1_idx + 1
6562	    while insert_at < len(lines) and lines[insert_at].strip() == "":
6563	        insert_at += 1
6564	    head = "\n".join(lines[:insert_at])
6565	    tail = "\n".join(lines[insert_at:])
6566	    # Keep a blank line between the title block and the banner, and preserve the banner's own bytes.
6567	    return head + "\n\n" + banner + tail

6709	def artifact_kind_for_status(status: "str | None", *, timed_out: bool = False) -> str:
6710	    """B11: choose the human-artifact KIND for a terminal status. ``timed_out`` (B20)

6712	    degraded body (loud, never silent). PURE — no faithfulness decision."""
6713	    if timed_out:

6715	    if not status:

6718	    unified = status if status in _STATUS_ARTIFACT_KIND else to_unified_status(status)
6719	    return _STATUS_ARTIFACT_KIND.get(
6720	        unified, _STATUS_ARTIFACT_KIND.get(status, _ARTIFACT_KIND_DEGRADED)
6721	    )

6724	def build_finalizer_artifact_body(
6725	    *,
6726	    research_question: str,

6728	    error: "str | None",

6730	    wall_clock_seconds: "float | None" = None,
6731	) -> str:

6733	    writing a report.md (a pre-success abort, an exception, or a HANG). PURE function so
6734	    a behavior test can call it without driving the pipeline.
6735	
6736	    This is the artifact the §-1.1 forensic audit reads when no findings report exists —
6737	    it must be self-documenting (what happened, why, the terminal status), never empty.
6738	    It carries NO claims and asserts NO findings, so it can never relax faithfulness."""

6744	        "## Pipeline verdict",

6747	        f"**Terminal status:** `{status or 'unknown'}`",
6748	    ]
6749	    if timed_out and wall_clock_seconds is not None:
6750	        lines.append(
6751	            f"**Wall-clock:** the run exceeded the {wall_clock_seconds:.0f}s "

6753	            "A hang produces no findings; this artifact is emitted so the run is "
6754	            "never silent."
6755	        )
6756	    lines.append("")
6757	    lines.append(f"### {heading}")
6758	    lines.append("")
6759	    if kind == _ARTIFACT_KIND_TIMEOUT:
6760	        lines.append(
6761	            "The pipeline did not complete within the run-level wall-clock. No "
6762	            "findings report was produced. This is a degraded-run disclosure, not a "
6763	            "findings report — no claim is asserted and none is silently dropped."
6764	        )
6765	    elif kind == _ARTIFACT_KIND_REFUSAL:
6766	        lines.append(

6784	        )
6785	    else:  # degraded

6793	        lines.append("### Detail")
6794	        lines.append("")

6796	    lines.append("")

6800	    lines.append("- Re-run after addressing the disclosed cause; widen retrieval or "

6813	def recover_seam_partial_verdicts(
6814	    run_dir: "Path | None", total_claims: int
6815	) -> "tuple[dict[str, str], float, int]":
6816	    """I-wire-007 (#1321): RECOVER the partial 4-role verdicts settled before the outer seam wall fired.
6817	
6818	    The outer seam WALL (`run_honest_sweep_r3` ~12186) orphans the evaluation worker on a timeout, so
6819	    the in-memory `final_verdicts` are unreachable and the old handler discarded everything
6820	    (`final_verdicts={}`). The compute drain persists each SETTLED claim's final verdict to the
6821	    `four_role_settled_verdicts.jsonl` sidecar (sweep_integration), so on a seam timeout we read it back
6822	    here and return the partials already computed.
6823	
6824	    Returns `(final_verdicts, coverage_fraction, settled_count)` where:
6825	      * `final_verdicts` maps claim_id -> the UNCHANGED `_compose_final_verdict` verdict for every
6826	        SETTLED claim (the unsettled remainder is ABSENT -> stays UNSUPPORTED/held downstream).
6827	      * `coverage_fraction` is the CONSERVATIVE fail-closed proxy `verified_settled / total_claims`:
6828	        the denominator is ALL claims (not just the settled ones), so the unsettled remainder DRAGS
6829	        coverage DOWN — a partial set can therefore only route to released_with_disclosed_gaps / held,
6830	        never a false full-certify. (Distinct covered-element ids per VERIFIED claim are unioned so a
6831	        duplicated id is not double-counted; the proxy is still bounded by claim count.)
6832	      * `settled_count` is how many claims settled before the wall (for the log / disclosed gap).
6833	
6834	    FAIL-CLOSED + FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: this RECOVERS already-computed verdicts; it never SYNTHESIZES
6835	    one, never marks an unsettled claim VERIFIED, and never relaxes a threshold. On ANY read error it
6836	    returns `({}, 0.0, 0)` — byte-equivalent to the prior discard-everything behaviour (no worse).
6837	    """
6838	    empty: "tuple[dict[str, str], float, int]" = ({}, 0.0, 0)
6839	    if run_dir is None or total_claims <= 0:
6840	        return empty
6841	    try:
6842	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.sweep_integration import (  # noqa: PLC0415
6843	            FOUR_ROLE_SETTLED_VERDICTS_FILENAME,
6844	        )
6845	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — resolver hiccup -> behave as the discard path.
6846	        return empty
6847	    sidecar = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_SETTLED_VERDICTS_FILENAME
6848	    if not sidecar.is_file():
6849	        return empty
6850	    final_verdicts: "dict[str, str]" = {}
6851	    verified_covered: set[str] = set()
6852	    try:
6853	        for line in sidecar.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
6854	            line = line.strip()
6855	            if not line:
6856	                continue
6857	            try:
6858	                row = json.loads(line)
6859	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
6860	                continue  # a torn final line (crash mid-append) — skip it, fail-closed.
6861	            claim_id = row.get("claim_id")
6862	            verdict = row.get("verdict")
6863	            if not claim_id or not verdict:
6864	                continue
6865	            # LAST write wins for a duplicated claim_id (e.g. the dedup fan-out) — harmless: the
6866	            # verdict is identical for byte-identical claims by construction.
6867	            final_verdicts[str(claim_id)] = str(verdict)
6868	            if str(verdict) == "VERIFIED":
6869	                for eid in (row.get("covered_element_ids") or []):
6870	                    if eid:
6871	                        verified_covered.add(str(eid))
6872	    except OSError:
6873	        return empty
6874	    if not final_verdicts:
6875	        return empty
6876	    # CONSERVATIVE coverage proxy: VERIFIED-covered distinct ids over ALL claims (so the unsettled
6877	    # remainder lowers it). Clamp to [0, 1]; if a claim carries multiple covered ids the union can
6878	    # exceed total_claims, so cap at 1.0 — but the SEAM_GAP_UNADJUDICATED disclosed gap (injected by
6879	    # build_seam_release_outcome) still forces a non-success disposition regardless of this number.
6880	    coverage_fraction = min(1.0, len(verified_covered) / float(total_claims))
6881	    return final_verdicts, coverage_fraction, len(final_verdicts)

6884	def _seam_cited_evidence_ids(sections: "list[Any]") -> set[str]:
6885	    """Collect every cited evidence_id from the SHIPPED (strict_verify-kept) sentences.
6886	
6887	    These are the citation IDENTITIES the un-run judge would have screened. Defensive: tolerates
6888	    missing kept_sentences / tokens (legacy sections, empty sections) -> contributes nothing."""
6889	    cited: set[str] = set()
6890	    for sr in sections or []:
6891	        for sv in (getattr(sr, "kept_sentences_pre_resolve", None) or []):
6892	            for tok in (getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []):
6893	                _eid = getattr(tok, "evidence_id", None)
6894	                if _eid:
6895	                    cited.add(str(_eid))
6896	    return cited
